struct.doc: Descriptor structure used for initialization of a VectorField.

field.name: CountX
field.doc: Number of entries in the vector field along the X axis.

field.name: CountY
field.doc: Number of entries in the vector field along the Y axis.

field.name: CountZ
field.doc: Number of entries in the vector field along the Z axis.

field.name: Bounds
field.doc: Spatial bounds of the vector field.

enum.doc: Type of mesh indices used, used for determining maximum number of vertices in a mesh.

enumentry.doc: 16-bit indices.
32-bit indices.

enum.doc: Values that represent in which order are euler angles applied when used in transformations.

struct.doc: Represents a quaternion used for 3D rotations.
Represents a quaternion used for 3D rotations.
Controls how are particle positions on a shape chosen.

field.name: Type
field.doc: Type that determines general behaviour.

field.name: Speed
field.doc: Speed along which particle generation should move around the shape, relevant for Loop and PingPing emission modes.

field.name: Interval
field.doc: Determines the minimum interval allowed between the generated particles. 0 specifies the particles can be generated anywhere on the shape.

enum.doc: Names of individual components of an area.

struct.doc: Encapsulates width/height/depth in a single structure.
Encapsulates width/height/depth in a single structure.

class.doc: Allows you to specify an environment map to use for sampling radiance of the sky.

property.name: Texture
property.doc: Determines an environment map to use for sampling skybox radiance. Must be a cube-map texture, and should ideally contain HDR data.

property.name: Brightness
property.doc: Brightness multiplier that will be applied to skybox values before they're being used. Allows you to make the skybox more or less bright. Equal to one by default.

class.doc: Changes the color of the particles over the particle lifetime.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle color evolver.
Creates a new particle color evolver.

property.name: Settings
property.doc: Options describing the evolver.

enum.doc: The "positive side" of the plane is the half space to which the plane normal points. The "negative side" is the other half space. The flag "no side" indicates the plane itself.
Represents verbosity level at which a specific log message will be displayed.

enumentry.doc: Same as Verbose, but for even spammier messages.
Same as Info, but the message will only be logged to the log file and not any console output.
An error happened that will not result in an immediate crash but may cause serious problems.
Fatal error happened that application cannot recover from and will crash.
Something went wrong but the application will not crash, although invalid behaviour might be observed.
Meta-type encompassing all verbosity types. Should not be used for logging directly.
Messages that can provide additional information and warnings, but are too spammy to be displayed under normal circumstances.
An informational message will be logged, can be used for debugging and tracing.

class.doc: Contains information about bones required for skeletal animation.

method.doc: Returns information about a bone at the provided index.

param.name: boneIdx
param.doc: Index of the bone to retrieve information for.

returns.doc: Information about the bone at the specified index.

property.name: NumBones
property.doc: Returns the total number of bones in the skeleton.

class.doc: A buffer describing a volume (3D), image (2D) or line (1D) of pixels in memory. Pixels are stored as a succession of "depth" slices, each containing "height" rows of "width" pixels.

method.doc: Returns a pixel at the specified location in the buffer.

param.name: x
param.doc: X coordinate of the pixel.

param.name: y
param.doc: Y coordinate of the pixel.

param.name: z
param.doc: Z coordinate of the pixel.

returns.doc: Value of the pixel, or undefined value if coordinates are out of range.

method.doc: Sets a pixel at the specified location in the buffer.

param.name: value
param.doc: Color of the pixel to set.

param.name: x
param.doc: X coordinate of the pixel.

param.name: y
param.doc: Y coordinate of the pixel.

param.name: z
param.doc: Z coordinate of the pixel.

method.doc: Returns values of all pixels.

returns.doc: All pixels in the buffer ordered consecutively. Pixels are stored as a succession of "depth" slices, each containing "height" rows of "width" pixels.

method.doc: Sets all pixels in the buffer.Caller must ensure that number of pixels match the extends of the buffer.

param.name: value
param.doc: All pixels in the buffer ordered consecutively. Pixels are stored as a succession of "depth" slices, each containing "height" rows of "width" pixels.

method.doc: Returns all pixels in the buffer as raw bytes.

returns.doc: Raw pixel bytes. It is up to the caller to interpret the pixel format and account for potential row and slice pitch values.

method.doc: Sets all pixels in the buffer as raw bytes.

param.name: value
param.doc: Raw pixel bytes. It is up to the caller to interpret the pixel format and account for potential row and slice pitch values.

property.name: RawRowPitch
property.doc: Returns the number of bytes that offsets one row from another. This can be exact number of bytes required to hold "width" pixel, but doesn't have to be as some buffers require padding.

property.name: RawSlicePitch
property.doc: Returns the number of bytes that offsets one depth slice from another. This can be exact number of bytes required to hold "width * height" pixels, but doesn't have to be as some buffers require padding.

property.name: Format
property.doc: Returns the pixel format used by the internal buffer for storing the pixels.

property.name: Extents
property.doc: Returns extents of the pixel volume this object is capable of holding.

property.name: RawIsConsecutive
property.doc: Return whether this buffer is laid out consecutive in memory (meaning the pitches are equal to the dimensions).

property.name: RawSize
property.doc: Return the size (in bytes) of the buffer this image requires.

struct.doc: A single log entry, containing a message and a channel the message was recorded on.

field.name: LocalTime
field.doc: Local time of message

enum.doc: Describes operation that will be used for rendering a certain set of vertices.

enumentry.doc: Each vertex represents a point.
Each vertex (except the first) forms a line with the previous vertex.
Each sequential pair of vertices represent a line.
Each vertex (except the first two) form a triangle with the previous two vertices.
Each sequential 3-tuple of vertices represent a triangle.
Each vertex (except the first two) form a triangle with the first vertex and previous vertex.

class.doc: Utility class providing various debug functionality.

method.doc: Logs a new message.

param.name: message
param.doc: The message describing the log entry.

param.name: verbosity
param.doc: Verbosity of the message, determining its importance.

param.name: categoryName
param.doc: Category of the message, determining which system is it relevant to.

method.doc: Removes all log entries for a specific category and/or verbosity level.

param.name: categoryName
param.doc: Name of the category to clear. Specify null to clear all categories.

param.name: verbosity
param.doc: Verbosity level to clear.

property.name: LogEntries
property.doc: Returns all existing log entries.

event.doc: Triggered when a new entry in the log is added.
Triggered whenever one or multiple log entries were added or removed. Triggers only once per frame.

class.doc: Represents a GUI element that can be rendered (i.e. has a visual representation). Renderable element can have a particular style, and provides one or multiple render elements to be drawn.

property.name: StyleSheetClass
property.doc: Sets new style class to be used by the element.

property.name: Tint
property.doc: Sets the tint of the GUI element.

struct.doc: Color represented as 4 components, each being a floating point value ranging from 0 to 1. Color components are red, green, blue and alpha.
A three dimensional vector.
A three dimensional vector.
Kerning pair representing larger or smaller offset between a specific pair of characters.

enum.doc: Filter to use when generating mip maps.

struct.doc: Contains information about a single shader parameter.

field.name: Name
field.doc: Name of the parameter variable.

field.name: Identifier
field.doc: Variable identifier of the parameter.

field.name: Type
field.doc: Data type of the parameter.

field.name: Flags
field.doc: Flags used to further describe the parameter.

enum.doc: Available cursor types.

struct.doc: Single key in a ColorGradient.

enum.doc: Projection type to use by the camera.

enumentry.doc: Projection type where object size remains constant and parallel lines remain parallel.
Projection type that emulates human vision. Objects farther away appear smaller.

class.doc: A GUI element container with support for vertical & horizontal scrolling.

method.doc: Scrolls the area up by specified amount of pixels, if possible.
Scrolls the area down by specified amount of pixels, if possible.
Scrolls the area left by specified amount of pixels, if possible.
Scrolls the area right by specified amount of pixels, if possible.
Scrolls the area up by specified percentage (ranging [0, 1]), if possible.
Scrolls the area down by specified percentage (ranging [0, 1]), if possible.
Scrolls the area left by specified percentage (ranging [0, 1]), if possible.
Scrolls the area right by specified percentage (ranging [0, 1]), if possible.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: contents
param.doc: Structure describing the contents of the GUI element to create.

param.name: styleClass
param.doc: Style class that will be used for determining GUI element visuals from the current style sheet. If no class is provided, default style is determined based on GUI element type.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: contents
param.doc: Structure describing the contents of the GUI element to create.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: styleClass
param.doc: Style class that will be used for determining GUI element visuals from the current style sheet. If no class is provided, default style is determined based on GUI element type.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

property.name: Layout
property.doc: Returns the scroll area layout that you may use to add elements inside the scroll area.

property.name: VerticalScroll
property.doc: Scrolls the contents to the specified position (0 meaning top-most part of the content is visible, and 1 meaning bottom-most part is visible).

property.name: HorizontalScroll
property.doc: Scrolls the contents to the specified position (0 meaning left-most part of the content is visible, and 1 meaning right-most part is visible)

property.name: ContentBounds
property.doc: Returns the bounds of the scroll area not including the scroll bars (meaning only the portion that contains the contents).

property.name: EnableCulling
property.doc: Enables/disables culling of child elements. If culling is enabled all child elements that are fully outside of the parent visible bounds will be marked as culled. Culled elements will never have their contents or mesh updated, their absolute coordinate will not be updated and they wont be drawn This is useful for layouts with a large amount of children, but comes with an overhead so it is disabled by default. Note this has no impact on layout update, which may still be expensive with many elements.

property.name: ScrollBarSize
property.doc: Returns the width or height of the scrollbar.

class.doc: Represents a range of color values over some parameters, similar to a curve. Internally represented as a set of keys that get interpolated between. Stores colors as 32-bit integers, and is therefor unable to represent a color range outside of [0, 1] - see ColorGradientHDR for an alternative.

method.doc: Keys that control the gradient, sorted by time from first to last. Key times should be in range [0, 1].
Returns the color key at the specified index. If out of range an empty key is returned.
Specify a "gradient" that represents a single color value.

property.name: NumKeys
property.doc: Returns the number of color keys in the gradient.

class.doc: Base class for all GUI elements. Provides general functionality such as element size/position, as well as handling child/parent relationships.

method.doc: Sets element position relative to parent GUI panel. Values should be provided in logical pixel units. Be aware that this value will be ignored if GUI element is part of a layout since then the layout controls its placement.

Sets element position relative to parent GUI panel. Values should be provided in logical pixel units. Be aware that this value will be ignored if GUI element is part of a layout since then the layout controls its placement.
Sets fixed element width. Value should be in logical pixel units.

Sets flexible element width. Element will be resized according to its contents and parent layout but will always stay within the provided range. If maximum width is zero, the element is allowed to expand as much as it needs. Values should be in logical pixel units.
Sets fixed element height. Value should be in logical pixel units.

Sets flexible element height. Element will be resized according to its contents and parent layout but will always stay within the provided range. If maximum height is zero, the element is allowed to expand as much as it needs. Values provided should be in logical pixel units.
Sets fixed width and height of a GUI element. Values provided should be in logical pixel units.
Resets element size constraints to their initial values dictated by the element's style.
Calculates position of the GUI element, relative to the provided parent element (or parent panel if null). The value is provided in logical pixel units.

param.name: relativeTo
param.doc: Parent element of the provided element relative to which to return the position. If null the position relative to parent panel is returned. Behavior is undefined if provided parent is not a parent of the element.

method.doc: Calculates bounds of the GUI element, relative to the provided parent element (or parent panel if null), with scaling applied. The values are provided in physical pixel units.

param.name: relativeTo
param.doc: Parent element of the provided element relative to which to return the bounds. If null the bounds relative to parent panel are returned. Behavior is undefined if provided parent is not a parent of the element.

method.doc: Converts a point relative to the parent widget, into a point relative to this element.
Converts a point relative to this element, into a point relative to the parent widget.
Converts an area relative to the parent widget, into an area relative to this element.
Converts an area relative to this element, into an area relative to the parent widget.
Destroy the element. Removes it from parent and widget, and queues it for deletion. Element memory will be released delayed, next frame.
Triggers a layout update immediately if the layout has been dirtied.

Sets flexible element width. Element will be resized according to its contents and parent layout but will always stay within the provided range. If maximum width is zero, the element is allowed to expand as much as it needs. Values should be in logical pixel units.

Sets flexible element width. Element will be resized according to its contents and parent layout but will always stay within the provided range. If maximum width is zero, the element is allowed to expand as much as it needs. Values should be in logical pixel units.

Sets flexible element height. Element will be resized according to its contents and parent layout but will always stay within the provided range. If maximum height is zero, the element is allowed to expand as much as it needs. Values provided should be in logical pixel units.

Sets flexible element height. Element will be resized according to its contents and parent layout but will always stay within the provided range. If maximum height is zero, the element is allowed to expand as much as it needs. Values provided should be in logical pixel units.

property.name: Hidden
property.doc: Hides or shows this element and recursively applies the same state to all the child elements. This will not remove the element from the layout, the room for it will still be reserved but it just won't be visible.

property.name: Active
property.doc: Activates or deactives this element and recursively applies the same state to all the child elements. This has the same effect as setVisible(), but when disabled it will also remove the element from the layout, essentially having the same effect is if you destroyed the element.

property.name: Disabled
property.doc: Disables or enables the element. Disabled elements cannot be interacted with and have a faded out appearance.

property.name: LayoutCalculatedSize
property.doc: Returns width/height of the GUI element. This will be the fixed width/height if set by the user, or automatically determined by the layout update pass if not fixed. Size is provided in logical pixel units.

property.name: AbsoluteBounds
property.doc: Calculates bounds of the GUI element, relative to the parent GUI widget, with scaling applied. The values are provided in physical pixel units.

property.name: ScreenBounds
property.doc: Calculates bounds of the GUI element in screen space.

property.name: Parent
property.doc: Returns parent GUI base element.

class.doc: Animation spline represented by a set of keyframes, each representing an endpoint of a cubic hermite curve. The spline can be evaluated at any time, and uses caching to speed up multiple sequential evaluations.

ctor.doc: Creates a new animation curve.

param.name: keyframes
param.doc: Keyframes to initialize the curve with. They must be sorted by time.

method.doc: Evaluate the animation curve at the specified time. If evaluating multiple values in a sequential order consider using the cached version of Evaluate() for better performance.

param.name: time
param.doc: %Time to evaluate the curve at.

param.name: loop
param.doc: If true the curve will loop when it goes past the end or beggining. Otherwise the curve value will be clamped.

returns.doc: Interpolated value from the curve at provided time.

property.name: KeyFrames
property.doc: Returns a list of all keyframes in the curve.

class.doc: Animation spline represented by a set of keyframes, each representing an endpoint of a cubic hermite curve. The spline can be evaluated at any time, and uses caching to speed up multiple sequential evaluations.

ctor.doc: Creates a new animation curve.

param.name: keyframes

method.doc: Evaluate the animation curve at the specified time. If evaluating multiple values in a sequential order consider using the cached version of Evaluate() for better performance.

param.name: time
param.doc: %Time to evaluate the curve at.

param.name: loop
param.doc: If true the curve will loop when it goes past the end or beggining. Otherwise the curve value will be clamped.

returns.doc: Interpolated value from the curve at provided time.

property.name: KeyFrames
property.doc: Returns a list of all keyframes in the curve.

ctor.doc: Creates a new animation curve.

enum.doc: Determines type of distribution used by distribution properties.

enumentry.doc: The distribution is a costant value.
The distribution is a random value in a specified constant range.
The distribution is a time-varying value.
The distribution is a random value in a specified time-varying range.

struct.doc: Information describing a ParticleEmitterSphereShape.

field.name: Radius
field.doc: Radius of the sphere.

field.name: Thickness
field.doc: Proportion of the volume that can emit particles. Thickness of 0 results in particles being emitted only from the edge of the volume, while thickness of 1 results in particles being emitted from the entire volume. In-between values will use a part of the volume.

class.doc: Represents a range of color values over some parameters, similar to a curve. Internally represented as a set of keys that get interpolated between. Capable of representing HDR colors, unlike the normal ColorGradient.

method.doc: Keys that control the gradient, sorted by time from first to last. Key times should be in range [0, 1].
Returns the color key at the specified index. If out of range an empty key is returned.
Specify a "gradient" that represents a single color value.

property.name: NumKeys
property.doc: Returns the number of color keys in the gradient.

class.doc: Contains a group of resources imported from a single source file.

struct.doc: Encapsulates width/height in a single structure.
Encapsulates width/height in a single structure.
Encapsulates width/height in a single structure.
Encapsulates width/height in a single structure.
Encapsulates width/height in a single structure.
Encapsulates width/height in a single structure.
Encapsulates width/height in a single structure.
Encapsulates width/height in a single structure.

enum.doc: Type of pointer event.

struct.doc: A two dimensional vector.
A two dimensional vector.
A two dimensional vector.
A two dimensional vector.
A two dimensional vector.

class.doc: Primary class for holding geometry. Stores data in the form of vertex buffers and optionally an index buffer, which may be bound to the pipeline for drawing. May contain multiple sub-meshes.

ctor.doc: Creates a new mesh with enough space to hold the a number of primitives using the specified layout. All indices will be part of a single sub-mesh.

param.name: numVertices
param.doc: Number of vertices in the mesh.

param.name: numIndices
param.doc: Number of indices in the mesh. Must be a multiple of primitive size as specified by provided topology.

param.name: topology
param.doc: Determines how should the provided indices be interpreted by the pipeline. Default option is a triangle list, where three indices represent a single triangle.

param.name: flags
param.doc: Flags to control various mesh options.

param.name: vertex
param.doc: Controls how are vertices organized in the vertex buffer and what data they contain.

param.name: index
param.doc: Size of indices, use smaller size for better performance, however be careful not to go over the number of vertices limited by the data type size.

ctor.doc: Creates a new mesh with enough space to hold the a number of primitives using the specified layout. Indices can be referenced by multiple sub-meshes.

param.name: numVertices
param.doc: Number of vertices in the mesh.

param.name: numIndices
param.doc: Number of indices in the mesh. Must be a multiple of primitive size as specified by provided topology.

param.name: subMeshes
param.doc: Defines how are indices separated into sub-meshes, and how are those sub-meshes rendered. Sub-meshes may be rendered independently, each with a different material.

param.name: flags
param.doc: Flags to control various mesh options.

param.name: vertex
param.doc: Controls how are vertices organized in the vertex buffer and what data they contain.

param.name: index
param.doc: Size of indices, use smaller size for better performance, however be careful not to go over the number of vertices limited by the data type size.

ctor.doc: Creates a new mesh from an existing mesh data. Created mesh will match the vertex and index buffers described by the mesh data exactly. Mesh will have no sub-meshes.

param.name: data
param.doc: Vertex and index data to initialize the mesh with.

param.name: topology
param.doc: Determines how should the provided indices be interpreted by the pipeline. Default option is a triangle list, where three indices represent a single triangle.

param.name: flags
param.doc: Flags to control various mesh options.

ctor.doc: Creates a new mesh with enough space to hold the a number of primitives using the specified layout. Indices can be referenced by multiple sub-meshes.

param.name: data
param.doc: Vertex and index data to initialize the mesh with.

param.name: subMeshes
param.doc: Defines how are indices separated into sub-meshes, and how are those sub-meshes rendered. Sub-meshes may be rendered independently, each with a different material.

param.name: flags
param.doc: Flags to control various mesh options.

property.name: Skeleton
property.doc: Gets the skeleton required for animation of this mesh, if any is available.

property.name: MorphShapes
property.doc: Returns an object containing all shapes used for morph animation, if any are available.

property.name: SubMeshes
property.doc: Returns all sub-meshes contained in the mesh.

property.name: SubMeshCount
property.doc: Returns the number of sub-meshes contained in this mesh.

property.name: MeshData
property.doc: Accesses the vertex and index data of the mesh. If reading, mesh must have been created with the MeshUsage::CPUCached flag. If writing the caller must ensure the data matches mesh's vertex/index counts, vertex layout and index format.

class.doc: Updates properties of all active particles in a particle system in some way.

enum.doc: Flags that determine which portion of the viewport to clear.
Available texture types.

enumentry.doc: Texture consisting out of six 2D textures describing an inside of a cube. Allows special sampling.
One dimensional texture. Just a row of pixels.
Two dimensional texture.
Three dimensional texture.

class.doc: Render target specialization that allows you to render into one or multiple textures. Such textures can then be used in other operations as GPU program input.

ctor.doc: Creates a new 2D render texture.

param.name: format
param.doc: Pixel format of the texture. Format must be a valid uncompressed color format.

param.name: width
param.doc: Width of the texture in pixels.

param.name: height
param.doc: Height of the texture in pixels.

param.name: numSamples
param.doc: Number of samples contained per pixel.

param.name: gammaCorrection
param.doc: Determines should the pixels written on the texture be gamma corrected.

param.name: createDepth
param.doc: Should the render texture also contain a depth/stencil buffer.

param.name: depthStencilFormat
param.doc: Format of the depth/stencil buffer, if is enabled. Format must be a valid depth/stencil format.

ctor.doc: Creates a new 2D render texture using an existing color texture, and no depth-stencil texture.

param.name: colorSurface
param.doc: Color texture to render color data to.

ctor.doc: Creates a new 2D render texture using existing textures as render destinations.

param.name: colorSurface
param.doc: Color texture to render color data to.

param.name: depthStencilSurface
param.doc: Optional depth/stencil texture to render depth/stencil data to.

ctor.doc: Creates a new 2D render texture using one or multiple color textures and no depth-stencil texture.

param.name: colorSurface
param.doc: Color texture(s) to render color data to.

ctor.doc: Creates a new 2D render texture using one or multiple color textures and a depth/stencil texture.

param.name: colorSurface
param.doc: Color texture(s) to render color data to.

param.name: depthStencilSurface
param.doc: Optional depth/stencil texture to render depth/stencil data to.

property.name: ColorSurface
property.doc: Returns the primary color surface that contains rendered color data.

property.name: ColorSurfaces
property.doc: Returns all the color surfaces.

property.name: DepthStencilSurface
property.doc: Returns the depth/stencil surface that contains rendered depth and stencil data.

struct.doc: Structure used for initializing a ParticleCollisions object.

field.name: Mode
field.doc: Collision mode determining with which geometry the particles will interact with.

field.name: Restitution
field.doc: Determines the elasticity (bounciness) of the particle collision. Lower values make the collision less bouncy and higher values more.

field.name: Dampening
field.doc: Determines how much velocity should a particle lose after a collision, in percent of its current velocity. In range [0, 1].

field.name: LifetimeLoss
field.doc: Determines how much should the particle lifetime be reduced after a collision, in percent of its original lifetime. In range [0, 1].

field.name: Radius
field.doc: Radius of every individual particle used for collisions, in meters.

field.name: Layer
field.doc: Physics layers that determine which objects will particle collide with. Only relevant when using the World collision mode.

struct.doc: A four dimensional vector.
A four dimensional vector.

enum.doc: Names of individual components of a size.

class.doc: Resource meta-data for user-defined managed resources.

struct.doc: A plane represented by a normal and a distance.
A plane represented by a normal and a distance.

enum.doc: Mode of the alpha channel in a texture.

struct.doc: Represents a joint limit that contraints movement to within an elliptical cone.

ctor.doc: Constructs a limit with a 45 degree cone.
Constructs a hard limit. Once the limit is reached the movement of the attached bodies will come to a stop.

param.name: yLimitAngle
param.doc: Y angle of the cone. Movement is constrainted between 0 and this angle on the Y axis.

param.name: zLimitAngle
param.doc: Z angle of the cone. Movement is constrainted between 0 and this angle on the Z axis.

param.name: contactDist
param.doc: Distance from the limit at which it becomes active. Allows the solver to activate earlier than the limit is reached to avoid breaking the limit. Specify -1 for the default.

ctor.doc: Constructs a soft limit. Once the limit is reached the bodies will bounce back according to the resitution parameter and will be pulled back towards the limit by the provided spring.

param.name: yLimitAngle
param.doc: Y angle of the cone. Movement is constrainted between 0 and this angle on the Y axis.

param.name: zLimitAngle
param.doc: Z angle of the cone. Movement is constrainted between 0 and this angle on the Z axis.

param.name: spring
param.doc: Spring that controls how are the bodies pulled back towards the limit when they breach it.

param.name: restitution
param.doc: Controls how do objects react when the limit is reached, values closer to zero specify non-ellastic collision, while those closer to one specify more ellastic (i.e bouncy) collision. Must be in [0, 1] range.

field.name: YLimitAngle
field.doc: Y angle of the cone. Movement is constrainted between 0 and this angle on the Y axis.

field.name: ZLimitAngle
field.doc: Z angle of the cone. Movement is constrainted between 0 and this angle on the Z axis.

field.name: ContactDist
field.doc: Distance from the limit at which it becomes active. Allows the solver to activate earlier than the limit is reached to avoid breaking the limit.

field.name: Restitution
field.doc: Controls how do objects react when the limit is reached, values closer to zero specify non-ellastic collision, while those closer to one specify more ellastic (i.e bouncy) collision. Must be in [0, 1] range.

field.name: Spring
field.doc: Spring that controls how are the bodies pulled back towards the limit when they breach it.

class.doc: Abstract class representing a texture. Specific render systems have their own Texture implementations. Internally represented as one or more surfaces with pixels in a certain number of dimensions, backed by a hardware buffer.

method.doc: Reads internal texture data into a newly allocated buffer.

param.name: face
param.doc: Texture face to read from.

param.name: mipLevel
param.doc: Mipmap level to read from.

returns.doc: Async operation object that will contain the buffer with the data once the operation completes.

method.doc: Returns pixels for the specified mip level & face. Pixels will be read from system memory, which means the texture has to be created with TextureUsage.CPUCached. If the texture was updated from the GPU the pixels retrieved from this method will not reflect that, and you should use GetGPUPixels instead.

param.name: face
param.doc: Face to read the pixels from. Cubemap textures have six faces whose face indices are as specified in the CubeFace enum. Array textures can have an arbitrary number of faces (if it's a cubemap array it has to be a multiple of 6).

param.name: mipLevel
param.doc: Mip level to retrieve pixels for. Top level (0) is the highest quality.

returns.doc: A set of pixels for the specified mip level.

method.doc: Sets pixels for the specified mip level and face.

param.name: data
param.doc: Pixels to assign to the specified mip level. Pixel data must match the mip level size and texture pixel format.

param.name: face
param.doc: Face to write the pixels to. Cubemap textures have six faces whose face indices are as specified in the CubeFace enum. Array textures can have an arbitrary number of faces (if it's a cubemap array it has to be a multiple of 6).

param.name: mipLevel
param.doc: Mip level to set pixels for. Top level (0) is the highest quality.

method.doc: Sets pixels for the specified mip level and face.

param.name: colors
param.doc: Pixels to assign to the specified mip level. Size of the array must match the mip level dimensions. Data is expected to be laid out row by row. Pixels will be automatically converted to the valid pixel format.

param.name: face
param.doc: Face to write the pixels to. Cubemap textures have six faces whose face indices are as specified in the CubeFace enum. Array textures can have an arbitrary number of faces (if it's a cubemap array it has to be a multiple of 6).

param.name: mipLevel
param.doc: Mip level to set pixels for. Top level (0) is the highest quality.

class.doc: GUI element that may be inserted into layouts in order to make a space of a fixed size.

ctor.doc: Creates a new fixed space GUI element.

property.name: Size
property.doc: Changes the size of the space to the specified value, in pixels.

struct.doc: Represents a 2D area. Area is represented with an origin in top left and width/height.
Represents a 2D area. Area is represented with an origin in top left and width/height.
Represents a 2D area. Area is represented with an origin in top left and width/height.
Represents a 2D area. Area is represented with an origin in top left and width/height.
Represents a 2D area. Area is represented with an origin in top left and width/height.
Represents a 2D area. Area is represented with an origin in top left and width/height.
Represents a 2D area. Area is represented with an origin in top left and width/height.
Axis aligned box represented by minimum and maximum point.
Axis aligned box represented by minimum and maximum point.
A sphere represented by a center point and a radius.

ctor.doc: Default constructor. Creates a unit sphere around the origin.

struct.doc: A sphere represented by a center point and a radius.

ctor.doc: Default constructor. Creates a unit sphere around the origin.

class.doc: Represents a single collider shape that can be assigned to a collider.

method.doc: Changes or sets the collider shape to a plane.
Changes or sets the collider shape to a box.
Changes or sets the collider shape to a sphere.
Changes or sets the collider shape to a capsule.
Changes or sets the collider shape to a mesh.

ctor.doc: Creates a new plane collider shape.
Creates a new box collider shape.
Creates a new sphere collider shape.
Creates a new capsule collider shape.
Creates a new mesh collider shape.

property.name: Type
property.doc: Returns the type of the collider shape.

property.name: Position
property.doc: Position of the collider shape, relative to the parent collider.

property.name: Rotation
property.doc: Rotation of the collider shape, relative to the parent collider.

property.name: Scale
property.doc: Scale of the collider shape, relative to the parent collider.

property.name: IsTrigger
property.doc: Enables/disables a collider as a trigger. A trigger will not be used for collisions (objects will pass through it), but collision events will still be reported.

property.name: Mass
property.doc: Determines the mass of the collider shape. Only relevant if the parent collider is part of a rigidbody. Ultimately this will determine the total mass, center of mass and inertia tensors of the parent rigidbody (if they're being calculated automatically).

property.name: Material
property.doc: Determines the physical material of the collider shape. The material determines how objects hitting the collider shape.

property.name: ContactOffset
property.doc: Determines how far apart do two shapes need to be away from each other before the physics runtime starts generating repelling impulse for them. This distance will be the sum of contact offsets of the two interacting objects. If objects are moving fast you can increase this value to start generating the impulse earlier and potentially prevent the objects from interpenetrating. This value is in meters. Must be positive and greater than rest offset. Also see SetRestOffset().

property.name: RestOffset
property.doc: Determines at what distance should two objects resting on one another come to an equilibrium. The value used in the runtime will be the sum of rest offsets for both interacting objects. This value is in meters. Cannot be larger than contact offset. Also see SetContactOffset().

property.name: Layer
property.doc: Determines the layer of the collider shape. Layer controls with which shapes will this shape collide.

property.name: CollisionReportMode
property.doc: Determines which (if any) collision events are reported.

property.name: PlaneShapeInformation
property.doc: Returns information about the plane shape. Will return default shape information if the current shape is not a plane.

property.name: BoxShapeInformation
property.doc: Returns information about the box shape. Will return default shape information if the current shape is not a box.

property.name: SphereShapeInformation
property.doc: Returns information about the sphere shape. Will return default shape information if the current shape is not a sphere.

property.name: CapsuleShapeInformation
property.doc: Returns information about the capsule shape. Will return default shape information if the current shape is not a capsule.

property.name: MeshShapeInformation
property.doc: Returns information about the mesh shape. Will return default shape information if the current shape is not a mesh.

class.doc: GUI element that displays text and optionally a content image.

method.doc: Changes the active content of the label.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: contents
param.doc: Structure describing the contents of the GUI element to create.

param.name: styleClass
param.doc: Style class that will be used for determining GUI element visuals from the current style sheet. If no class is provided, default style is determined based on GUI element type.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: contents
param.doc: Structure describing the contents of the GUI element to create.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: styleClass
param.doc: Style class that will be used for determining GUI element visuals from the current style sheet. If no class is provided, default style is determined based on GUI element type.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

class.doc: Generates pseudo random numbers using the Xorshift128 algorithm. Suitable for high performance requirements.

ctor.doc: Initializes a new generator using the specified seed.

method.doc: Changes the seed of the generator to the specified value.
Returns a random value in range [0, std::numeric_limits<uint32_t>::max()].
Returns a random value in range [min, max].
Returns a random value in range [0, 1].
Returns a random value in range [-1, 1].
Returns a random unit vector in three dimensions.
Returns a random unit vector in two dimensions.
Returns a random point inside a unit sphere.

Returns a random point inside the specified range in a sphere shell of unit radius, with the specified thickness, in range [0, 1]. Thickness of 0 will generate points on the sphere surface, while thickness of 1 will generate points within the entire sphere volume. Intermediate values represent the shell, which is a volume between two concentric spheres.
Returns a random point inside a unit circle.

Returns a random point inside the specified range in a circle shell of unit radius, with the specified thickness, in range [0, 1]. Thickness of 0 will generate points on the circle edge, while thickness of 1 will generate points within the entire circle surface. Intermediate values represent the shell, which is the surface between two concentric circles.
Returns a random point on a unit arc with the specified length (angle). Angle of 360 represents a circle.

Returns a random point inside the specified range in an arc shell of unit radius, with the specified length (angle) and thickness in range [0, 1]. Angle of 360 represents a circle shell. Thickness of 0 will generate points on the arc edge, while thickness of 1 will generate points on the entire arc 'slice'. Intermediate vlaues represent the shell, which is the surface between two concentric circles.
Returns a random set of Barycentric coordinates that may be used for generating random points on a triangle.

struct.doc: A ray in 3D space represented with an origin and direction.
A ray in 3D space represented with an origin and direction.

Represents a rectangle in three dimensional space. It is represented by two axes that extend from the specified origin. Axes should be perpendicular to each other and they extend in both positive and negative directions from the origin by the amount specified by extents.

class.doc: Implementation of SpriteImage that renders a vector path.

ctor.doc: Creates a new sprite vector path.
Creates a new sprite vector path.

struct.doc: A three dimensional vector with integer coordinates.
A three dimensional vector with integer coordinates.
Information describing a ParticleEmitterConeShape.

field.name: Type
field.doc: Determines where on the cone are the particles emitter from.

field.name: Radius
field.doc: Radius of the cone base.

field.name: Angle
field.doc: Angle of the cone.

field.name: Length
field.doc: Length of the cone. Irrelevant if emission type is Base.

field.name: Thickness
field.doc: Proportion of the volume that can emit particles. Thickness of 0 results in particles being emitted only from the edge of the cone, while thickness of 1 results in particles being emitted from the entire volume. In-between values will use a part of the volume.

field.name: Arc
field.doc: Angular portion of the cone from which to emit particles from, in degrees.

field.name: Mode
field.doc: Determines how will particle positions on the shape be generated.

enum.doc: Type of parameters that can be defined by a shader.

struct.doc: A four dimensional vector with integer coordinates.
A four dimensional vector with integer coordinates.

enum.doc: Types of available chromatic aberration effects.

enumentry.doc: More complex chromatic aberration effect that takes longer to execute but may yield more visually pleasing results than the simple variant.
Simple chromatic aberration effect that is fast to execute.

class.doc: String handle. Provides a wrapper around an Unicode string, primarily for localization purposes. Actual value for this string is looked up in a global string table based on the provided identifier string and currently active language. If such value doesn't exist then the identifier is used as is. Use {0}, {1}, etc. in the string value for values that might change dynamically.

ctor.doc: Creates a new localized string with the specified identifier. If the identifier doesn't previously exist in the string table, identifier value will also be used for initializing the default language version of the string.

param.name: identifier
param.doc: String you can use for later referencing the localized string.

param.name: stringTableId
param.doc: Unique identifier of the string table to retrieve the string from.

ctor.doc: Creates a new localized string with the specified identifier and sets the default language version of the string. If a string with that identifier already exists default language string will be updated.

param.name: identifier
param.doc: String you can use for later referencing the localized string.

param.name: defaultString
param.doc: Default string to assign to the specified identifier. Language to which it will be assigned depends on the StringTable::DEFAULT_LANGUAGE value.

param.name: stringTableId
param.doc: Unique identifier of the string table to retrieve the string from.

ctor.doc: Creates a new empty localized string.

param.name: stringTableId
param.doc: Unique identifier of the string table to retrieve the string from.

ctor.doc: Creates a new empty localized string.

method.doc: Sets a value of a string parameter. Parameters are specified as bracketed values within the string itself (for example {0}, {1}) etc. Use ^ as an escape character.

param.name: index
param.doc: Index of the parameter to set.

param.name: value
param.doc: Value to assign to the parameter.

struct.doc: References a subset of surfaces within a texture.

field.name: MipLevel
field.doc: First mip level to reference.

field.name: MipLevelCount
field.doc: Number of mip levels to reference. Must be greater than zero.

field.name: Face
field.doc: First face to reference. Face can represent a single cubemap face, or a single array entry in a texture array. If cubemaps are laid out in a texture array then every six sequential faces represent a single array entry.

field.name: FaceCount
field.doc: Number of faces to reference, if the texture has more than one.

field.name: IsBoundAs2DArray
field.doc: Forces a cubemap or a 3D texture to be bound as a 2D texture array.

class.doc: Contains a set of morph channels used for morph target animation. Each morph channel contains one or multiple shapes which are blended together depending on frame animation. Each channel is then additively blended together depending on some weight.

property.name: Channels
property.doc: Returns a list of all morph channels in the morph animation.

enum.doc: Filtering types to use when scaling images.
Determines the type of the source image for generating cubemaps.

enumentry.doc: Source is a single image that will be replicated on all cubemap faces.
Source is a list of 6 images, either sequentially next to each other or in a cross format. The system will automatically guess the layout and orientation based on the aspect ratio.
Source is a single spherical panoramic image.
Source is a single cylindrical panoramic image.

enum.doc: Type of force that can be applied to a rigidbody at an arbitrary point.

enumentry.doc: Value applied is an impulse (a direct change in its linear or angular momentum).
Value applied is a force.

class.doc: Settings that control how is a VectorPath drawn.
Contains data about a single field in a managed object (class or struct).

enum.doc: Valid types of a mesh used for physics.

enumentry.doc: A regular triangle mesh. Mesh can be of arbitrary size but cannot be used for triggers and non-kinematic objects. Incurrs a significantly larger performance impact than convex meshes.
Mesh representing a convex shape. Mesh will not have more than 256 vertices. Incurrs a significantly lower performance impact than triangle meshes.

class.doc: Particle emitter shape that emits particles from a surface of a static (non-animated) mesh. Particles can be emitted from mesh vertices, edges or triangles. If information about normals exists, particles will also inherit the normals.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle emitter static mesh shape.
Creates a new particle emitter static mesh shape.

property.name: Settings
property.doc: Options describing the shape.

struct.doc: Represents a 3D region of pixels used for referencing pixel data.

enum.doc: Names of individual components of a vector.

class.doc: Settings that control the bloom effect. Bloom adds an extra highlight to bright areas of the scene.

property.name: Enabled
property.doc: Enables or disables the bloom effect.

property.name: Quality
property.doc: Quality of the bloom effect. Higher values will use higher resolution texture for calculating bloom, at the cost of lower performance. Valid range is [0, 3], default is 2.

property.name: Threshold
property.doc: Determines the minimal threshold of pixel luminance to be included in the bloom calculations. Any pixel with luminance below this value will be ignored for the purposes of bloom. The value represents luminance after it is scaled by exposure. Set to zero or negative to disable the threshold and include all pixels in the calculations.

property.name: Intensity
property.doc: Determines the intensity of the bloom effect. Ideally should be in [0, 4] range but higher values are allowed.

property.name: Tint
property.doc: Tint color to apply to the bloom highlight. A pure white means the bloom inherits the underlying scene color.

property.name: FilterSize
property.doc: Determines the percent of the texture to account for when filtering for bloom. Larger values will include farther away pixels.

class.doc: Helper class for dealing with animations, animation clips and curves.

method.doc: Converts a curve in euler angles (in degrees) into a curve using quaternions.
Converts a curve in quaternions into a curve using euler angles (in degrees).
Splits a Vector3 curve into three individual curves, one for each component.
Combines three single component curves into a Vector3 curve.
Splits a Vector2 curve into two individual curves, one for each component.
Combines two single component curves into a Vector2 curve.

enum.doc: Types of notifications we would like to receive when we start a FolderMonitor on a certain folder.

enumentry.doc: Called when file is created, moved or removed.
Called when directory is created, moved or removed.
Called when file is written to.

enum.doc: Names of individual components of a color.
Identifiers representing a range of values.
Type of GUI element options.

class.doc: Contains user-specified meta-data for a resource stored in a Package.

struct.doc: Contains internal information about a single bone in a Skeleton.

field.name: Name
field.doc: Unique name of the bone.

field.name: Parent
field.doc: Index of the parent bone (within the relevant Skeleton object). -1 if root bone.

field.name: InvBindPose
field.doc: Inverse transform of the pose the skeleton was initially created in.

enum.doc: Controls what kind of mobility restrictions a scene object has. This is used primarily as a performance hint to other systems. Generally the more restricted the mobility the higher performance can be achieved.

enumentry.doc: Scene object can be moved and has no mobility restrictions.
Scene object isn't allowed to be moved but is allowed to be visually changed in other ways (e.g. changing the displayed mesh or light intensity (depends on attached components).
Scene object isn't allowed to be moved nor is it allowed to be visually changed. Object must be fully static.

class.doc: Contains a set of animation curves used for moving and rotating the root bone.

property.name: Position
property.doc: Animation curve representing the movement of the root bone.

property.name: Rotation
property.doc: Animation curve representing the rotation of the root bone.

enum.doc: Flags that describe how a texture is used.

enumentry.doc: Allows retrieving views of the texture using a different format than specified on creation.
Texture used as a depth/stencil buffer by the GPU. Must be combined with StoreOnGPU flag.

Places the texture into CPU memory accessible to the GPU. This means the texture is faster to update from the CPU, but it's slower to access by the GPU. Not allowed for GPU-writeable textures (render target, depth stencil or unordered access).
Default setting suitable for majority of textures.
Texture that can be rendered to by the GPU. Must be combined with StoreOnGPU flag.
Ensures that the GPU can perform unordered write operations on the texture. Generally used for textures in compute operations. Must be combined with StoreOnGPU flag.
All texture data will also be cached in CPU memory for fast read access from the CPU. Only relevant for main thread textures, ignored for render thread textures.

enum.doc: Type of scaling modes for GUI images.

enumentry.doc: Image will keep its original size, but will repeat in order to fill the assigned area.
Image will stretch non-uniformly in all dimensions in order to cover the assigned area fully.
Image will scale uniformly until one dimension is aligned with the assigned area. Remaining dimension might have empty space.
Image will scale uniformly until both dimensions are larger or aligned with the assigned area. Remaining dimension might be cropped.

class.doc: Contains data about serializable fields of a managed object, and the object's class hierarchy if it belongs to one. All public fields are by default serializable if their type support serialization. Type is serializable if it has the SerializeObject attribute, or is one of the built-in supported serializable types (primitive such as int or bool, game object, resource or resource reference). Array/List/Dictionary of serializable types is also considered serializable. Public field can be made non-serializable via the DontSerializeField attribute. Private/protected/internal field can be made serializable by specifying the SerializeField attribute.

Particle emitter shape that emits particles from a surface of a skinned (animated) mesh. Particles can be emitted from mesh vertices, edges or triangles. If information about normals exists, particles will also inherit the normals.

class.doc: Manages global time related functionality.

property.name: RealTimeInSeconds
property.doc: Gets the time elapsed since application start. Only gets updated once per frame.

property.name: RealTimeInMilliseconds
property.doc: Gets the time elapsed since application start. Only gets updated once per frame.

property.name: FrameDelta
property.doc: Gets the time since last frame was executed. Only gets updated once per frame.

property.name: CurrentFrameIndex
property.doc: Returns the sequential index of the current frame. First frame is 0.

property.name: TimePrecise
property.doc: Returns the precise time since application start, in microseconds. Unlike other time methods this is not only updated every frame, but will return exact time at the moment it is called.

struct.doc: Same as TResult, but with no output object.

class.doc: A collection of morph shapes that are sequentially blended together. Each shape has a weight in range [0, 1] which determines at what point is that shape blended. As the channel percent moves from 0 to 1, different shapes will be blended with those before or after them, depending on their weight.

property.name: Name
property.doc: Returns the unique name of the channel.

property.name: Shapes
property.doc: Returns all morph shapes within this channel, in order from lowest to highest.

class.doc: Represents a source for emitting audio. Audio can be played spatially (gun shot), or normally (music). Each audio source must have an AudioClip to play-back, and it can also have a position in the case of spatial (3D) audio. Whether or not an audio source is spatial is controlled by the assigned AudioClip. The volume and the pitch of a spatial audio source is controlled by its position and the AudioListener's position/direction/velocity.

method.doc: Starts playing the currently assigned audio clip.
Pauses the audio playback.
Stops audio playback, rewinding it to the start.

property.name: Clip
property.doc: Audio clip to play.

property.name: Volume
property.doc: Volume of the audio played from this source, in [0, 1] range.

property.name: Pitch
property.doc: Determines the pitch of the played audio. 1 is the default.

property.name: Loop
property.doc: Determines whether the audio clip should loop when it finishes playing.

property.name: Priority
property.doc: Determines the priority of the audio source. If more audio sources are playing than supported by the hardware, some might get disabled. By setting a higher priority the audio source is guaranteed to be disabled after sources with lower priority.

property.name: MinDistance
property.doc: Minimum distance at which audio attenuation starts. When the listener is closer to the source than this value, audio is heard at full volume. Once farther away the audio starts attenuating.

property.name: Attenuation
property.doc: Attenuation that controls how quickly does audio volume drop off as the listener moves further from the source.

property.name: Time
property.doc: Determines the current time of playback. If playback hasn't yet started, it specifies the time at which playback will start at. The time is in seconds, in range [0, clipLength].

property.name: PlayOnStart
property.doc: Sets whether playback should start as soon as the component is enabled.

property.name: State
property.doc: Returns the current state of the audio playback (playing/paused/stopped).

class.doc: Contains mesh vertex and index data used for initializing, updating and reading mesh data from Mesh.
Manages simulation time for a particular scene. This time runs only while scene is simulating.

method.doc: Resets the simulation time to zero. Primarily used for editor purposes for resetting the time when ending play in editor.
Pauses or unpauses the simulation time. This is equivalent to setting the time scale to 0.

property.name: TimeInSeconds
property.doc: Gets the time since the simulation started playing, multiplied by the time scale factor. In editor this will reset to zero every time you start playing in editor, and in a standalone application this will be similar to GetRealTimeInSeconds(), except simulation time can be sped up/down, or stopped entirely by setting the time scale.

property.name: Scale
property.doc: Allows you to speed time up or down, or completely pause it by providing zero. Must be zero or larger.

field.name: Value
field.doc: Value of the key.

field.name: InTangent
field.doc: Input tangent (going from the previous key to this one) of the key.

field.name: OutTangent
field.doc: Output tangent (going from this key to next one) of the key.

field.name: Time
field.doc: Position of the key along the animation spline.

enum.doc: Flags that control Mesh behaviour.

enumentry.doc: Mesh is not going to change ever, or is going to change rarely. Mesh vertex & index buffers will be allocated in GPU memory.
Mesh is going to change often (e.g. every frame). Mesh vertex & index buffers will be allocated in CPU memory which is GPU accessible.
Mesh vertex and input buffers can be bound for unordered access (i.e. structured storage buffers) in the shaders. Provide this your shader is manually pulling vertex/index data in the shader.

Normally when a mesh is uploaded to the GPU, the CPU memory is no longer needed so it will be released. If this flag is provided the CPU mesh data will be kept. This allows you to access mesh data on the CPU.

class.doc: Represents a three dimensional field of vectors. It is represented by spatial bounds which are split into a grid of values with user-defined density, where each grid cell is assigned a vector.

ctor.doc: Creates a new vector field.

param.name: desc
param.doc: Description of the vector field to create.

param.name: values
param.doc: Values to assign to the vector field. Number of entries must match countX * countY * countZ.

enum.doc: Flags that describe an animation curve.

enumentry.doc: If enabled, the curve was imported from an external file and not created within the engine. This will affect how are animation results applied to scene objects (with imported animations it is assumed the curve is animating bones and with in-engine curves it is assumed the curve is animating scene objects).
Signifies the curve is used to animate between different frames within a morph channel. In range [0, 1].
Signifies the curve is used to adjust the weight of a morph channel. In range [0, 1].

struct.doc: Options used to control texture mip map generation.
Options used to control texture compression.
An animation curve and its name.

enum.doc: Types of builtin meshes that are always available.

struct.doc: Information used for initializing a virtual axis.

field.name: Type
field.doc: Type of physical axis to map to. See InputAxis type for common types, but you are not limited to those values.

field.name: DeadZone
field.doc: Value below which to ignore axis value and consider it 0.

field.name: Sensitivity
field.doc: Higher sensitivity means the axis will more easily reach its maximum values.

field.name: Invert
field.doc: Should the axis be inverted.

field.name: Normalize
field.doc: If enabled, axis values will be normalized to [-1, 1] range. Most axes already come in normalized form and this value will not affect such axes. Some axes, like mouse movement are not normalized by default and will instead report relative movement. By enabling this you will normalize such axes to [-1, 1] range.

class.doc: Contains information about a single variation of a Shader. Each variation can have a separate set of #defines that control shader compilation.

method.doc: Returns the value of a signed integer parameter with the specified name. Returns 0 if the parameter cannot be found.
Returns the value of a unsigned integer parameter with the specified name. Returns 0 if the parameter cannot be found.
Returns the value of a float parameter with the specified name. Returns 0 if the parameter cannot be found.
Returns the value of a boolean parameter with the specified name. Returns false if the parameter cannot be found.
Sets the value of the parameter for the provided name. Any previous value for a parameter with the same name will be overwritten.
Sets the value of the parameter for the provided name. Any previous value for a parameter with the same name will be overwritten.
Sets the value of the parameter for the provided name. Any previous value for a parameter with the same name will be overwritten.
Sets the value of the parameter for the provided name. Any previous value for a parameter with the same name will be overwritten.
Removes a parameter with the specified name.
Checks if the variation has a parameter with the specified name.
Removes all parameters.

property.name: ParamNames
property.doc: Returns a list of names of all registered parameters.

struct.doc: Structure used for initializing a ParticleTextureAnimation object.

field.name: RandomizeRow
field.doc: Randomly pick a row to use for animation when the particle is first spawned. This implies that only a single row of the grid will be used for individual particle's animation.

field.name: CycleCount
field.doc: Number of cycles to loop the animation during particle's lifetime.

enum.doc: Pixel formats usable by images, textures and render surfaces.

enumentry.doc: 8-bit 1-channel pixel format, unsigned normalized.
8-bit 2-channel pixel format, unsigned normalized.
8-bit 3-channel pixel format, unsigned normalized.
8-bit 3-channel pixel format, unsigned normalized.
32-bit 4-channel pixel format, unsigned integer.
8-bit 4-channel pixel format, unsigned normalized.
8-bit 4-channel pixel format, unsigned normalized.
8-bit 2-channel pixel format, signed integer.
DXT1/BC1 format containing opaque RGB or 1-bit alpha RGB. 4 bits per pixel.
16-bit 2-channel pixel format, signed integer.
DXT3/BC2 format containing RGB with explicit alpha. 8 bits per pixel.
16-bit 3-channel pixel format, unsigned normalized.
DXT5/BC2 format containing RGB with explicit alpha. 8 bits per pixel. Better alpha gradients than BC2.
32-bit 1-channel pixel format, signed float.
One channel compressed format. 4 bits per pixel.
16-bit 1-channel pixel format, signed integer.
Two channel compressed format. 8 bits per pixel.
8-bit 1-channel pixel format, signed normalized.
8-bit 4-channel pixel format, signed integer.
Format storing RGB in half (16-bit) floating point format usable for HDR. 8 bits per pixel.
Format storing RGB with optional alpha channel. Similar to BC1/BC2/BC3 formats but with higher quality and higher decompress overhead. 8 bits per pixel.
32-bit 3-channel pixel format, unsigned integer.
16-bit 1-channel pixel format, signed float.
16-bit 4-channel pixel format, signed normalized.
16-bit 2-channel pixel format, signed float.
16-bit 2-channel pixel format, signed normalized.
Depth stencil fomrat, 24bit depth + 8bit stencil. Depth stored as unsigned normalized.
16-bit 4-channel pixel format, signed float.
32-bit 2-channel pixel format, signed float.
16-bit 1-channel pixel format, unsigned integer.
32-bit 3-channel pixel format, signed float.
32-bit 4-channel pixel format, signed float.
Depth stencil format, 32bit depth, 8bit stencil + 24 unused. Depth stored as signed float.
Depth format, 32bits. Signed float.
Depth format, 16bits. Unsigned normalized.
32-bit 2-channel pixel format, signed integer.
8-bit 4-channel pixel format, signed normalized.
8-bit 1-channel pixel format, signed integer.
32-bit 2-channel pixel format, unsigned integer.
8-bit 1-channel pixel format, unsigned integer.
8-bit 2-channel pixel format, unsigned integer.
8-bit 4-channel pixel format, unsigned integer.
8-bit 2-channel pixel format, signed normalized.
32-bit 1-channel pixel format, unsigned integer.
16-bit 4-channel pixel format, signed integer.
16-bit 2-channel pixel format, unsigned integer.
32-bit 1-channel pixel format, signed integer.
16-bit 4-channel pixel format, unsigned integer.
32-bit 3-channel pixel format, signed integer.
32-bit 4-channel pixel format, signed integer.
16-bit 1-channel pixel format, signed normalized.
16-bit 1-channel pixel format, unsigned normalized.
16-bit 2-channel pixel format, unsigned normalized.
16-bit 4-channel pixel format, unsigned normalized.

struct.doc: Information about a single page containing font bitmaps.

enum.doc: Types of texture compression quality.
Wrap mode to use when generating mip maps.
Supported image file formats for export.

struct.doc: Structure describing contents of a GUIToggle element.

class.doc: Viewport determines to which RenderTarget should rendering be performed. It allows you to render to a sub-region of the target by specifying the area rectangle, and allows you to set up color/depth/stencil clear values for that specific region.

ctor.doc: Creates a new viewport.

property.name: Target
property.doc: Determines the render target the viewport is associated with.

property.name: Area
property.doc: Determines the area that the viewport covers. Coordinates are in normalized [0, 1] range.

property.name: PixelArea
property.doc: Returns the area of the render target covered by the viewport, in pixels.

property.name: ClearFlags
property.doc: Determines which portions of the render target should be cleared before rendering to this viewport is performed.

property.name: ClearColor
property.doc: Determines the color to clear the viewport to before rendering, if color clear is enabled.

property.name: ClearDepth
property.doc: Determines the value to clear the depth buffer to before rendering, if depth clear is enabled.

property.name: ClearStencil
property.doc: Determines the value to clear the stencil buffer to before rendering, if stencil clear is enabled.

struct.doc: Information describing a plane collider shape that extends infinitely in the X/Z axes.

class.doc: Render target is a frame buffer or a texture that the render system renders the scene to.

struct.doc: Video mode contains information about how a render window presents its information to an output device like a monitor.

ctor.doc: Creates a new video mode.

param.name: width
param.doc: Width of the frame buffer in pixels.

param.name: height
param.doc: Height of the frame buffer in pixels.

param.name: refreshRate
param.doc: How often should the output device refresh the output image in hertz.

param.name: outputIdx
param.doc: Output index of the output device. Normally this means output monitor. 0th index always represents the primary device while order of others is undefined.

field.name: Width
field.doc: Width of the front/back buffer in pixels.

field.name: Height
field.doc: Height of the front/back buffer in pixels.

field.name: RefreshRate
field.doc: Refresh rate in hertz.

field.name: OutputIdx
field.doc: Index of the parent video output.

field.name: IsCustom
field.doc: Determines was video mode user created or provided by the API/OS. API/OS created video modes can contain additional information that allows the video mode to be used more accurately and you should use them when possible.

struct.doc: Structure that is used for initializing a render window.

field.name: VideoMode
field.doc: Output monitor, frame buffer resize and refresh rate. This is the size of the window's client area (which means actual window may be larger due to title bar and border.

field.name: Fullscreen
field.doc: Should the window be opened in fullscreen mode.

field.name: Vsync
field.doc: Should the window wait for vertical sync before swapping buffers.

field.name: VsyncInterval
field.doc: Determines how many vsync intervals occur per frame. FPS = refreshRate/interval. Usually 1 when vsync active.

field.name: Hidden
field.doc: Should the window be hidden initially.

field.name: DepthBuffer
field.doc: Should the window be created with a depth/stencil buffer.

field.name: MultisampleCount
field.doc: If higher than 1, texture containing multiple samples per pixel is created.

field.name: MultisampleHint
field.doc: Hint about what kind of multisampling to use. Render system specific.

field.name: Gamma
field.doc: Should the written color pixels be gamma corrected before write.

field.name: Left
field.doc: Window origin on X axis in pixels. -1 == screen center. Relative to monitor provided in videoMode.

field.name: Top
field.doc: Window origin on Y axis in pixels. -1 == screen center. Relative to monitor provided in videoMode.

field.name: Title
field.doc: Title of the window.

field.name: ShowTitleBar
field.doc: Determines if the title-bar should be shown or not.

field.name: ShowBorder
field.doc: Determines if the window border should be shown or not.

field.name: AllowResize
field.doc: Determines if the user can resize the window by dragging on the window edges.

field.name: ToolWindow
field.doc: Tool windows have no task bar entry and always remain on top of their parent window.

field.name: Modal
field.doc: When a modal window is open all other windows will be locked until modal window is closed.

field.name: HideUntilSwap
field.doc: Window will be created as hidden and only be shown when the first framebuffer swap happens.

field.name: CreateRenderSurface
field.doc: If true the window render surface will be created, which internally provides a swap chain and allows the GPU to render to the window.

field.name: Headless
field.doc: When true, no OS window is created. Instead, the window renders to internal GPU textures that mimic a swap chain. All window-handle-dependent operations (focus, input capture, cursor, etc.) become no-ops. Use this for headless rendering in automated testing or offscreen rendering scenarios.

struct.doc: Information describing a ParticleEmitterHemisphereShape.

field.name: Radius
field.doc: Radius of the hemisphere.

field.name: Thickness
field.doc: Proportion of the volume that can emit particles. Thickness of 0 results in particles being emitted only from the edge of the volume, while thickness of 1 results in particles being emitted from the entire volume. In-between values will use a part of the volume.

struct.doc: Information describing a mesh collider shape.
Structure containing parameters for starting the application.

field.name: GpuBackend
field.doc: Name of the GPU backend plugin to use.

field.name: Renderer
field.doc: Name of the renderer plugin to use.

field.name: Physics
field.doc: Name of physics plugin to use.

field.name: Audio
field.doc: Name of the audio plugin to use.

field.name: Input
field.doc: Name of the input plugin to use.

field.name: PhysicsCooking
field.doc: True if physics cooking library should be loaded. Cooking is useful for creating collision meshes during development type, but might be unnecessary in the final application. When turned off you can save on space by not shipping the cooking library.

field.name: AsyncAnimation
field.doc: True if animation should be evaluated at the same time while rendering is happening. This introduces a one frame delay to all animations but can result in better performance. If false the animation will be forced to finish evaluating before rendering starts, ensuring up-to-date frame but potentially blocking the rendering thread from moving forward until the animation finishes.

field.name: PrimaryWindow
field.doc: Describes the window to create during start-up.

field.name: Importers
field.doc: A list of importer plugins to load.

class.doc: Primary entry point for the framework. Handles startup and shutdown.

enum.doc: Determines how is text horizontally aligned in a GUI element.
Controls distance joint options.

enumentry.doc: Enables minimum distance limit.
Enables maximum distance limit.
Enables spring when maintaining limits.

enum.doc: Determines how is text vertically aligned in a GUI element.

class.doc: Physics object that can be interacted with in the physics world. It can either block other physics objects, or act as a trigger. Trigger will report collision events, but won't actually prevent other physical objects from going through them. This object is intended to remain static in the world. You /can/ move it, but it will not interact with the physics world correctly when moved. For that case use Rigidbody instead.

property.name: Trigger
property.doc: Determines if the collider is a trigger. Trigger collider will not prevent objects from going through its shapes but it will still report collision events.

event.doc: Triggered when some object starts interacting with the collider. Only triggered if proper collision report mode is turned on.
Triggered for every frame that an object remains interacting with a collider. Only triggered if proper collision report mode is turned on.
Triggered when some object stops interacting with the collider. Only triggered if proper collision report mode is turned on.

class.doc: A set of animation curves representing translation/rotation/scale and generic animation.

method.doc: Registers a new curve used for animating position.

param.name: name
param.doc: Unique name of the curve. This name will be used mapping the curve to the relevant bone in a skeleton, if any.

param.name: curve
param.doc: Curve to add to the clip.

method.doc: Registers a new curve used for animating rotation.

param.name: name
param.doc: Unique name of the curve. This name will be used mapping the curve to the relevant bone in a skeleton, if any.

param.name: curve
param.doc: Curve to add to the clip.

method.doc: Registers a new curve used for animating scale.

param.name: name
param.doc: Unique name of the curve. This name will be used mapping the curve to the relevant bone in a skeleton, if any.

param.name: curve
param.doc: Curve to add to the clip.

method.doc: Registers a new curve used for generic animation.

param.name: name
param.doc: Unique name of the curve. This can be used for retrieving the value of the curve from animation.

param.name: curve
param.doc: Curve to add to the clip.

method.doc: Removes an existing curve from the clip.
Removes an existing curve from the clip.
Removes an existing curve from the clip.
Removes an existing curve from the clip.

property.name: Position
property.doc: Curves for animating scene object's position.

property.name: Rotation
property.doc: Curves for animating scene object's rotation.

property.name: Scale
property.doc: Curves for animating scene object's scale.

property.name: Generic
property.doc: Curves for animating generic component properties.

struct.doc: Event that is triggered when animation reaches a certain point.

ctor.doc: Constructs a new animation event.

param.name: name
param.doc: Name used to identify the event when triggered.

param.name: time
param.doc: Time at which to trigger the event, in seconds.

field.name: Name
field.doc: Name used to identify the event when triggered.

field.name: Time
field.doc: Time at which to trigger the event, in seconds.

struct.doc: Identifier for a device that can be used for playing audio.

class.doc: Contains data about a single property in a managed object (class or struct).
Contains animation curves for translation/rotation/scale of scene objects/skeleton bones, as well as curves for generic property animation.

ctor.doc: Creates an animation clip with no curves. After creation make sure to register some animation curves before using it.
Creates an animation clip with specified curves.

param.name: curves
param.doc: Curves to initialize the animation with.

param.name: isAdditive
param.doc: Determines does the clip contain additive curve data. This will change the behaviour how is the clip blended with other animations.

param.name: sampleRate
param.doc: If animation uses evenly spaced keyframes, number of samples per second. Not relevant if keyframes are unevenly spaced.

param.name: rootMotion
param.doc: Optional set of curves that can be used for animating the root bone. Not used by the animation system directly but is instead provided to the user for manual evaluation.

property.name: Curves
property.doc: A set of all curves stored in the animation. Returned value will not be updated if the animation clip curves are added or removed, as it is a copy of clip's internal values.

property.name: Events
property.doc: A set of all events to be triggered as the animation is playing.

property.name: RootMotion
property.doc: Returns a set of curves containing motion of the root bone. This allows the user to evaluate the root bone animation curves manually, instead of through the normal animation process. This property is only available if animation clip was imported with root motion import enabled.

property.name: HasRootMotion
property.doc: Checks if animation clip has root motion curves separate from the normal animation curves.

property.name: IsAddtive
property.doc: Checks are the curves contained within the clip additive. Additive clips are intended to be added on top of other clips.

property.name: Length
property.doc: Returns the length of the animation clip, in seconds.
