property.name: SampleRate
property.doc: Number of samples per second the animation clip curves were sampled at. This value is not used by the animation clip or curves directly since unevenly spaced keyframes are supported. But it can be of value when determining the original sample rate of an imported animation or similar.

enum.doc: Type of tangent on a keyframe in an animation curve.
Flags that are used for describing how are tangents calculated for a specific keyframe in an animation curve. Modes for "in" and "out" tangents can be combined.

enumentry.doc: Both tangents are calculated automatically based on the two surrounding keyframes.
Left tangent is calculated automatically based on the two surrounding keyframes.
Tangent is infinite, ensuring there is a instantaneus jump between previous and current keyframe value.
Right tangents are calculated automatically based on the two surrounding keyframes.
Left tangent is manually adjusted by the user.
Tangent is calculated automatically based on the previous keyframe.
Right tangent is manually adjusted by the user.
Tangent is calculated automatically based on the next keyframe.
Tangent is infinite, ensuring there is a instantaneus jump between current and next keyframe value.
Both tangents are manually adjusted by the user.

class.doc: A slider with a draggable handle that can be vertical or horizontal.

method.doc: Sets a minimum and maximum allow values in the input field. Set to large negative/positive values if you don't require clamping.

property.name: HandlePositionInPercent
property.doc: Current position of the slider handle, in percent ranging [0.0f, 1.0f].

property.name: HandlePositionInRange
property.doc: Current position of the slider handle, scaled within the current minimum and maximum range, rounded up to nearest step increment. If no range is provided, the range is [0, 1].

property.name: RangeMinimum
property.doc: Returns the minimum value of the slider

property.name: RangeMaximum
property.doc: Returns the maximum value of the slider

property.name: Step
property.doc: Step that defines the minimal increment the value can be increased/decreased by. Set to zero to have no step.

event.doc: Triggered when the user changes the value of the slider.

struct.doc: Represents an animation clip used in 1D blending. Each clip has a position on the number line.
Structure containing a reference to a keyframe tangent, as a keyframe reference and type of the tangent.

class.doc: Name and weight of a single shape in a morph target animation. Each shape internally represents a set of vertices that describe the morph shape.

property.name: Name
property.doc: Returns the name of the shape.

property.name: Weight
property.doc: Returns the weight of the shape, determining how are different shapes within a channel blended.

class.doc: Provides global functionality relating to sounds and music.

property.name: Volume
property.doc: Determines global audio volume. In range [0, 1].

property.name: Paused
property.doc: Determines if audio reproduction is paused globally.

property.name: ActiveDevice
property.doc: Determines the device on which is the audio played back on.

property.name: DefaultDevice
property.doc: Returns the default audio device identifier.

property.name: AllDevices
property.doc: Returns a list of all available audio devices.

enum.doc: Valid internal engine audio formats.

enumentry.doc: Pulse code modulation audio ("raw" uncompressed audio).
Vorbis compressed audio.

enum.doc: Modes that determine how and when is audio data read.

enumentry.doc: Entire audio clip will be loaded and decompressed. Uses most memory but has lowest CPU impact.
Entire audio clip will be loaded, but will be decompressed while playing. Uses medium amount of memory and has the highest CPU impact.

Audio will be slowly streamed from the disk, and decompressed if needed. Uses very little memory, and has either low or high CPU impact depending if the audio is in a compressed format. Since data is streamed from the disk, read speeds could also be a bottleneck.

class.doc: Audio clip stores audio data in a compressed or uncompressed format. Clips can be provided to audio sources or other audio methods to be played.

property.name: BitDepth
property.doc: Returns the size of a single sample, in bits.

property.name: SampleRate
property.doc: Returns how many samples per second is the audio encoded in.

property.name: NumChannels
property.doc: Returns the number of channels provided by the clip.

property.name: Format
property.doc: Returns in which format is audio data stored in.

property.name: ReadMode
property.doc: Returns how is the audio data read/decoded.

property.name: Duration
property.doc: Returns the length of the audio clip, in seconds.

property.name: NumSamples
property.doc: Returns the total number of samples in the clip (includes all channels).

property.name: Is3D
property.doc: Determines will the clip be played a spatial 3D sound, or as a normal sound (for example music).

class.doc: Base class for creating import options from. Import options are specific for each importer and control how is data imported.

enum.doc: Possible modifiers that can be applied to a SceneObject.

enumentry.doc: Object will be skipped when saving the scene hierarchy or a prefab.
Object will remain in the scene even after scene clear, unless destroyed directly. This only works with top-level objects. Runtime persistent objects cannot be saved.
Provides a hint to external systems that his object is used by engine internals. For example, those systems might not want to display those objects together with the user created ones.

class.doc: Represents a GUI element that can be interacted with. All interactable elements are also renderable (i.e. have a visual component).

method.doc: Change the GUI element focus state.

param.name: enabled
param.doc: Give the element focus or take it away.

param.name: clear
param.doc: If true the focus will be cleared from any elements currently in focus. Otherwise the element will just be appended to the in-focus list (if enabling focus).

property.name: OptionFlags
property.doc: A set of flags controlling various aspects of the GUIElement. See GUIElementOptions.

property.name: ContextMenu
property.doc: Assigns a new context menu that will be opened when the element is right clicked. Null is allowed in case no context menu is wanted.

event.doc: Triggered when the element gains focus.
Triggered when the element loses focus.

class.doc: A horizontal slider with a draggable handle.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: styleClass
param.doc: Style class that will be used for determining GUI element visuals from the current style sheet. If no class is provided, default style is determined based on GUI element type.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

class.doc: Holds references to built-in resources used by the core engine.

method.doc: Returns one of the builtin shader types.
Retrieves one of the builtin meshes.
Loads a shader at the specified path.

param.name: path
param.doc: Path relative to the default shader folder with no file extension.

method.doc: Attempts to return a font of the given font family. Returns the default font is provided font is not found.
Loads the shader with the specified name from the cache if available, or compiles the shader from source if not available.

param.name: path
param.doc: Absolute path to the shader source file.

returns.doc: Valid shader if successful, or null otherwise.

method.doc: Retrieves one of the builtin textures.

property.name: WhiteSpriteTexture
property.doc: Returns a small entirely white texture.

property.name: DefaultFont
property.doc: Returns the default font used by the engine.

struct.doc: Information describing a ParticleEmitterSkinnedMeshShape.

field.name: Type
field.doc: Determines from which portion of the mesh are the particles emitted from.

field.name: Sequential
field.doc: When enabled the particles will be emitted sequentially from mesh vertices in the order they are defined. Only relevant for the Vertex emit mode.

field.name: Renderable
field.doc: Renderable object containing a mesh to spawn particles on, as well as the attached Animation object resposible for performing skinned animation. Mesh must at least contain per-vertex position data encoded as 3D float vectors, blend indices encoded in 4-byte format, and blend weights encoded a 4D float vectors. Can optionally contain per-vertex normals encoded as 3D float vectors or as 4-byte unsigned-normalized format.

class.doc: Settings that control temporal anti-aliasing.

property.name: Enabled
property.doc: Enables or disables temporal anti-aliasing.

property.name: JitteredPositionCount
property.doc: Number of different jittered positions to use. Each frame will use one position and subsequent frames will use subsequent positions until this number of reached, at which point the positions start getting re-used from the start.

property.name: Sharpness
property.doc: Determines the distance between temporal AA samples. Larger values result in a sharper image.

class.doc: Contains import options you may use to control how an audio clip is imported.

ctor.doc: Creates a new import options object that allows you to customize how are audio clips imported.

property.name: Format
property.doc: Audio format to import the audio clip as.

property.name: ReadMode
property.doc: Determines how is audio data loaded into memory.

property.name: Is3D
property.doc: Determines should the clip be played as spatial (3D) audio or as normal audio. 3D clips will be converted to mono on import.

property.name: BitDepth
property.doc: Size of a single sample in bits. The clip will be converted to this bit depth on import.

enum.doc: Valid serializable script types.
Flags that control hinge joint options.

enumentry.doc: Joint limit is enabled.
Joint drive is enabled.

enum.doc: Flags that control options of a Rigidbody object.

enumentry.doc: No options.
Automatically calculate center of mass transform and inertia tensors from child shapes (colliders).
Calculate mass distribution from child shapes (colliders). Only relevant when auto-tensors is on.
Enables continous collision detection. This can prevent fast moving bodies from tunneling through each other. This must also be enabled globally in Physics otherwise the flag will be ignored.

enum.doc: Determines how an animation clip behaves when it reaches the end.

enumentry.doc: Loop around to the beginning/end when the last/first frame is reached.
Clamp to end/beginning, keeping the last/first frame active.

struct.doc: Contains information about a currently playing animation clip.

field.name: Layer
field.doc: Layer the clip is playing on. Multiple clips can be played simulatenously on different layers.

field.name: Time
field.doc: Current time the animation is playing from.

field.name: Speed
field.doc: Speed at which the animation is playing.

field.name: Weight
field.doc: Determines how much of an influence does the clip have on the final pose.

field.name: WrapMode
field.doc: Determines what happens to other animation clips when a new clip starts playing.

field.name: Stopped
field.doc: Determines should the time be advanced automatically. Certain type of animation clips don't involve playback (e.g. for blending where animation weight controls the animation).

struct.doc: Defines a 1D blend where multiple animation clips are blended between each other using linear interpolation.
Defines a 2D blend where two animation clips are blended between each other using bilinear interpolation.

class.doc: Handles animation playback. Takes one or multiple animation clips as input and evaluates them every animation update tick depending on set properties. The evaluated data is used by the render thread for skeletal animation, by the main thread for updating attached scene objects and bones (if skeleton is attached), or the data is made available for manual queries in the case of generic animation.

method.doc: Plays the specified animation clip.

Plays the specified animation clip on top of the animation currently playing in the main layer. Multiple such clips can be playing at once, as long as you ensure each is given its own layer. Each animation can also have a weight that determines how much it influences the main animation.

param.name: clip
param.doc: Clip to additively blend. Must contain additive animation curves.

param.name: weight
param.doc: Determines how much of an effect will the blended animation have on the final output. In range [0, 1].

param.name: fadeLength
param.doc: Applies the blend over a specified time period, increasing the weight as the time passes. Set to zero to blend immediately. In seconds.

param.name: layer
param.doc: Layer to play the clip in. Multiple additive clips can be playing at once in separate layers and each layer has its own weight.

method.doc: Blend multiple animation clips between each other using linear interpolation. Unlike normal animations these animations are not advanced with the progress of time, and is instead expected the user manually changes the parameter.

param.name: info
param.doc: Information about the clips to blend. Clip positions must be sorted from lowest to highest.

param.name: alpha
param.doc: Parameter that controls the blending. Range depends on the positions of the provided animation clips.

method.doc: Blend four animation clips between each other using bilinear interpolation. Unlike normal animations these animations are not advanced with the progress of time, and is instead expected the user manually changes the parameter.

param.name: info
param.doc: Information about the clips to blend.

param.name: alpha
param.doc: Parameter that controls the blending, in range [(0, 0), (1, 1)]. alpha = (0, 0) means top left animation has full influence, alpha = (1, 0) means top right animation has full influence, alpha = (0, 1) means bottom left animation has full influence, alpha = (1, 1) means bottom right animation has full influence.

method.doc: Fades the specified animation clip in, while fading other playing animation out, over the specified time period.

param.name: clip
param.doc: Clip to fade in.

param.name: fadeLength
param.doc: Determines the time period over which the fade occurs. In seconds.

method.doc: Samples an animation clip at the specified time, displaying only that particular frame without further playback.

param.name: clip
param.doc: Animation clip to sample.

param.name: time
param.doc: Time to sample the clip at.

method.doc: Stops playing all animations on the provided layer. Specify ~0u to stop animation on the main layer (non-additive animations).
Stops playing all animations.
Retrieves detailed information about a currently playing animation clip.

param.name: clip
param.doc: Clip to retrieve the information for.

param.name: outState
param.doc: Animation clip state containing the requested information. Only valid if the method returns true.

returns.doc: True if the state was found (animation clip is playing), false otherwise.

method.doc: Changes the state of a playing animation clip. If animation clip is not currently playing the playback is started for the clip.

param.name: clip
param.doc: Clip to change the state for.

param.name: state
param.doc: New state of the animation (e.g. changing the time for seeking).

method.doc: Changes a weight of a single morph channel, determining how much of it to apply on top of the base mesh.

param.name: name
param.doc: Name of the morph channel to modify. This depends on the mesh the animation is currently animating.

param.name: weight
param.doc: Weight that determines how much of the channel to apply to the mesh, in range [0, 1].

property.name: DefaultClip
property.doc: Determines the default clip to play as soon as the component is enabled. If more control over playing clips is needed use the Play(), Blend*(), CrossFade() methods to queue clips for playback manually, and SetState() method to modify their states individually.

property.name: WrapMode
property.doc: Determines the wrap mode for all active animations. Wrap mode determines what happens when animation reaches the first or last frame.

property.name: Speed
property.doc: Determines the speed for all animations. The default value is 1.0f. Use negative values to play-back in reverse.

property.name: IsPlaying
property.doc: Checks if any animation clips are currently playing.

property.name: CustomBounds
property.doc: Determines bounds that will be used for animation and mesh culling. Only relevant if SetUseCustomBounds() is set to true.

property.name: UseCustomBounds
property.doc: Determines should animation bounds be used for visibility determination (culling). If false the bounds of the mesh attached to the relevant Renderable component will be used instead.

property.name: Cull
property.doc: Enables or disables culling of the animation when out of view. Culled animation will not be evaluated.

class.doc: Shader specific resource meta-data containing information about referenced include files.

Represents a listener that hears audio sources. For spatial audio the volume and pitch of played audio is determined by the distance, orientation and velocity differences between the source and the listener.

enum.doc: Flags that are used to further define a field in a managed serializable object.

enumentry.doc: Integer or floating point field with min/max range.
Field will be automatically serialized.
Field containing a reference type that should never be null.
Field will be visible in the default inspector.
Integer or floating point field with a minimum increment/decrement step.
Field can be animated through the animation window.
Integer field rendered as a layer selection dropdown.
Signifies that the field containing a class/struct should display the child fields of that objects as if they were part of the parent class in the inspector.
Field containing a reference type being passed by copy instead of by reference.
Field represents a property that wraps a native object. Getters and setters of such a property issue calls into native code to update the native object.
When a field changes those changes need to be applied to the parent object by calling the field setter. Only applicable to properties containing reference types.
Signifies that a resource reference should be loaded when assigned to field through the inspector.

When a quaternion is displayed in the inspector, by default it will be displayed as converted into euler angles. Use this flag to force it to be displayed as a quaternion (4D value) with no conversion instead.
Fields contains information about a category, which is used for grouping fields under a foldout in the inspector. Retrieve the category field style for information about the category.
Field contains information about its order relative to other fields. Retrieve the order field style for information about the order.
Field containing a color that supports high dynamic range.

enum.doc: Valid states in which AudioSource can be in.

enumentry.doc: Source is currently playing.
Source is currently paused (play will resume from paused point).
Source is currently stopped (play will resume from start).

class.doc: Component that maps animation for specific bone also be applied to the SceneObject this component is attached to. The component will attach to the first found parent Animation component.

property.name: Name
property.doc: Determines the name of the bone the component is referencing.

struct.doc: Information about a single probe in the light probe volume.

enum.doc: Specifies first or second body referenced by a Joint.

enumentry.doc: Body the joint is influencing.
Body the joint is attached to (if any).

class.doc: GUI element that may be inserted into layouts to make a space of a flexible size. The space will expand only if there is room and other elements are not squished because of it. If multiple flexible spaces are in a layout, their sizes will be shared equally.

ctor.doc: Creates a new flexible space GUI element.

struct.doc: Information about a single contact point during physics collision.

field.name: Position
field.doc: Contact point in world space.

field.name: Normal
field.doc: Normal pointing from the second shape to the first shape.

field.name: Impulse
field.doc: Impulse applied to the objects to keep them from penetrating. Divide by simulation step to get the force.

field.name: Separation
field.doc: Determines how far are the objects. Negative value denotes penetration.

struct.doc: Contains per-vertex bone weights and indexes used for skinning, for up to four bones.
Information about a collision between two physics objects.

field.name: Collider
field.doc: Components of the colliders that have collided.

field.name: ColliderShapes
field.doc: Shapes of that have collided.

field.name: ContactPoints
field.doc: Information about all the contact points for the hit.

class.doc: Applies gravity to the particles.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle gravity evolver.
Creates a new particle gravity evolver.

property.name: Settings
property.doc: Options describing the evolver.

enum.doc: Light type that determines how is light information parsed by the renderer and other systems.
Determines which collision events will be reported by physics objects.

enumentry.doc: Collision events will be triggered when object enters and/or leaves collision.
No collision events will be triggered.
Collision events will be triggered when object enters and/or leaves collision, but also every frame the object remains in collision.

class.doc: Camera determines how is world geometry projected onto a 2D surface. You may position and orient it in space, set options like aspect ratio and field or view and it outputs view and projection matrices required for rendering.

method.doc: Requests an asynchronous capture of the next rendered frame from this camera.

returns.doc: Async operation with captured pixel data, or nullptr if camera is inactive.

method.doc: Notifies a on-demand camera that it should re-draw its contents on the next frame. Ignored for a camera that isn't on-demand.
Converts a point in world space to screen coordinates.

param.name: worldPoint
param.doc: 3D point in world space.

returns.doc: 2D point on the render target attached to the camera's viewport, in pixels.

method.doc: Converts a point in world space to normalized device coordinates.

param.name: worldPoint
param.doc: 3D point in world space.

returns.doc: 2D point in normalized device coordinates ([-1, 1] range), relative to the camera's viewport.

method.doc: Converts a point in world space to view space coordinates.

param.name: worldPoint
param.doc: 3D point in world space.

returns.doc: 3D point relative to the camera's coordinate system.

method.doc: Converts a point in screen space to a point in world space.

param.name: screenPoint
param.doc: 2D point on the render target attached to the camera's viewport, in pixels.

param.name: depth
param.doc: Depth to place the world point at, in world coordinates. The depth is applied to the vector going from camera origin to the point on the near plane.

returns.doc: 3D point in world space.

method.doc: Converts a point in screen space to a point in view space.

param.name: screenPoint
param.doc: 2D point on the render target attached to the camera's viewport, in pixels.

param.name: depth
param.doc: Depth to place the world point at, in device depth. The depth is applied to the vector going from camera origin to the point on the near plane.

returns.doc: 3D point relative to the camera's coordinate system.

method.doc: Converts a point in screen space to normalized device coordinates.

param.name: screenPoint
param.doc: 2D point on the render target attached to the camera's viewport, in pixels.

returns.doc: 2D point in normalized device coordinates ([-1, 1] range), relative to the camera's viewport.

method.doc: Converts a point in view space to world space.

param.name: viewPoint
param.doc: 3D point relative to the camera's coordinate system.

returns.doc: 3D point in world space.

method.doc: Converts a point in view space to screen space.

param.name: viewPoint
param.doc: 3D point relative to the camera's coordinate system.

returns.doc: 2D point on the render target attached to the camera's viewport, in pixels.

method.doc: Converts a point in view space to normalized device coordinates.

param.name: viewPoint
param.doc: 3D point relative to the camera's coordinate system.

returns.doc: 2D point in normalized device coordinates ([-1, 1] range), relative to the camera's viewport.

method.doc: Converts a point in normalized device coordinates to world space.

param.name: ndcPoint
param.doc: 2D point in normalized device coordinates ([-1, 1] range), relative to the camera's viewport.

param.name: depth
param.doc: Depth to place the world point at. The depth is applied to the vector going from camera origin to the point on the near plane.

returns.doc: 3D point in world space.

method.doc: Converts a point in normalized device coordinates to view space.

param.name: ndcPoint
param.doc: 2D point in normalized device coordinates ([-1, 1] range), relative to the camera's viewport.

param.name: depth
param.doc: Depth to place the world point at. The depth is applied to the vector going from camera origin to the point on the near plane.

returns.doc: 3D point relative to the camera's coordinate system.

method.doc: Converts a point in normalized device coordinates to screen space.

param.name: ndcPoint
param.doc: 2D point in normalized device coordinates ([-1, 1] range), relative to the camera's viewport.

returns.doc: 2D point on the render target attached to the camera's viewport, in pixels.

method.doc: Converts a point in screen space to a ray in world space.

param.name: screenPoint
param.doc: 2D point on the render target attached to the camera's viewport, in pixels.

returns.doc: Ray in world space, originating at the selected point on the camera near plane.

method.doc: Projects a point in view space to normalized device coordinates. Similar to ViewToNDCPoint() but preserves the depth component.

param.name: point
param.doc: 3D point relative to the camera's coordinate system.

returns.doc: 3D point in normalized device coordinates ([-1, 1] range), relative to the camera's viewport. Z value range depends on active render API.

method.doc: Un-projects a point in normalized device space to view space.

param.name: point
param.doc: 3D point in normalized device coordinates ([-1, 1] range), relative to the camera's viewport. Z value range depends on active render API.

returns.doc: 3D point relative to the camera's coordinate system.

property.name: Main
property.doc: Determines whether this is the main application camera. Main camera controls the final render surface that is displayed to the user.

property.name: Flags
property.doc: Determines flags used for controlling the camera behaviour.

property.name: FieldOfView
property.doc: Determines the camera horizontal field of view. This determines how wide the camera viewing angle is along the horizontal axis. Vertical FOV is calculated from the horizontal FOV and the aspect ratio.

property.name: NearClipPlane
property.doc: Determines the distance from the frustum to the near clipping plane. Anything closer than the near clipping plane will not be rendered. Decreasing this value decreases depth buffer precision.

property.name: FarClipPlane
property.doc: Determines the distance from the frustum to the far clipping plane. Anything farther than the far clipping plane will not be rendered. Increasing this value decreases depth buffer precision.

property.name: AspectRatio
property.doc: Determines the current viewport aspect ratio (width / height).

property.name: ProjMatrix
property.doc: Returns the standard projection matrix that determines how are 3D points projected to two dimensions. The layout of this matrix depends on currently used GPU backend.

property.name: ViewMatrix
property.doc: Gets the camera view matrix. Used for positioning/orienting the camera.

property.name: ProjectionType
property.doc: Determines the type of projection used by the camera. Projection type controls how is 3D geometry projected onto a 2D plane.

property.name: OrthographicHeight
property.doc: Determines the type of projection used by the camera. Projection type controls how is 3D geometry projected onto a 2D plane.

property.name: OrthographicWidth
property.doc: Determines the orthographic window width, for use with orthographic rendering only. The height of the window will be calculated from the aspect ratio. Value is specified in world units.

property.name: Priority
property.doc: Determines a priority that determines in which orders the cameras are rendered. This only applies to cameras rendering to the same render target. Higher value means the camera will be rendered sooner.

property.name: Layers
property.doc: Determines layer bitfield that is used when determining which object should the camera render.

property.name: SampleCount
property.doc: Determines number of samples to use when rendering to this camera. Values larger than 1 will enable MSAA rendering.

property.name: Viewport
property.doc: Returns the viewport used by the camera.

property.name: RenderSettings
property.doc: Settings that control rendering for this view. They determine how will the renderer process this view, which effects will be enabled, and what properties will those effects use.

class.doc: Settings that control the screen-space lens flare effect.

property.name: Enabled
property.doc: Enables or disables the lens flare effect.

property.name: DownsampleCount
property.doc: Determines how many times to downsample the scene texture before using it for lens flare effect. Lower values will use higher resolution texture for calculating lens flare, at the cost of lower performance. Valid range is [1, 6], default is 4.

property.name: Threshold
property.doc: Determines the minimal threshold of pixel luminance to be included in the lens flare calculations. Any pixel with luminance below this value will be ignored for the purposes of lens flare. Set to zero or negative to disable the threshold and include all pixels in the calculations.

property.name: GhostCount
property.doc: Determines the number of ghost features to appear, shown as blurred blobs of bright areas of the scene.

property.name: GhostSpacing
property.doc: Determines the distance between ghost features. Value is in normalized screen space, in range [0,1] where 1 represents the full screen length.

property.name: Brightness
property.doc: Determines the brightness of the lens flare effect. Value of 1 means the lens flare will be displayed at the same intensity as the scene it was derived from. In range [0, 1], default being 0.05.

property.name: FilterSize
property.doc: Determines the size of the filter when blurring the lens flare features. Larger values yield a blurrier image and will require more performance.

property.name: Halo
property.doc: Determines if a halo effect should be rendered as part of the lens flare.

property.name: HaloRadius
property.doc: Determines how far away from the screen center does the halo appear, in normalized screen space (range [0,1]) where 0.5 represents half screen length.

property.name: HaloThickness
property.doc: Determines the thickness of the halo ring. In normalized screen space (range [0.01,0.5]).

property.name: HaloThreshold
property.doc: Determines the minimal threshold of pixel luminance to be included for halo generation. Any pixel with luminance below this value will be ignored for the purposes of halo generation.

property.name: HaloAspectRatio
property.doc: Determines the shape of the halo. Set to value other than 1 to make the halo an oval rather than a circle.

property.name: ChromaticAberration
property.doc: Enables or disables chromatic aberration of the lens flare and halo features. Chromatic aberration separates the values of red, green and blue channels according to a user provided offset.

property.name: ChromaticAberrationOffset
property.doc: Determines the distance between pixels within which to sample different channels. The value is in UV coordinates, range [0, 1].

property.name: BicubicUpsampling
property.doc: Uses a higher quality upscaling when blending the lens flare features with scene color. Results in less blocky artifacts at a cost to performance.

struct.doc: Hit information from a physics query.

field.name: Point
field.doc: Position of the hit in world space.

field.name: Normal
field.doc: Normal to the surface that was hit.

field.name: Uv
field.doc: Barycentric coordinates of the triangle that was hit (only applicable when triangle meshes are hit).

field.name: Distance
field.doc: Distance from the query origin to the hit position.

field.name: TriangleIdx
field.doc: Index of the triangle that was hit (only applicable when triangle meshes are hit).

field.name: UnmappedTriangleIdx
field.doc: Unmapped index of the triangle that was hit (only applicable when triangle meshes are hit). It represents an index into the original MeshData used to create the PhysicsMesh associated with . In contrast, is only a valid index for the MeshData directly obtained from #collider which can differ from the original MeshData due to the internal implementation.

field.name: Collider
field.doc: Component of the collider that was hit. This may be null if the hit collider has no owner component, in which case refer to #colliderRaw.

field.name: ColliderShape
field.doc: Collider shape that was hit.

enum.doc: Supported collider shapes.

struct.doc: Contains data about a collision of a character controller and a collider.

field.name: Collider
field.doc: Component of the controller that was touched. Can be null if the controller has no component parent, in which case check #colliderRaw.

field.name: TriangleIndex
field.doc: Touched triangle index for mesh colliders.

field.name: Position
field.doc: Contact position.

field.name: Normal
field.doc: Contact normal.

field.name: MotionDir
field.doc: Direction of motion after the hit.

field.name: MotionAmount
field.doc: Magnitude of motion after the hit.

struct.doc: Information describing box collider shape defined by its extents (half-size).

field.name: Extents
field.doc: Half-size of the box (Distance from center to one side of the box).

struct.doc: Information describing a sphere collider shape defined by is radius.
Information describing a capsule collider shape defined by its radius and half-height.

class.doc: Collider with box geometry.

property.name: Extents
property.doc: Determines the extents (half size) of the geometry of the box.

property.name: Center
property.doc: Determines the position of the box shape, relative to the component's scene object.

enum.doc: Flags for controlling Camera options.

enumentry.doc: If set the camera will only render when requested by the user through Camera::notifyNeedsRedraw(). Otherwise the camera will render every frame (unless disabled).

class.doc: Collider with a capsule geometry.

property.name: Normal
property.doc: Normal vector that determines how is the capsule oriented.

property.name: Center
property.doc: Determines the position of the capsule shape, relative to the component's scene object.

property.name: HalfHeight
property.doc: Determines the half height of the capsule, from the origin to one of the hemispherical centers, along the normal vector.

property.name: Radius
property.doc: Determines the radius of the capsule.

enum.doc: Controls climbing behaviour for a capsule character controller. Normally the character controller will not automatically climb when heights are greater than the assigned step offset. However due to the shape of the capsule it might automatically climb over slightly larger heights than assigned step offsets.

enumentry.doc: Normal behaviour. Capsule character controller will be able to auto-step even above the step offset.
The system will attempt to limit auto-step to the provided step offset and no higher.

class.doc: Image that references a part of a texture by specifying an UV range. When the sprite image is rendered only the portion of the texture specified by the UV range will be rendered. Sprite images also allow you to specify sprite sheet animation by varying which portion of the UV is selected over time.

property.name: AnimationFrameSize
property.doc: Returns the size of a single animation frame in logical pixel units. If the texture has no animation this is the same as GetLogicalSize().

property.name: Animation
property.doc: Sets properties describing sprite animation. The animation splits the sprite area into a grid of sub-images which can be evaluated over time. In order to view the animation you must also enable playback through setAnimationPlayback().

property.name: AnimationPlayback
property.doc: Determines if and how should the sprite animation play.

enum.doc: Controls behaviour when a character controller reaches a slope thats larger than its slope offset.

enumentry.doc: Character will be prevented from going further, but will be allowed to move laterally.
Character will be prevented from going further, but also slide down the slope.

enum.doc: Reports in which directions is the character colliding with other objects.

enumentry.doc: Character is colliding with its sides.
Character is colliding with the ceiling.
Character is colliding with the ground.

class.doc: Applies an arbitrary force to the particles.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle force evolver.
Creates a new particle force evolver.

property.name: Settings
property.doc: Options describing the evolver.

struct.doc: Contains data about a collision of a character controller and another object.

field.name: Position
field.doc: Contact position.

field.name: Normal
field.doc: Contact normal.

field.name: MotionDir
field.doc: Direction of motion after the hit.

field.name: MotionAmount
field.doc: Magnitude of motion after the hit.

struct.doc: Contains separate GUI content images for every possible GUI element state.

class.doc: Contains mesh vertex and index data used for initializing, updating and reading mesh data from Mesh.

property.name: Data
property.doc: Returns the underlying MeshData structure.

property.name: Positions
property.doc: An array of all vertex positions. Only valid if the vertex layout contains vertex positions.

property.name: Normals
property.doc: An array of all vertex normals. Only valid if the vertex layout contains vertex normals.

property.name: Tangents
property.doc: An array of all vertex tangents. Only valid if the vertex layout contains vertex tangents.

property.name: Colors
property.doc: An array of all vertex colors. Only valid if the vertex layout contains vertex colors.

property.name: UV0
property.doc: An array of all vertex texture coordinates in the UV0 channel. Only valid if the vertex layout contains UV0 coordinates.

property.name: UV1
property.doc: An array of all vertex texture coordinates in the UV1 channel. Only valid if the vertex layout contains UV1 coordinates.

property.name: BoneWeights
property.doc: An array of all vertex bone weights. Only valid if the vertex layout contains bone weights.

property.name: Indices
property.doc: An array of all indices.

property.name: VertexCount
property.doc: Returns the number of vertices contained in the mesh.

property.name: IndexCount
property.doc: Returns the number of indices contained in the mesh.

enum.doc: Type of playback to use for an animation of a SpriteTexture.

enumentry.doc: Loop the animation but reverse playback when the end is reached.
Animate to the end of the animation then loop around.
Do not animate.
Animate once until the end of the animation is reached.

struct.doc: Contains data about a collision between two character controllers.

field.name: Controller
field.doc: Component of the controller that was touched. Can be null if the controller has no component parent, in which case check #controllerRaw.

field.name: Position
field.doc: Contact position.

field.name: Normal
field.doc: Contact normal.

field.name: MotionDir
field.doc: Direction of motion after the hit.

field.name: MotionAmount
field.doc: Magnitude of motion after the hit.

class.doc: Special physics controller meant to be used for game characters. Uses the "slide-and-collide" physics instead of of the standard physics model to handle various issues with manually moving kinematic objects. Uses a capsule to represent the character's bounds.

method.doc: Moves the controller in the specified direction by the specified amount, while interacting with surrounding geometry. Returns flags signaling where collision occurred after the movement. Does not account for gravity, you must apply it manually.

property.name: FootPosition
property.doc: Determines the position of the bottom of the controller. Position takes contact offset into account. Changing this will teleport the character to the location. Use Move() for movement that includes physics.

property.name: Radius
property.doc: Determines the radius of the controller capsule.

property.name: Height
property.doc: Determines the height between the centers of the two spheres of the controller capsule.

property.name: Up
property.doc: Determines the up direction of capsule. Determines capsule orientation.

property.name: Layer
property.doc: Determines the layer that controls what can the controller collide with.

event.doc: Triggered when the controller hits a collider.
Triggered when the controller hits another character controller.

class.doc: GUI element containing a background image and a fill image that is scaled depending on the percentage set by the caller.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: styleClass
param.doc: Style class that will be used for determining GUI element visuals from the current style sheet. If no class is provided, default style is determined based on GUI element type.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

property.name: Percent
property.doc: Determines how far is the progress bar filled, in range [0, 1].

struct.doc: Controls spring parameters for a physics joint limits. If a limit is soft (body bounces back due to restition when the limit is reached) the spring will pull the body back towards the limit using the specified parameters.

ctor.doc: Constructs a spring with no force.
Constructs a spring.

param.name: stiffness
param.doc: Spring strength. Force proportional to the position error.

param.name: damping
param.doc: Damping strength. Force propertional to the velocity error.

field.name: Stiffness
field.doc: Spring strength. Force proportional to the position error.

field.name: Damping
field.doc: Damping strength. Force propertional to the velocity error.

struct.doc: Contains common values used by all Joint limit types.

field.name: ContactDist
field.doc: Distance from the limit at which it becomes active. Allows the solver to activate earlier than the limit is reached to avoid breaking the limit.

field.name: Restitution
field.doc: Controls how do objects react when the limit is reached, values closer to zero specify non-ellastic collision, while those closer to one specify more ellastic (i.e bouncy) collision. Must be in [0, 1] range.

field.name: Spring
field.doc: Spring that controls how are the bodies pulled back towards the limit when they breach it.

class.doc: Specialization of a GUIScrollBar for horizontal scrolling, with the ability to resize the scroll bar.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: styleClass
param.doc: Style class that will be used for determining GUI element visuals from the current style sheet. If no class is provided, default style is determined based on GUI element type.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

struct.doc: Represents a joint limit between two distance values. Lower value must be less than the upper value.

ctor.doc: Constructs an empty limit.
Constructs a hard limit. Once the limit is reached the movement of the attached bodies will come to a stop.

param.name: lower
param.doc: Lower distance of the limit. Must be less than .

param.name: upper
param.doc: Upper distance of the limit. Must be more than .

param.name: contactDist
param.doc: Distance from the limit at which it becomes active. Allows the solver to activate earlier than the limit is reached to avoid breaking the limit. Specify -1 for the default.

ctor.doc: Constructs a soft limit. Once the limit is reached the bodies will bounce back according to the resitution parameter and will be pulled back towards the limit by the provided spring.

param.name: lower
param.doc: Lower distance of the limit. Must be less than .

param.name: upper
param.doc: Upper distance of the limit. Must be more than .

param.name: spring
param.doc: Spring that controls how are the bodies pulled back towards the limit when they breach it.

param.name: restitution
param.doc: Controls how do objects react when the limit is reached, values closer to zero specify non-ellastic collision, while those closer to one specify more ellastic (i.e bouncy) collision. Must be in [0, 1] range.

field.name: Lower
field.doc: Lower distance of the limit. Must be less than #upper.

field.name: Upper
field.doc: Upper distance of the limit. Must be more than #lower.

field.name: ContactDist
field.doc: Distance from the limit at which it becomes active. Allows the solver to activate earlier than the limit is reached to avoid breaking the limit.

field.name: Restitution
field.doc: Controls how do objects react when the limit is reached, values closer to zero specify non-ellastic collision, while those closer to one specify more ellastic (i.e bouncy) collision. Must be in [0, 1] range.

field.name: Spring
field.doc: Spring that controls how are the bodies pulled back towards the limit when they breach it.

class.doc: Provides various utility methods for reflection-like operations on managed objects.

method.doc: Retrieves information about the object's type. Object must be a primitive type, scene object, component, resource, resource reference type, or a custom type marked with SerializeObject attribute in order for type information to be present. Type information can also be retrieved for arrays, lists or dictionaries of the above types.
Retrieves detailed information about a serializable object. Provided object's type must be marked with a SerializeObject attribute for this information to be available.
Deduces the RTTI ID of the native object that is wrapped by the provided object type. Returns ~0u for types that do not wrap native types.
Creates a new instance of a serialized object of the provided type.
Creates a new instance of an array of the provided type and size. Size array must have an element for each rank of the array type.
Creates a new instance of a list of the provided type with the provided initial capacity.
Creates a new instance of a dictionary of the provided type.
Clones the specified object. Non-serializable types and fields are ignored in clone. A deep copy is performed on all serializable elements except for resources or game objects.

param.name: original
param.doc: Non-null reference to the object to clone. Object type must be serializable.

returns.doc: Deep copy of the original object

method.doc: Creates an uninitialized object of the specified type.

param.name: type
param.doc: Type of the object to create. Must be serializable

returns.doc: New instance of the specified type, or null if the type is not serializable.

struct.doc: Represents a joint limit between zero a single distance value.

ctor.doc: Constructs an empty limit.
Constructs a hard limit. Once the limit is reached the movement of the attached bodies will come to a stop.

param.name: extent
param.doc: Distance at which the limit becomes active.

param.name: contactDist
param.doc: Distance from the limit at which it becomes active. Allows the solver to activate earlier than the limit is reached to avoid breaking the limit. Specify -1 for the default.

ctor.doc: Constructs a soft limit. Once the limit is reached the bodies will bounce back according to the resitution parameter and will be pulled back towards the limit by the provided spring.

param.name: extent
param.doc: Distance at which the limit becomes active.

param.name: spring
param.doc: Spring that controls how are the bodies pulled back towards the limit when they breach it.

param.name: restitution
param.doc: Controls how do objects react when the limit is reached, values closer to zero specify non-ellastic collision, while those closer to one specify more ellastic (i.e bouncy) collision. Must be in [0, 1] range.

field.name: Extent
field.doc: Distance at which the limit becomes active.

field.name: ContactDist
field.doc: Distance from the limit at which it becomes active. Allows the solver to activate earlier than the limit is reached to avoid breaking the limit.

field.name: Restitution
field.doc: Controls how do objects react when the limit is reached, values closer to zero specify non-ellastic collision, while those closer to one specify more ellastic (i.e bouncy) collision. Must be in [0, 1] range.

field.name: Spring
field.doc: Spring that controls how are the bodies pulled back towards the limit when they breach it.

struct.doc: Represents a joint limit between two angles.

ctor.doc: Constructs an empty limit.
Constructs a hard limit. Once the limit is reached the movement of the attached bodies will come to a stop.

param.name: lower
param.doc: Lower angle of the limit. Must be less than .

param.name: upper
param.doc: Upper angle of the limit. Must be more than .

param.name: contactDist
param.doc: Distance from the limit at which it becomes active. Allows the solver to activate earlier than the limit is reached to avoid breaking the limit. Specify -1 for the default.

ctor.doc: Constructs a soft limit. Once the limit is reached the bodies will bounce back according to the resitution parameter and will be pulled back towards the limit by the provided spring.

param.name: lower
param.doc: Lower angle of the limit. Must be less than .

param.name: upper
param.doc: Upper angle of the limit. Must be more than .

param.name: spring
param.doc: Spring that controls how are the bodies pulled back towards the limit when they breach it.

param.name: restitution
param.doc: Controls how do objects react when the limit is reached, values closer to zero specify non-ellastic collision, while those closer to one specify more ellastic (i.e bouncy) collision. Must be in [0, 1] range.

field.name: Lower
field.doc: Lower angle of the limit. Must be less than #upper.

field.name: Upper
field.doc: Upper angle of the limit. Must be less than #lower.

field.name: ContactDist
field.doc: Distance from the limit at which it becomes active. Allows the solver to activate earlier than the limit is reached to avoid breaking the limit.

field.name: Restitution
field.doc: Controls how do objects react when the limit is reached, values closer to zero specify non-ellastic collision, while those closer to one specify more ellastic (i.e bouncy) collision. Must be in [0, 1] range.

field.name: Spring
field.doc: Spring that controls how are the bodies pulled back towards the limit when they breach it.

class.doc: Base class for all Joint types. Joints constrain how two rigidbodies move relative to one another (for example a door hinge). One of the bodies in the joint must always be movable (non-kinematic).

method.doc: Determines a body managed by the joint. One of the bodies must be movable (non-kinematic).
Returns the position at which the body is anchored to the joint, relative to the body.
Returns the rotation at which the body is anchored to the joint, relative to the body.
Sets the position and rotation at which the body is anchored to the joint, relative to the body.

property.name: BreakForce
property.doc: Determines the maximum force the joint can apply before breaking. Broken joints no longer participate in physics simulation.

property.name: BreakTorque
property.doc: Determines the maximum torque the joint can apply before breaking. Broken joints no longer participate in physics simulation.

property.name: EnableCollision
property.doc: Determines whether collision between the two bodies managed by the joint are enabled.

event.doc: Triggered when the joint's break force or torque is exceeded.

enum.doc: Specifies axes that the D6 joint can constrain motion on.

enumentry.doc: Movement on the X axis.
Movement on the Y axis.
Movement on the Z axis.
Rotation around the Y axis.
Rotation around the X axis.
Rotation around the Z axis.

class.doc: Particle emitter shape that emits particles from a circle. Using the thickness parameter you can control whether to emit only from circle edge, the entire surface or just a part of the surface. Using the arc parameter you can emit from a specific angular portion of the circle.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle emitter circle shape.
Creates a new particle emitter circle shape.

property.name: Settings
property.doc: Options describing the shape.

enum.doc: Specifies type of constraint placed on a specific axis.

enumentry.doc: Axis will not be constrained.
Axis is immovable.
Axis will be constrained by the specified limits.

class.doc: Primary module used for dealing with input. Allows you to receieve and query raw or OS input for mouse/keyboard/gamepad.

method.doc: Returns value of the specified input axis. Normally in range [-1.0, 1.0] but can be outside the range for devices with unbound axes (for example mouse).

param.name: type
param.doc: Type of axis to query. Usually a type from InputAxis but can be a custom value.

param.name: deviceIndex
param.doc: Index of the device in case more than one is hooked up (0 - primary).

method.doc: Query if the provided button is currently being held (this frame or previous frames).

param.name: keyCode
param.doc: Code of the button to query.

param.name: deviceIndex
param.doc: Device to query the button on (0 - primary).

method.doc: Query if the provided button is currently being released (only true for one frame).

param.name: keyCode
param.doc: Code of the button to query.

param.name: deviceIndex
param.doc: Device to query the button on (0 - primary).

method.doc: Query if the provided button is currently being pressed (only true for one frame).

param.name: keyCode
param.doc: Code of the button to query.

param.name: deviceIndex
param.doc: Device to query the button on (0 - primary).

method.doc: Query if the provided pointer button is currently being held (this frame or previous frames).

param.name: pointerButton
param.doc: Code of the button to query.

method.doc: Query if the provided pointer button is currently being released (only true for one frame).

param.name: pointerButton
param.doc: Code of the button to query.

method.doc: Query if the provided pointer button is currently being pressed (only true for one frame).

param.name: pointerButton
param.doc: Code of the button to query.

method.doc: Query has the left pointer button has been double-clicked this frame.
Enables or disables mouse smoothing. Smoothing makes the changes to mouse axes more gradual.

property.name: PointerPosition
property.doc: Returns position of the pointer (for example mouse cursor) relative to the screen.

property.name: PointerDelta
property.doc: Returns difference between pointer position between current and last frame.

event.doc: Triggered whenever a button is first pressed.
Triggered whenever a button is first released.
Triggered whenever user inputs a text character.
Triggers when some pointing device (mouse cursor, touch) moves.
Triggers when some pointing device (mouse cursor, touch) button is pressed.
Triggers when some pointing device (mouse cursor, touch) button is released.
Triggers when some pointing device (mouse cursor, touch) button is double clicked.

enum.doc: Type of drives that can be used for moving or rotating bodies attached to the joint.

enumentry.doc: Rotation using spherical linear interpolation. Uses the SLERP angular drive mode which performs rotation by interpolating the quaternion values directly over the shortest path (applies to all three axes, which they all must be unlocked).
Linear movement on the X axis using the linear drive model.

Rotation using spherical linear interpolation. Uses the SLERP angular drive mode which performs rotation by interpolating the quaternion values directly over the shortest path (applies to all three axes, which they all must be unlocked).
Linear movement on the Y axis using the linear drive model.
Linear movement on the Z axis using the linear drive model.
Rotation around the Y axis using the twist/swing angular drive model. Should not be used together with SLERP mode.
Rotation around the Z axis using the twist/swing angular drive model. Should not be used together with SLERP mode.

struct.doc: Specifies parameters for a drive that will attempt to move the joint bodies to the specified drive position and velocity.

field.name: Stiffness
field.doc: Spring strength. Force proportional to the position error.

field.name: Damping
field.doc: Damping strength. Force propertional to the velocity error.

field.name: ForceLimit
field.doc: Maximum force the drive can apply.

field.name: Acceleration
field.doc: If true the drive will generate acceleration instead of forces. Acceleration drives are easier to tune as they account for the masses of the actors to which the joint is attached.

class.doc: Represents the most customizable type of joint. This joint type can be used to create all other built-in joint types, and to design your own custom ones, but is less intuitive to use. Allows a specification of a linear constraint (for example for slider), twist constraint (rotating around X) and swing constraint (rotating around Y and Z). It also allows you to constrain limits to only specific axes or completely lock specific axes.

method.doc: Allows you to constrain motion of the specified axis. Be aware that when setting drives for a specific axis you must also take care not to constrain its motion in a conflicting way (for example you cannot add a drive that moves the joint on X axis, and then lock the X axis). Unlocking translations degrees of freedom allows the bodies to move along the subset of the unlocked axes. (for example unlocking just one translational axis is the equivalent of a slider joint.) Angular degrees of freedom are partitioned as twist (around X axis) and swing (around Y and Z axes). Different effects can be achieves by unlocking their various combinations: - If a single degree of angular freedom is unlocked it should be the twist degree as it has extra options for that case (for example for a hinge joint). - If both swing degrees are unlocked but twist is locked the result is a zero-twist joint. - If one swing and one twist degree of freedom are unlocked the result is a zero-swing joint (for example an arm attached at the elbow) - If all angular degrees of freedom are unlocked the result is the same as the spherical joint.
Returns motion constraint for the specified axis.
Determines a drive that will attempt to move the specified degree(s) of freedom to the wanted position and velocity.
Sets the drive's target position and rotation relative to the joint's first body.
Sets the drive's target linear and angular velocities.

property.name: Twist
property.doc: Returns the current rotation of the joint around the X axis.

property.name: SwingY
property.doc: Returns the current rotation of the joint around the Y axis.

property.name: SwingZ
property.doc: Returns the current rotation of the joint around the Z axis.

property.name: LimitLinear
property.doc: Determines the linear limit used for constraining translation degrees of freedom.

property.name: LimitTwist
property.doc: Determines the angular limit used for constraining the twist (rotation around X) degree of freedom.

property.name: LimitSwing
property.doc: Determines the cone limit used for constraining the swing (rotation around Y and Z) degree of freedom.

property.name: DrivePosition
property.doc: Returns the drive's target position relative to the joint's first body.

property.name: DriveRotation
property.doc: Returns the drive's target rotation relative to the joint's first body.

property.name: DriveLinearVelocity
property.doc: Returns the drive's target linear velocity.

property.name: DriveAngularVelocity
property.doc: Returns the drive's target angular velocity.

class.doc: Interface that provides editor-specific information about a scene instance.
Contains information about an instantiated scene.

method.doc: Removes all scene objects from the scene, except for persistent objects. If is true, removes even the persistent objects.
Creates a new scene object in the scene instance.

param.name: name
param.doc: Name of the scene object.

param.name: flags
param.doc: Optional flags that control object behavior. See SceneObjectFlags.

ctor.doc: Creates a new empty scene instance.
Creates a new scene instance with an existing hierarchy.

property.name: Name
property.doc: Name of the scene.

property.name: Root
property.doc: Root object of the scene.

property.name: IsActive
property.doc: Checks is the scene currently active. IF inactive the scene properties aside from the name are undefined.

property.name: Physics
property.doc: Representation of the scene used by the physics sub-system. Contains all the objects that can be physically interacted with. Exact implementation depends on the physics plugin used.

property.name: AssociatedResourceId
property.doc: Returns the ID of the resource that the scene instance is associated with (e.g. resource the scene was loaded from.).

property.name: MainCamera
property.doc: Returns the camera in the scene marked as main. Main camera controls the final render surface that is displayed to the user. If there are multiple main cameras, the first one found returned.

property.name: EditorSceneInstance
property.doc: Editor scene instance, if running from within the editor.

property.name: IsRunning
property.doc: Checks are the components currently in the Running state.

class.doc: Wraps Decal as a Component.

property.name: Size
property.doc: Width and height of the decal.

property.name: Material
property.doc: Determines the material to use when rendering the decal.

property.name: MaxDistance
property.doc: Determines the maximum distance (from its origin) at which the decal is displayed.

property.name: LayerMask
property.doc: Bitfield that allows you to mask on which objects will the decal be projected onto. Only objects with the matching layers will be projected onto. Note that decal layer mask only supports 32-bits and objects with layers in bits >= 32 will always be projected onto.

property.name: Layer
property.doc: Determines the layer that controls whether a system is considered visible in a specific camera. Layer must match camera layer bitfield in order for the camera to render the decal.

class.doc: A joint that maintains an upper or lower (or both) bound on the distance between two bodies.

method.doc: Enables or disables a flag that controls joint behaviour.
Checks whether a certain joint flag is enabled.

property.name: Distance
property.doc: Returns the current distance between the two joint bodies.

property.name: MinDistance
property.doc: Determines the minimum distance the bodies are allowed to be at, they will get no closer. You must enable min distance flag in order for this limit to be applied.

property.name: MaxDistance
property.doc: Determines the maximum distance the bodies are allowed to be at, they will get no further. You must enable max distance flag in order for this limit to be applied.

property.name: Tolerance
property.doc: Determines the error tolerance of the joint at which the joint becomes active. This value slightly extends the lower and upper limit.

property.name: Spring
property.doc: Determines a spring that controls how the joint responds when a limit is reached. You must enable the spring flag on the joint in order for this to be recognized.

class.doc: Physics joint that will maintain a fixed distance and orientation between its two attached bodies.

struct.doc: Properties of a drive that drives the joint's angular velocity towards a paricular value.

field.name: Speed
field.doc: Target speed of the joint.

field.name: ForceLimit
field.doc: Maximum torque the drive is allowed to apply .

field.name: GearRatio
field.doc: Scales the velocity of the first body, and its response to drive torque is scaled down.

field.name: FreeSpin
field.doc: If the joint is moving faster than the drive's target speed, the drive will try to break. If you don't want the breaking to happen set this to true.

class.doc: Hinge joint removes all but a single rotation degree of freedom from its two attached bodies (for example a door hinge).

method.doc: Enables or disables a flag that controls joint behaviour.
Checks is the specified option enabled.

property.name: Angle
property.doc: Returns the current angle between the two attached bodes.

property.name: Speed
property.doc: Returns the current angular speed of the joint.

property.name: Limit
property.doc: Determines the limit of the joint. Limit constrains the motion to the specified angle range. You must enable the limit flag on the joint in order for this to be recognized.

property.name: Drive
property.doc: Determines the drive properties of the joint. It drives the joint's angular velocity towards a particular value. You must enable the drive flag on the joint in order for the drive to be active.

class.doc: Rigidbody is a dynamic physics object that can be moved using forces (or directly). It will interact with other static and dynamic physics objects in the scene accordingly (it will push other non-kinematic rigidbodies, and collide with static objects). The shape and mass of a rigidbody is governed by its colliders. At least one collider must be attached to the collider. To attach a collider, place it on the same scene object as the rigidbody, or a child scene object.

method.doc: Moves the rigidbody to a specific position. This method will ensure physically correct movement, meaning the body will collide with other objects along the way.
Rotates the rigidbody. This method will ensure physically correct rotation, meaning the body will collide with other objects along the way.
Forces the object to sleep. Useful if you know the object will not move in any significant way for a while.

Wakes an object up. Useful if you modified properties of this object, and potentially surrounding objects which might result in the object being moved by physics (although the physics system will automatically wake the object up for majority of such cases).
Applies a force to the center of the mass of the rigidbody. This will produce linear momentum.

param.name: force
param.doc: Force to apply.

param.name: mode
param.doc: Determines what is the type of .

method.doc: Applies a torque to the rigidbody. This will produce angular momentum.

param.name: torque
param.doc: Torque to apply.

param.name: mode
param.doc: Determines what is the type of .

method.doc: Applies a force to a specific point on the rigidbody. This will in most cases produce both linear and angular momentum.

param.name: force
param.doc: Force to apply.

param.name: position
param.doc: World position to apply the force at.

param.name: mode
param.doc: Determines what is the type of .

method.doc: Returns the total (linear + angular) velocity at a specific point.

param.name: point
param.doc: Point in world space.

returns.doc: Total velocity of the point.

property.name: Mass
property.doc: Determines the mass of the object and all of its collider shapes. Only relevant if RigidbodyFlag::AutoMass or RigidbodyFlag::AutoTensors is turned off. Value of zero means the object is immovable (but can be rotated).

property.name: IsKinematic
property.doc: Determines if the body is kinematic. Kinematic body will not move in response to external forces (for example gravity, or another object pushing it), essentially behaving like collider. Unlike a collider though, you can still move the object and have other dynamic objects respond correctly (meaning it will push other objects).

property.name: IsSleeping
property.doc: Checks if the body is sleeping. Objects that aren't moved/rotated for a while are put to sleep to reduce load on the physics system.

property.name: SleepThreshold
property.doc: Determines a threshold of force and torque under which the object will be considered to be put to sleep.

property.name: UseGravity
property.doc: Determines whether or not the rigidbody will have the global gravity force applied to it.

property.name: Velocity
property.doc: Determines the linear velocity of the body.

property.name: AngularVelocity
property.doc: Determines the angular velocity of the body.

property.name: Drag
property.doc: Determines the linear drag of the body. Higher drag values means the object resists linear movement more.

property.name: AngularDrag
property.doc: Determines the angular drag of the body. Higher drag values means the object resists angular movement more.

property.name: InertiaTensor
property.doc: Determines the inertia tensor in local mass space. Inertia tensor determines how difficult is to rotate the object. Values of zero in the inertia tensor mean the object will be unable to rotate around a specific axis. Only relevant if RigidbodyFlag::AutoTensors is turned off.

property.name: MaxAngularVelocity
property.doc: Determines the maximum angular velocity of the rigidbody. Velocity will be clamped to this value.

property.name: CenterOfMassPosition
property.doc: Determines the rigidbody's center of mass position. Only relevant if RigibodyFlag::AutoTensors is turned off.

property.name: CenterOfMassRotation
property.doc: Determines the rigidbody's center of mass rotation. Only relevant if RigibodyFlag::AutoTensors is turned off.

property.name: PositionSolverCount
property.doc: Determines the number of iterations to use when solving for position. Higher values can improve precision and numerical stability of the simulation.

property.name: VelocitySolverCount
property.doc: Determines the number of iterations to use when solving for velocity. Higher values can improve precision and numerical stability of the simulation.

property.name: CollisionReportMode
property.doc: Sets a value that determines which (if any) collision events are reported.

property.name: Flags
property.doc: Flags that control the behaviour of the rigidbody.

event.doc: Triggered when one of the colliders owned by the rigidbody starts colliding with another object.
Triggered when a previously colliding collider stays in collision. Triggered once per frame.
Triggered when one of the colliders owned by the rigidbody stops colliding with another object.

enum.doc: Determines the emission type for the cone particle emitter shape.

enumentry.doc: Particles will be emitted from the entire volume.
Particles will be emitted only from box edge.
Particles will be emitted only from box surface.

class.doc: A collider represented by an arbitrary mesh.

property.name: Mesh
property.doc: Determines a mesh that represents the collider geometry. This can be a generic triangle mesh, or and convex mesh. Triangle meshes are not supported as triggers, nor are they supported for colliders that are parts of a non-kinematic rigidbody.

class.doc: Contains definitions of GPU programs used for rendering, as well as a set of global parameters to control those programs.

property.name: VariationParams
property.doc: Returns the list of all variation parameters supported by this shader, possible values of each parameter and other meta-data.

property.name: Parameters
property.doc: Returns information about all parameters available in the shader.

struct.doc: Structure describing contents of a GUIPanel element.

field.name: Depth
field.doc: Determines rendering order of the GUI panel. Panels with lower depth will be rendered in front of panels with higher depth. Provided depth is relative to depth of the parent GUI panel (if any).

field.name: DepthRangeMinimum
field.doc: Minimum range of depths that children of this GUI panel can have. If any panel has depth outside of the range [Depth - DepthRangeMinimum, Depth + DepthRangeMaximum] it will be clamped to nearest extreme. Value of 65535 means infinite range.

field.name: DepthRangeMaximum
field.doc: Maximum range of depths that children of this GUI panel can have. If any panel has depth outside of the range [Depth - DepthRangeMinimum, Depth + DepthRangeMaximum] it will be clamped to nearest extreme. Value of 65535 means infinite range.

class.doc: Contains information about font characters rendered into one or multiple bitmaps, for specific font size.

method.doc: Returns a character description for the character with the specified Unicode key.

property.name: Size
property.doc: Font size for which the bitmaps are rendered.

property.name: BaselineOffset
property.doc: Y offset to the baseline on which the characters are placed, in pixels.

property.name: LineHeight
property.doc: Height of a single line of the font, in pixels.

property.name: MissingGlyph
property.doc: Character to use when data for a character is missing.

property.name: SpaceWidth
property.doc: Width of a space in pixels.

property.name: Type
property.doc: Determines the type of the light.

property.name: CastsShadow
property.doc: Determines does this light cast shadows when rendered.

property.name: ShadowBias
property.doc: Shadow bias determines offset at which the shadows are rendered from the shadow caster. Bias value of 0 means the shadow will be renderered exactly at the casters position. If your geometry has thin areas this will produce an artifact called shadow acne, in which case you can increase the shadow bias value to eliminate it. Note that increasing the shadow bias will on the other hand make the shadow be offset from the caster and may make the caster appear as if floating (Peter Panning artifact). Neither is perfect, so it is preferable to ensure all your geometry has thickness and keep the bias at zero, or even at negative values. Default value is 0.5. Should be in rough range [-1, 1].

property.name: Color
property.doc: Determines the color emitted by the light.

property.name: AttenuationRadius
property.doc: Range at which the light contribution fades out to zero. Use SetUseAutoAttenuation to provide a radius automatically dependant on light intensity. The radius will cut-off light contribution and therefore manually set very small radius can end up being very physically incorrect.

property.name: SourceRadius
property.doc: Radius of the light source. If non-zero then this light represents an area light, otherwise it is a punctual light. Area lights have different attenuation then punctual lights, and their appearance in specular reflections is realistic. Shape of the area light depends on light type: - For directional light the shape is a disc projected on the hemisphere on the sky. This parameter represents angular radius (in degrees) of the disk and should be very small (think of how much space the Sun takes on the sky - roughly 0.25 degree radius). - For radial light the shape is a sphere and the source radius is the radius of the sphere. - For spot lights the shape is a disc oriented in the direction of the spot light and the source radius is the radius of the disc.

property.name: UseAutoAttenuation
property.doc: If enabled the attenuation radius will automatically be controlled in order to provide reasonable light radius, depending on its intensity.

property.name: Intensity
property.doc: Determines the power of the light source. This will be luminous flux for radial & spot lights, luminance for directional lights with no area, and illuminance for directional lights with area (non-zero source radius).

property.name: SpotAngle
property.doc: Determines the total angle covered by a spot light.

property.name: SpotAngleFalloff
property.doc: Determines the falloff angle covered by a spot light. Falloff angle determines at what point does light intensity starts quadratically falling off as the angle approaches the total spot angle.

property.name: Bounds
property.doc: Returns world space bounds that completely encompass the light's area of influence.

class.doc: Contains import options you may use to control how is a shader imported.

method.doc: Sets a define and its value. Replaces an existing define if one already exists with the provided name.

param.name: define
param.doc: Name of the define.

param.name: value
param.doc: Value to assign to the define.

method.doc: Checks if the define exists and returns its value if it does.

param.name: define
param.doc: Name of the define to get the value for.

param.name: outValue
param.doc: Value of the define. Only defined if the method returns true.

returns.doc: True if the define was found, false otherwise.

method.doc: Checks if the provided define exists.

param.name: define
param.doc: Name of the define to check.

returns.doc: True if the define was found, false otherwise.

method.doc: Unregisters a previously set define.

param.name: define
param.doc: Name of the define to unregister.

ctor.doc: Creates a new import options object that allows you to customize how are meshes imported.

property.name: Languages
property.doc: Low-level shading language identifiers (for example "hlsl", "vksl") the BSL shader should be converted into. This ultimately controls on which render backends it will be able to run on.

class.doc: Settings that control color grading post-process.

property.name: Saturation
property.doc: Saturation to be applied during color grading. Larger values increase vibrancy of the image. In range [0.0f, 2.0f].

property.name: Contrast
property.doc: Contrast to be applied during color grading. Larger values increase difference between light and dark areas of the image. In range [0.0f, 2.0f].

property.name: Gain
property.doc: Gain to be applied during color grading. Simply increases all color values by an equal scale. In range [0.0f, 2.0f].

property.name: Offset
property.doc: Gain to be applied during color grading. Simply offsets all color values by an equal amount. In range [-1.0f, 1.0f].

class.doc: Allows you to define a volume of light probes that will be used for indirect lighting. Lighting information in the scene will be interpolated from nearby probes to calculate the amount of indirect lighting at that position. It is up to the caller to place the light probes in areas where the lighting changes in order to yield the best results. The volume can never have less than 4 probes.

method.doc: Adds a new probe at the specified position and returns a handle to the probe. The position is relative to the volume origin.
