Removes the probe with the specified handle. Note that if this is one of the last four remaining probes in the volume it cannot be removed.
Updates the position of the probe with the specified handle.
Retrieves the position of the probe with the specified handle.
Returns a list of all light probes in the volume.
Causes the information for this specific light probe to be updated. You generally want to call this when the probe is moved or the scene around the probe changes.
Causes the information for all lights probes to be updated. You generally want to call this if you move the entire light volume or the scene around the volume changes.

Resizes the light probe grid and inserts new light probes, if the new size is larger than previous size. New probes are inserted in a grid pattern matching the new size and density parameters. Note that shrinking the volume will not remove light probes. In order to remove probes outside of the new volume call Clip(). Resize will not change the positions of current light probes. If you wish to reset all probes to the currently set grid position, call Reset().

param.name: volume
param.doc: Axis aligned volume to be covered by the light probes.

param.name: cellCount
param.doc: Number of grid cells to split the volume into. Minimum number of 1, in which case each corner of the volume is represented by a single probe. Higher values subdivide the volume in an uniform way.

method.doc: Removes any probes outside of the current grid volume.
Resets all probes to match the original grid pattern. This will reset probe positions, as well as add/remove probes as necessary, essentially losing any custom changes to the probes.

Resizes the light probe grid and inserts new light probes, if the new size is larger than previous size. New probes are inserted in a grid pattern matching the new size and density parameters. Note that shrinking the volume will not remove light probes. In order to remove probes outside of the new volume call Clip(). Resize will not change the positions of current light probes. If you wish to reset all probes to the currently set grid position, call Reset().

param.name: volume
param.doc: Axis aligned volume to be covered by the light probes.

property.name: GridVolume
property.doc: Returns the volume that's used for adding probes in a uniform grid pattern.

property.name: CellCount
property.doc: Returns the cell count that's used for determining the density of probes within a grid volume.

class.doc: Specifies a color as a distribution, which can include a constant color, random color range or a color gradient.

ctor.doc: Creates a new empty distribution.
Creates a new distribution that returns a constant color.
Creates a new distribution that returns a random color in the specified range.
Creates a new distribution that evaluates a color gradient.
Creates a new distribution that returns a random color in a range determined by two gradients.

method.doc: Returns the constant value of the distribution, or the minimal value of a constant range. Undefined if the distribution is represented by a gradient.
Returns the maximum value of a constant range. Only defined if the distribution represents a non-gradient range.
Returns the gradient representing the distribution, or the first gradient representing a gradient range. Undefined if the distribution is represented by a constant or a non-gradient range.
Returns the curve representing the second gradient of a gradient range. Only defined if the distribution represents a gradient range.

property.name: DistributionType
property.doc: Returns the type of the represented distribution.

class.doc: Specifies a color as a distribution, which can include a constant color, random color range or a color gradient.

ctor.doc: Creates a new empty distribution.
Creates a new distribution that returns a constant color.
Creates a new distribution that returns a random color in the specified range.
Creates a new distribution that evaluates a color gradient.
Creates a new distribution that returns a random color in a range determined by two gradients.

method.doc: Returns the constant value of the distribution, or the minimal value of a constant range. Undefined if the distribution is represented by a gradient.
Returns the maximum value of a constant range. Only defined if the distribution represents a non-gradient range.
Returns the gradient representing the distribution, or the first gradient representing a gradient range. Undefined if the distribution is represented by a constant or a non-gradient range.
Returns the curve representing the second gradient of a gradient range. Only defined if the distribution represents a gradient range.

property.name: DistributionType
property.doc: Returns the type of the represented distribution.

class.doc: Specifies a value as a distribution, which can include a constant value, random range or a curve.

ctor.doc: Creates a new empty distribution.
Creates a new distribution that returns a constant value.
Creates a new distribution that returns a random value in the specified range.
Creates a new distribution that evaluates a curve.
Creates a new distribution that returns a random value in a range determined by two curves.

method.doc: Returns the constant value of the distribution, or the minimal value of a constant range. Undefined if the distribution is represented by a curve.
Returns the maximum value of a constant range. Only defined if the distribution represents a non-curve range.
Returns the curve representing the distribution, or the first curve representing a curve range. Undefined if the distribution is represented by a constant or a non-curve range.
Returns the curve representing the second curve of a curve range. Only defined if the distribution represents a curve range.
Evaluates the value of the distribution.

param.name: t
param.doc: Time at which to evaluate the distribution. This is only relevant if the distribution contains curves.

param.name: factor
param.doc: Value in range [0, 1] that determines how to interpolate between min/max value, if the distribution represents a range. Value of 0 will return the minimum value, while value of 1 will return the maximum value, and interpolate the values in-between.

returns.doc: Evaluated value.

method.doc: Evaluates the value of the distribution.

param.name: t
param.doc: Time at which to evaluate the distribution. This is only relevant if the distribution contains curves.

param.name: factor
param.doc: Random number generator that determines the factor. Factor determines how to interpolate between min/max value, if the distribution represents a range.

returns.doc: Evaluated value.

property.name: DistributionType
property.doc: Returns the type of the represented distribution.

class.doc: Specifies a value as a distribution, which can include a constant value, random range or a curve.

ctor.doc: Creates a new empty distribution.
Creates a new distribution that returns a constant value.
Creates a new distribution that returns a random value in the specified range.
Creates a new distribution that evaluates a curve.
Creates a new distribution that returns a random value in a range determined by two curves.

method.doc: Returns the constant value of the distribution, or the minimal value of a constant range. Undefined if the distribution is represented by a curve.
Returns the maximum value of a constant range. Only defined if the distribution represents a non-curve range.
Returns the curve representing the distribution, or the first curve representing a curve range. Undefined if the distribution is represented by a constant or a non-curve range.
Returns the curve representing the second curve of a curve range. Only defined if the distribution represents a curve range.
Evaluates the value of the distribution.

param.name: t
param.doc: Time at which to evaluate the distribution. This is only relevant if the distribution contains curves.

param.name: factor
param.doc: Value in range [0, 1] that determines how to interpolate between min/max value, if the distribution represents a range. Value of 0 will return the minimum value, while value of 1 will return the maximum value, and interpolate the values in-between.

returns.doc: Evaluated value.

method.doc: Evaluates the value of the distribution.

param.name: t
param.doc: Time at which to evaluate the distribution. This is only relevant if the distribution contains curves.

param.name: factor
param.doc: Random number generator that determines the factor. Factor determines how to interpolate between min/max value, if the distribution represents a range.

returns.doc: Evaluated value.

property.name: DistributionType
property.doc: Returns the type of the represented distribution.

class.doc: Specifies a value as a distribution, which can include a constant value, random range or a curve.

Creates a new distribution that returns a constant value.
Creates a new distribution that returns a random value in the specified range.
Creates a new distribution that evaluates a curve.
Creates a new distribution that returns a random value in a range determined by two curves.

Returns the maximum value of a constant range. Only defined if the distribution represents a non-curve range.
Returns the curve representing the distribution, or the first curve representing a curve range. Undefined if the distribution is represented by a constant or a non-curve range.

enum.doc: Possible orientations when rendering billboard particles.

enumentry.doc: Orient towards view (camera) plane.
Orient towards view (camera) position.
Orient with a user-provided axis.

enum.doc: Space in which to spawn/transform particles.

enumentry.doc: Particles will always remain local to their transform parent. This means if the transform parent moves so will all the particles.

Particles will be placed in world space. This means they will spawn at the location of the transform parent, but are no longer affected by its transform after spawn (e.g. smoke rising from a moving train).

enum.doc: Determines how to sort particles before rendering.

enumentry.doc: Sort by age, youngest to oldest.
Do not sort the particles.
Sort by distance from the camera, furthest to nearest.
Sort by age, oldest to youngest.

struct.doc: Rectangle represented in the form of offsets from some parent rectangle.

enum.doc: Determines how are particles represented on the screen.

enumentry.doc: Particle is represented using a 3D mesh.
Particle is represented using a 2D quad.

class.doc: Controls depth buffer collisions for GPU simulated particles.

property.name: Enabled
property.doc: Determines if depth collisions are enabled.

property.name: Restitution
property.doc: Determines the elasticity (bounciness) of the particle collision. Lower values make the collision less bouncy and higher values more.

property.name: Dampening
property.doc: Determines how much velocity should a particle lose after a collision, in percent of its current velocity. In range [0, 1].

property.name: RadiusScale
property.doc: Scale which to apply to particle size in order to determine the collision radius.

class.doc: Settings used for controlling a vector field in a GPU simulated particle system.

property.name: VectorField
property.doc: Vector field resource used for influencing the particles.

property.name: Intensity
property.doc: Intensity of the forces and velocities applied by the vector field.

property.name: Tightness
property.doc: Determines how closely does the particle velocity follow the vectors in the field. If set to 1 particles will be snapped to the exact velocity of the value in the field, and if set to 0 the field will not influence particle velocities directly.

property.name: Scale
property.doc: Scale to apply to the vector field bounds. This is multiplied with the bounds of the vector field resource.

property.name: Offset
property.doc: Amount of to move the vector field by relative to the parent particle system. This is added to the bounds provided in the vector field resource.

property.name: Rotation
property.doc: Initial rotation of the vector field.

property.name: RotationRate
property.doc: Determines the amount to rotate the vector field every second, in degrees, around XYZ axis respectively. Evaluated over the particle system lifetime.

property.name: TilingX
property.doc: Determines should the field influence particles outside of the field bounds. If true the field will be tiled infinitely in the X direction.

property.name: TilingY
property.doc: Determines should the field influence particles outside of the field bounds. If true the field will be tiled infinitely in the Y direction.

property.name: TilingZ
property.doc: Determines should the field influence particles outside of the field bounds. If true the field will be tiled infinitely in the Z direction.

class.doc: Generic settings used for controlling a ParticleSystem.

property.name: Material
property.doc: Material to render the particles with.

property.name: Mesh
property.doc: Mesh used for representing individual particles when using the Mesh rendering mode.

property.name: SimulationSpace
property.doc: Determines in which space are particles in.

property.name: Orientation
property.doc: Determines how are particles oriented when rendering.

property.name: Duration
property.doc: Determines the time period during which the system runs, in seconds. This effects evaluation of distributions with curves using particle system time for evaluation.

property.name: IsLooping
property.doc: Determines should the particle system time wrap around once it reaches its duration.

property.name: MaxParticles
property.doc: Determines the maximum number of particles that can ever be active in this system. This number is ignored if GPU simulation is enabled, and instead particle count is instead only limited by the size of the internal buffers (shared between all particle systems).

property.name: GpuSimulation
property.doc: If true the particle system will be simulated on the GPU. This allows much higher particle counts at lower performance cost. GPU simulation ignores any provided evolvers and instead uses ParticleGpuSimulationSettings to customize the GPU simulation.

property.name: RenderMode
property.doc: Determines how is each particle represented on the screen.

property.name: OrientationLockY
property.doc: Determines should the particles only be allowed to orient themselves around the Y axis, or freely. Ignored if using the Plane orientation mode.

property.name: OrientationPlaneNormal
property.doc: Determines a normal of the plane to orient particles towards. Only used if particle orientation mode is set to ParticleOrientation::Plane.

property.name: SortMode
property.doc: Determines how (and if) are particles sorted. Sorting controls in what order are particles rendered. If GPU simulation is enabled only distance based sorting is supported.

property.name: UseAutomaticSeed
property.doc: Determines should an automatic seed be used for the internal random number generator. This ensures the particle system yields different results each time it is ran.

property.name: ManualSeed
property.doc: Determines the seed to use for the internal random number generator. Allows you to guarantee identical behaviour between different runs. Only relevant if automatic seed is disabled.

property.name: UseAutomaticBounds
property.doc: Determines should the particle system bounds be automatically calculated, or should the fixed value provided be used. Bounds are used primarily for culling purposes. Note that automatic bounds are not supported when GPU simulation is enabled.

property.name: CustomBounds
property.doc: Custom bounds to use them is disabled. The bounds are in the simulation space of the particle system.

class.doc: Settings used for controlling particle system GPU simulation.

property.name: ColorOverLifetime
property.doc: Determines particle color, evaluated over the particle lifetime.

property.name: SizeScaleOverLifetime
property.doc: Determines particle size, evaluated over the particle lifetime. Multiplied by the initial particle size.

property.name: Acceleration
property.doc: Constant acceleration to apply for each step of the simulation.

property.name: Drag
property.doc: Amount of resistance to apply in the direction opposite of the particle's velocity.

property.name: DepthCollision
property.doc: Settings controlling particle depth buffer collisions.

struct.doc: Event that gets sent out when user inputs some text. These events may be preceeded by normal button events if user is typing on a keyboard.

field.name: TextChar
field.doc: Character the that was input.

field.name: IsUsed
field.doc: This will be set to true if some previous event receiver has marked the event as used.

struct.doc: Information about a Font.

field.name: Name
field.doc: Optional name of the font. Used primarily for easier debugging.

field.name: DPI
field.doc: Determines dots per inch scale that will be used when rendering the characters.

field.name: RenderMode
field.doc: Determines the render mode used for rendering the characters into a bitmap.

class.doc: Controls spawning, evolution and rendering of particles. Particles can be 2D or 3D, with a variety of rendering options. Particle system should be used for rendering objects that cannot properly be represented using static or animated meshes, like liquids, smoke or flames. The particle system requires you to specify at least one ParticleEmitter, which controls how are new particles generated. You will also want to specify one or more ParticleEvolver%s, which change particle properties over time.

method.doc: Enables or disables preview mode. Preview mode allows the particle system to play while the game is not running, primarily for preview purposes in the editor. Returns true if the preview mode was enabled, false if it was disabled or enabling preview failed.

property.name: Settings
property.doc: Determines general purpose settings that apply to the particle system.

property.name: GpuSimulationSettings
property.doc: Determines settings that control particle GPU simulation.

property.name: Emitters
property.doc: Set of objects that determine initial position, normal and other properties of newly spawned particles. Each particle system must have at least one emitter.

property.name: Evolvers
property.doc: Set of objects that determine how particle properties change during their lifetime. Evolvers only affect CPU simulated particles.

property.name: Layer
property.doc: Determines the layer bitfield that controls whether a system is considered visible in a specific camera. Layer must match camera layer in order for the camera to render the component.

class.doc: A collider with plane geometry. Plane colliders cannot be a part of non-kinematic rigidbodies.

property.name: Normal
property.doc: Normal vector that determines the local orientation of the plane.

property.name: Distance
property.doc: Determines the distance of the plane from the local origin, along its normal vector.

enum.doc: Probe type that determines the shape of the probe and how is it interpreted by the renderer.

enumentry.doc: Reflection probe cubemap is generated, and box extents are used for calculating influence ranges and box geometry.
Reflection probe cubemap is generated, but sphere is used for calculating the influence radius and proxy geometry.

class.doc: Specifies a location at which a pre-computed texture containing scene radiance will be generated. This texture will then be used by the renderer to provide specular reflections.

method.doc: Captures the scene at the current location and generates a filtered reflection cubemap. No action is taken if a custom texture is set.

property.name: Type
property.doc: Determines the type of the probe.

property.name: Radius
property.doc: Determines the radius of a sphere reflection probe.

property.name: Extents
property.doc: Determines the extents of a box reflection probe. Determines range of influence.

property.name: CustomTexture
property.doc: Allows you assign a custom texture to use as a reflection map. This will disable automatic generation of reflections. To re-enable auto-generation call this with a null parameter.

property.name: WorldRadius
property.doc: Returns the radius of a sphere reflection probe, scaled by scene object transform.

property.name: WorldExtents
property.doc: Returns the extents of a box reflection probe, scaled by scene object transform.

class.doc: Renderable represents any visible object in the scene. It has a mesh, bounds and a set of materials. Renderer will render any Renderable objects visible by a camera.

method.doc: Sets a material that will be used for rendering a sub-mesh with the specified index. If a sub-mesh doesn't have a specific material set then the primary material will be used.
Sets the primary material to use for rendering. Any sub-mesh that doesn't have an explicit material set will use this material.
Returns the material used for rendering a sub-mesh with the specified index.

property.name: Mesh
property.doc: Determines the mesh to render. All sub-meshes of the mesh will be rendered, and you may set individual materials for each sub-mesh.

property.name: Materials
property.doc: Determines all materials used for rendering this renderable. Each of the materials is used for rendering a single sub-mesh. If number of materials is larger than number of sub-meshes, they will be ignored. If lower, the remaining materials will be removed.

property.name: Layers
property.doc: Determines the layer bitfield that controls whether a renderable is considered visible in a specific camera. Renderable layer must match camera layer in order for the camera to render the component.

property.name: WriteVelocity
property.doc: If enabled this renderable will write per-pixel velocity information when rendered. This is required for effects such as temporal anti-aliasing and motion blur, but comes with a minor performance overhead. If you are not using those effects you can disable this for a performance gain.

property.name: CullDistance
property.doc: Factor to be applied to the cull distance set in the camera's render settings.

property.name: Bounds
property.doc: Gets world bounds of the mesh rendered by this object.

enum.doc: Type of force or torque that can be applied to a rigidbody.

enumentry.doc: Value applied is an impulse (a direct change in its linear or angular momentum).
Value applied is a force.
Value applied is velocity.
Value applied is accelearation.

enum.doc: Flag that controls slider joint's behaviour.

enumentry.doc: Enables the linear range limit.

class.doc: Contains information about a type of a managed Dictionary.
Joint that removes all but a single translational degree of freedom. Bodies are allowed to move along a single axis.

method.doc: Enables or disables a flag that controls the joint's behaviour.
Checks is the specified flag enabled.

property.name: Position
property.doc: Returns the current position of the slider.

property.name: Speed
property.doc: Returns the current speed of the slider.

property.name: Limit
property.doc: Determines a limit that constrains the movement of the joint to a specific minimum and maximum distance. You must enable the limit flag on the joint in order for this to be recognized.

class.doc: A GUI element that allows the user to draw custom graphics. All drawn elements relative to the canvas, to its origin in the top left corner.

method.doc: Draws a line going from to .

param.name: a
param.doc: Starting point of the line, relative to the canvas origin (top-left).

param.name: b
param.doc: Ending point of the line, relative to the canvas origin (top-left).

param.name: color
param.doc: Color of the line.

param.name: depth
param.doc: Depth at which to draw the element. Elements with higher depth will be drawn before others. Additionally elements of the same type (triangle or line) will be drawn in order they are submitted if they share the same depth.

method.doc: Draws multiple lines following the path by the provided vertices. First vertex connects to the second vertex, and every following vertex connects to the previous vertex.

param.name: vertices
param.doc: Points to use for drawing the line. Must have at least two elements. All points are relative to the canvas origin (top-left).

param.name: color
param.doc: Color of the line.

param.name: depth
param.doc: Depth at which to draw the element. Elements with higher depth will be drawn before others. Additionally elements of the same type (triangle or line) will be drawn in order they are submitted if they share the same depth.

method.doc: Draws a quad with a the provided image displayed.

param.name: image
param.doc: Image to draw.

param.name: area
param.doc: Position and size of the texture to draw. Position is relative to the canvas origin (top-left). If size is zero, the default texture size will be used.

param.name: color
param.doc: Color to tint the drawn texture with.

param.name: scaleMode
param.doc: Scale mode to use when sizing the texture. Only relevant if the provided quad size doesn't match the texture size.

param.name: depth
param.doc: Depth at which to draw the element. Elements with higher depth will be drawn before others. Additionally elements of the same type (triangle or line) will be drawn in order they are submitted if they share the same depth.

method.doc: Draws a triangle strip. First three vertices are used to form the initial triangle, and every next vertex will form a triangle with the previous two.

param.name: vertices
param.doc: A set of points defining the triangles. Must have at least three elements. All points are relative to the canvas origin (top-left).

param.name: color
param.doc: Color of the triangles.

param.name: depth
param.doc: Depth at which to draw the element. Elements with higher depth will be drawn before others. Additionally elements of the same type (triangle or line) will be drawn in order they are submitted if they share the same depth.

method.doc: Draws a triangle list. Every three vertices in the list represent a unique triangle.

param.name: vertices
param.doc: A set of points defining the triangles. Must have at least three elements, and its size must be a multiple of three.

param.name: color
param.doc: Color of the triangles.

param.name: depth
param.doc: Depth at which to draw the element. Elements with higher depth will be drawn before others. Additionally elements of the same type (triangle or line) will be drawn in order they are submitted if they share the same depth.

method.doc: Draws a piece of text with the wanted font. The text will be aligned to the top-left corner of the provided position, and will not be word wrapped.

param.name: text
param.doc: Text to draw.

param.name: position
param.doc: Position of the text to draw. This represents the top-left corner of the text. It is relative to the canvas origin (top-left).

param.name: font
param.doc: Font to draw the text with.

param.name: size
param.doc: Size of the font.

param.name: color
param.doc: Color of the text.

param.name: depth
param.doc: Depth at which to draw the element. Elements with higher depth will be drawn before others. Additionally elements of the same type (triangle or line) will be drawn in order they are submitted if they share the same depth.

method.doc: Clears the canvas, removing any previously drawn elements.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: styleClass
param.doc: Style class that will be used for determining GUI element visuals from the current style sheet. If no class is provided, default style is determined based on GUI element type.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

class.doc: A collider with sphere geometry.

property.name: Radius
property.doc: Determines the radius of the sphere geometry.

property.name: Center
property.doc: Determines position of the sphere shape, relative to the component's scene object.

enum.doc: Flags that control options for the spherical joint

enumentry.doc: Enables the cone range limit.

class.doc: A spherical joint removes all translational degrees of freedom but allows all rotational degrees of freedom. Essentially this ensures that the anchor points of the two bodies are always coincident. Bodies are allowed to rotate around the anchor points, and their rotation can be limited by an elliptical cone.

method.doc: Enables or disables a flag that controls the joint's behaviour.
Checks is the specified flag enabled.

property.name: Limit
property.doc: Determines the limit of the joint. This clamps the rotation inside an eliptical angular cone. You must enable limit flag on the joint in order for this to be recognized.

class.doc: A top level container for all types of GUI elements. Every GUI element, layout or area must be assigned to a widget in order to be rendered. Widgets are the only GUI objects that may be arbitrarily transformed, allowing you to create 3D interfaces.

method.doc: Checks are the specified coordinates within widget bounds. Coordinates should be relative to the parent window.
Returns bounds of the widget, relative to the parent window.

property.name: Panel
property.doc: Returns the root GUI panel for the widget.

property.name: Depth
property.doc: Determines the depth to render the widget at. If two widgets overlap the widget with the lower depth will be rendered in front.

class.doc: A set of animation events that will be added to an animation clip during animation import.

enum.doc: Type of filter to use when filtering samples contributing to a blurred pixel.

enumentry.doc: Samples will be simply averaged together to create the blurred pixel.

A more advanced reconstruction filter will be used. This filter provides better blur quality at a performance cost. In particular the filter will improve blurring at object boundaries, allowing blur to extend beyond the object silhouette. It will also try to estimate blurred background and provide better weighting between background, center and foreground samples.

struct.doc: Data about a sub-mesh range and the type of primitives contained in the range.

class.doc: GUI element representing a toggle (on/off) button.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: contents
param.doc: Structure describing the contents of the GUI element to create.

param.name: styleClass
param.doc: Style class that will be used for determining GUI element visuals from the current style sheet. If no class is provided, default style is determined based on GUI element type.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: contents
param.doc: Structure describing the contents of the GUI element to create.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

param.doc: Style class that will be used for determining GUI element visuals from the current style sheet. If no class is provided, default style is determined based on GUI element type.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

struct.doc: Structure used for initializing a ParticleRotation object.

field.name: Rotation
field.doc: Determines the rotation of the particles in degrees, applied around the particle's local Z axis. Only used if 3D rotation is disabled.

field.name: Rotation3D
field.doc: Determines the rotation of the particles in degrees as Euler angles. Only used if 3D rotation is enabled.

field.name: Use3DRotation
field.doc: Determines should the particle rotation be a single angle applied around a Z axis (if disabled), or a set of Euler angles that allow you to rotate around every axis (if enabled).

method.doc: Creates a deep copy of the material and returns the new object.
Assigns a float value to the shader parameter with the specified name. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index to assign the value to.
Assigns a color to the shader parameter with the specified name. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index to assign the value to.

Assigns a color gradient to the shader parameter with the specified name. The system will automatically evaluate the gradient with the passage of time and apply the evaluated value to the parameter. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index to assign the value to.
Assigns a 2D vector to the shader parameter with the specified name. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index to assign the value to.
Assigns a 3D vector to the shader parameter with the specified name. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index to assign the value to.
Assigns a 4D vector to the shader parameter with the specified name. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index to assign the value to.
Assigns a 3x3 matrix to the shader parameter with the specified name. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index to assign the value to.
Assigns a 4x4 matrix to the shader parameter with the specified name. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index to assign the value to.

Returns a float value assigned with the parameter with the specified name. If a curve is assigned to this parameter, returns the curve value evaluated at time 0. Use getBoundParamType() to determine the type of the parameter. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index you which to retrieve.

Returns a curve value assigned to the parameter with the specified name. If the parameter has a constant value bound instead of a curve then this method returns an empty curve. Use getBoundParamType() to determine the type of the parameter. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index you which to retrieve.

Returns a color assigned with the parameter with the specified name. If a color gradient is assigned to this parameter, returns the gradient color evaluated at time 0. Use getBoundParamType() to determine the type of the parameter. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index you which to retrieve.

Returns a color gradient assigned with the parameter with the specified name. If the parameter has a constant value bound instead of a gradient then this method returns an empty gradient. Use getBoundParamType() to determine the type of the parameter. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index you which to retrieve.
Returns a 2D vector assigned with the parameter with the specified name. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index you which to retrieve.
Returns a 3D vector assigned with the parameter with the specified name. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index you which to retrieve.
Returns a 4D vector assigned with the parameter with the specified name. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index you which to retrieve.
Returns a 3x3 matrix assigned with the parameter with the specified name. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index you which to retrieve.
Returns a 4x4 matrix assigned with the parameter with the specified name. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index you which to retrieve.
Checks does the data parameter with the specified name currently contains animated data. This could be an animation curve or a color gradient.

ctor.doc: Creates a new empty material.
Creates a new material with the specified shader.

property.name: Shader
property.doc: Sets a shader that will be used by the material. Material will be initialized using all compatible variations from the shader. Shader must be set before doing any other operations with the material.

property.name: Variation
property.doc: Set of parameters that determine which subset of variations in the assigned shader should be used. Only the variations that have the provided parameters with the provided values will match. This will control which variation is considered the default variation and which subset of variations are searched during a call to FindVariation().

enum.doc: Available vertex layouts that specify what data is provided per-vertex in a mesh. Combinations other than those provided are allowed.

class.doc: Class containing meta-information describing a resource.

struct.doc: Represents a single potential value of a shader variation parameter and optionally its name.

field.name: Name
field.doc: Optional human-readable name describing what this particular value represents.

field.name: Value
field.doc: Integer value of the parameter.

struct.doc: Represents a single shader variation parameter and a set of all possible values.

field.name: Name
field.doc: Optional human-readable name describing the variation parameter.

field.name: Identifier
field.doc: BSL identifier for the parameter.

field.name: IsInternal
field.doc: True if the parameter is for internal use by the renderer, and false if its intended to be set by the user.

field.name: Values
field.doc: A list of potential values this parameter can take on.

class.doc: Allows you to change and retrieve the active renderer. Active renderer will be used for rendering all objects in the following frame.

method.doc: Queues GPU command capture of the next frame, if a frame capture is set up.

struct.doc: Identifier for a virtual axis. Primary purpose of this class is to avoid expensive string compare (axis names), and instead use a unique axis identifier for compare. Generally you want to create one of these using the axis name, and then store it for later use.

enum.doc: Types of builtin textures that are always available.

class.doc: Specialization of a GUIScrollBar for vertical scrolling, with the ability to resize the scroll bar.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: styleClass
param.doc: Style class that will be used for determining GUI element visuals from the current style sheet. If no class is provided, default style is determined based on GUI element type.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

enum.doc: Types of builtin shaders that are always available.

enumentry.doc: Special shader used for rendering decals that project onto other geometry.
Special shader used for rendering particles with lighting using the deferred rendering pipeline (no support for transparency).
Physically based shader used for opaque 3D geometry.
Physically based shader used for transparent 3D geometry.
Special shader used for rendering particles without any lighting, with support for transparency.
Special shader used for rendering particles with lighting using the forward rendering pipeline (supports transparency).

class.doc: Information about a single or multiple dragged resources, represented as paths.

enum.doc: Contains all possible input buttons, including keyboard scan codes, mouse buttons and gamepad buttons.

struct.doc: Information describing a ParticleEmitterBoxShape.

field.name: Type
field.doc: Determines from which portion of the box should particles be emitted from.

field.name: Extents
field.doc: Extends of the box.

struct.doc: Contains data about a button input event.

field.name: ButtonCode
field.doc: Button code this event is referring to.

field.name: Timestamp
field.doc: Timestamp in ticks when the event happened.

field.name: DeviceIndex
field.doc: Index of the device that the event originated from.

field.name: IsUsed
field.doc: This will be set to true if some previous event receiver has marked the event as used.

enum.doc: Pointer buttons. Generally these correspond to mouse buttons, but may be used in some form for touch input as well.

struct.doc: Event that gets sent out when user interacts with the screen in some way, usually by moving the mouse cursor or using touch input.

field.name: ScreenPos
field.doc: Screen position where the input event occurred.

field.name: Delta
field.doc: Change in movement since last sent event.

field.name: Button
field.doc: Button that triggered the pointer event. Might be irrelevant depending on event type. (for example move events don't correspond to a button.

field.name: Type
field.doc: Type of the pointer event.

field.name: Shift
field.doc: Is shift button on the keyboard being held down.

field.name: Control
field.doc: Is control button on the keyboard being held down.

field.name: Alt
field.doc: Is alt button on the keyboard being held down.

field.name: MouseWheelScrollAmount
field.doc: If mouse wheel is being scrolled, what is the amount. Only relevant for move events.

field.name: IsUsed
field.doc: This will be set to true if some previous event receiver has marked the event as used.

class.doc: Base class for layout GUI element. Layout element positions and sizes any child elements according to element styles and layout options.

method.doc: Adds a new element to the layout after all existing elements.
Removes the specified element from the layout.
Removes a child element at the specified index.
Inserts a GUI element before the element at the specified index.
Returns a child element at the specified index, or null if the index is not valid.
Removes all child elements and destroys them.

property.name: ChildCount
property.doc: Returns the number of children in the layout.

property.name: EnableCulling
property.doc: Enables/disables culling of child elements. If culling is enabled all child elements that are fully outside of the parent visible bounds will be marked as culled. Culled elements will never have their contents or mesh updated, their absolute coordinate will not be updated and they wont be drawn This is useful for layouts with a large amount of children, but comes with an overhead so it is disabled by default. Note this has no impact on layout update, which may still be expensive with many elements.

enum.doc: Common input axis types.

enumentry.doc: Gamepad left trigger. Provides normalized ([-1, 1] range) absolute position.
Mouse axis X. Provides unnormalized relative movement.
Mouse axis Y. Provides unnormalized relative movement.
Mouse wheel/scroll axis. Provides unnormalized relative movement.
Gamepad right stick Y. Provides normalized ([-1, 1] range) absolute position.
Gamepad left stick X. Provides normalized ([-1, 1] range) absolute position.
Gamepad left stick Y. Provides normalized ([-1, 1] range) absolute position.
Gamepad right stick X. Provides normalized ([-1, 1] range) absolute position.
Gamepad right trigger. Provides normalized ([-1, 1] range) absolute position.

enum.doc: Modifiers used with along with keyboard buttons.

class.doc: Base type that should be inherited to provide specific data relevant to a drag and drop operation.

enum.doc: Controls what type of collision mesh should be imported during mesh import.

enumentry.doc: No collision mesh will be imported.
A convex hull will be generated from the source mesh.
Normal triangle mesh will be imported.

class.doc: Information about a single or multiple dragged scene objects.

Handles GUI drag and drop operations. When active GUI elements will be notified of any drag events and will be able to retrieve dragged data. When cursor is released the data will be dropped and underlying GUI elements will be notified the data is dropped. The dropped data is also available for a single frame via GetDropData() method.

method.doc: Starts a drag operation with the specified data. This means GUI elements will start receiving drag and drop related events and they may choose to handle them.

param.name: data
param.doc: Some operation specific data that is just passed through to however needs it.

property.name: IsDragInProgress
property.doc: Returns true if drag is currently in progress.

property.name: IsDropInProgress
property.doc: Returns true if a drop operation happened this frame.

property.name: DragData
property.doc: Gets drag specific data specified when the drag started. Only valid if drag is in progress.

property.name: DropData
property.doc: Gets drag specific data specified when the drag started. Only valid if drop is in progress. This is only valid for a single frame when a drop happens.

struct.doc: Location in 2D space in physical pixels. Physical pixels represent actual pixels as displayed on the output monitor.

Location in 2D space in logical pixels. Logical pixels are defined at 1/96th of one logical inch. Logical pixels are transformed into physical pixels by scaling it by the display's DPI scale. If your display is set to 96 DPI, then one logical pixel equals one physical pixel.
Contains a number value and an associated unit. Used primarily to prevent implicit conversion between numbers of different units.
Contains a number value and an associated unit. Used primarily to prevent implicit conversion between numbers of different units.
Contains a number value and an associated unit. Used primarily to prevent implicit conversion between numbers of different units.
Contains a number value and an associated unit. Used primarily to prevent implicit conversion between numbers of different units.
Controls GUI element layout options, possibly by overriding the default options specified in GUI element style. These options control GUI element placement and size in a GUI layout.

enum.doc: Contains options that change GUIElement behaviour.

enumentry.doc: Enable this option if you want pointer events to pass through this element by default. This will allow elements underneath this element to receive pointer events.
Enable this option if the element accepts keyboard/gamepad input focus. This will allow the element to be navigated to using keys/buttons.
Pointer events on the GUI element will be ignored.

class.doc: List box GUI element which when active opens a drop down selection with provided elements.

method.doc: Changes the list box elements.
Makes the element with the specified index selected.
Deselect element the element with the specified index. Only relevant for multi-select list boxes.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: contents
param.doc: Structure describing the contents of the GUI element to create.

param.name: styleClass
param.doc: Style class that will be used for determining GUI element visuals from the current style sheet. If no class is provided, default style is determined based on GUI element type.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

property.name: IsMultiselect
property.doc: Checks whether the listbox supports multiple selected elements at once.

property.name: SelectedElementIndex
property.doc: Returns the index of the currently selected element. If the list box allows multi-select, returns the index of the first selected element, or ~0u if none is selected.

property.name: ElementStates
property.doc: Sets states for all list box elements. Only valid for multi-select list boxes. Number of states must match number of list box elements.

event.doc: Triggered whenever user selects or deselects an element in the list box. Returned index maps to the element in the elements array that the list box was initialized with.

enum.doc: Possible positions used for positioning content image within a GUI element.
Type of GUI element states.

enumentry.doc: Same as Active, if the element is also in the "on" state.
State when the element has input focus and pointer is not hovering over the element.
Normal state when element is not being interacted with.
State when pointer is hovering over the element.
State when element is being clicked.
State when the element has input focus and pointer is hovering over the element.
Same as Normal, if the element is also in the "on" state.
Same as Hover, if the element is also in the "on" state.
Same as Focused, if the element is also in the "on" state.
Same as FocusedHover, if the element is also in the "on" state.

struct.doc: Structure describing contents of a GUIScrollArea element.
Holds data used for displaying content in a GUIElement. Content can consist of a string, image, a tooltip or none of those.

ctor.doc: Constructs an empty content.
Constructs content with just a string.
Constructs content with a string and a tooltip.
Constructs content with just an image.
Constructs content with an image and a tooltip.
Constructs content with a string and an image.
Constructs content with a string, an image and a tooltip.

class.doc: Base class for a clickable GUI button element.

method.doc: Change content displayed by the button.

event.doc: Triggered when button is clicked.
Triggered when pointer hovers over the button.
Triggered when pointer that was previously hovering leaves the button.
Triggered when button is clicked twice in rapid succession.

class.doc: Particle emitter shape that emits particles from a hemisphere. Particles can be emitted from the hemisphere surface, the entire volume or a proportion of the volume depending on the thickness parameter. All particles will have normals pointing outwards in a spherical direction.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle emitter sphere shape.
Creates a new particle emitter sphere shape.

property.name: Settings
property.doc: Options describing the shape.

property.name: ScrollHandlePosition
property.doc: Position of the scroll handle in percent (ranging [0, 1]).

property.name: ScrollHandleSize
property.doc: Size of the scroll handle in percent (ranging [0, 1]) of the total scroll bar area.

event.doc: Triggered whenever the scrollbar handle is moved or resized. Values provided are the handle position and size in percent (ranging [0, 1]).

struct.doc: Identifier for a virtual button. Primary purpose of this class is to avoid expensive string compare, and instead use a unique button identifier for compare. Generally you want to create one of these using the button name, and then store it for later use.

class.doc: Contains virtual <-> physical key mappings.

method.doc: Registers a new virtual button.

param.name: name
param.doc: Unique name used to access the virtual button.

param.name: buttonCode
param.doc: Physical button the virtual button is triggered by.

param.name: modifiers
param.doc: Modifiers required to be pressed with the physical button to trigger the virtual button.

param.name: repeatable
param.doc: If true, the virtual button events will be sent continually while the physical button is being held.

method.doc: Unregisters a virtual button with the specified name. Events will no longer be generated for that button.
Registers a new virtual axis.

param.name: name
param.doc: Unique name used to access the axis.

param.name: createInformation
param.doc: Descriptor structure containing virtual axis creation parameters.

method.doc: Unregisters a virtual axis with the specified name. You will no longer be able to retrieve valid values for that axis.

property.name: RepeatInterval
property.doc: Repeat interval for held virtual buttons. Buttons will be continously triggered in interval increments as long as they button is being held.

class.doc: Handles virtual input that allows you to receive virtual input events that hide the actual physical input, allowing you to easily change the input keys while being transparent to the external code.

method.doc: Creates a new virtual button associated with the name, or returns an existing button if it exists.
Creates a new virtual axis associated with the name, or returns an existing axis if it exists.
Check is the virtual button just getting pressed. This state is only active for one frame.

param.name: button
param.doc: Virtual button identifier.

param.name: deviceIndex
param.doc: Optional device index in case multiple input devices are available.

method.doc: Check is the virtual button just getting released. This state is only active for one frame.

param.name: button
param.doc: Virtual button identifier.

param.name: deviceIndex
param.doc: Optional device index in case multiple input devices are available.

method.doc: Check is the virtual button is being held. This state is active as long as the button is being held down, possibly for multiple frames.

param.name: button
param.doc: Virtual button identifier.

param.name: deviceIndex
param.doc: Optional device index in case multiple input devices are available.

method.doc: Returns normalized value for the specified input axis. Returned value will usually be in [-1.0, 1.0] range, but can be outside the range for devices with unbound axes (for example mouse).

param.name: axis
param.doc: Virtual axis identifier.

param.name: deviceIndex
param.doc: Optional device index in case multiple input devices are available.

property.name: Configuration
property.doc: Input configuration that determines how physical keys map to virtual buttons.

event.doc: Triggered when a virtual button is pressed.
Triggered when a virtual button is released.
Triggered every frame when a virtual button is being held down.

class.doc: Settings that control rendering for a specific camera (view).

property.name: DepthOfField
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing the gaussian depth of field effect.

property.name: ChromaticAberration
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing the chromatic aberration effect.

property.name: EnableAutoExposure
property.doc: Determines should automatic exposure be applied to the HDR image. When turned on the average scene brightness will be calculated and used to automatically expose the image to the optimal range. Use the parameters provided by autoExposure to customize the automatic exposure effect. You may also use exposureScale to manually adjust the automatic exposure. When automatic exposure is turned off you can use exposureScale to manually set the exposure.

property.name: AutoExposure
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing automatic scene exposure.

property.name: EnableTonemapping
property.doc: Determines should the image be tonemapped. Tonemapping converts an HDR image into LDR image by applying a filmic curve to the image, simulating the effect of film cameras. Filmic curve improves image quality by tapering off lows and highs, preventing under- and over-exposure. This is useful if an image contains both very dark and very bright areas, in which case the global exposure parameter would leave some areas either over- or under-exposed. Use #tonemapping to customize how tonemapping performed. If this is disabled, then color grading and white balancing will not be enabled either. Only relevant for HDR images.

property.name: Tonemapping
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing tonemapping.

property.name: WhiteBalance
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing white balancing. White balancing converts a scene illuminated by a light of the specified temperature into a scene illuminated by a standard D65 illuminant (average midday light) in order to simulate the effects of chromatic adaptation of the human visual system.

property.name: ColorGrading
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing color grading.

property.name: AmbientOcclusion
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing screen space ambient occlusion.

property.name: ScreenSpaceReflections
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing screen space reflections.

property.name: Bloom
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing the bloom effect.

property.name: ScreenSpaceLensFlare
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing the screen space lens flare effect.

property.name: FilmGrain
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing the film grain effect.

property.name: MotionBlur
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing the motion blur effect.

property.name: TemporalAa
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing the temporal anti-aliasing effect.

property.name: EnableFxaa
property.doc: Enables the fast approximate anti-aliasing effect.

property.name: ExposureScale
property.doc: Log2 value to scale the eye adaptation by (for example 2^0 = 1). Smaller values yield darker image, while larger yield brighter image. Allows you to customize exposure manually, applied on top of eye adaptation exposure (if enabled). In range [-8, 8].

property.name: Gamma
property.doc: Gamma value to adjust the image for. Larger values result in a brighter image. When tonemapping is turned on the best gamma curve for the output device is chosen automatically and this value can by used to merely tweak that curve. If tonemapping is turned off this is the exact value of the gamma curve that will be applied.

property.name: EnableHdr
property.doc: High dynamic range allows light intensity to be more correctly recorded when rendering by allowing for a larger range of values. The stored light is then converted into visible color range using exposure and a tone mapping operator.

property.name: EnableLighting
property.doc: Determines if scene objects will be lit by lights. If disabled everything will be rendered using their albedo texture with no lighting applied.

property.name: EnableShadows
property.doc: Determines if shadows cast by lights should be rendered. Only relevant if lighting is turned on.

property.name: EnableVelocityBuffer
property.doc: Determines if the G-Buffer should contain per-pixel velocity information. This can be useful if you are rendering an effect that requires this information. Note that effects such as motion blur or temporal anti-aliasing might force the velocity buffer to be enabled regardless of this setting.

property.name: ShadowSettings
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing shadow rendering.

property.name: EnableIndirectLighting
property.doc: Determines if indirect lighting (e.g. from light probes or the sky) is rendered.

property.name: OverlayOnly
property.doc: Signals the renderer to only render overlays (like GUI), and not scene objects. Such rendering doesn't require depth buffer or multi-sampled render targets and will not render any scene objects. This can improve performance and memory usage for overlay-only views.

property.name: EnableSkybox
property.doc: If enabled the camera will use the skybox for rendering the background. A skybox has to be present in the scene. When disabled the camera will use the clear color for rendering the background.

property.name: CullDistance
property.doc: The absolute base cull-distance for objects rendered through this camera in world units. Objects will use this distance and apply their own factor to it to determine whether they should be visible.

struct.doc: Structure describing contents of a GUIInputBox element.

field.name: AllowMultiline
field.doc: If true, allows multiline input.

class.doc: Input box is a GUI element that accepts Unicode textual input. It can be single or multi-line and handles various types of text manipulation.

field.name: IsTransparent
field.doc: Determines should the texture be rendered with transparency active.

enum.doc: Determines when and if to display a scroll bar on the scroll area.
Determines how are elements positioned in a scroll area.

enumentry.doc: GUI elements will be placed below/above each other (e.g. as in GUILayoutY). This is the standard scroll area.
GUI elements will be placed left/right to each other (e.g. as in GUILayoutX).
GUI elements must be explicitly positioned (e.g. as in GUIPanel).

class.doc: A vertical slider with a draggable handle.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

enum.doc: Flags that control how does a slider handle behave.

enumentry.doc: Determines should the slider handle provide additional side-handles that allow it to be resized.
Slider handle will move horizontally. Cannot be used with the Vertical option.
Slider handle will move vertically. Cannot be used with the Horizontal option.
If enabled, clicking on a specific slider position will cause the handle to jump to that position. If false the handle will only slightly move in that direction.

struct.doc: Structure describing contents of a GUISliderHandle element.

field.name: Flags
field.doc: Flags that control how does the slider handle behave.

class.doc: Object that allows you to group multiple GUI toggle buttons. Only one button among the grouped ones can be active.

ctor.doc: Creates a toggle group that you may provide to GUIToggleable upon construction. Toggles sharing the same group will only have a single element active at a time.

param.name: allowAllOff
param.doc: If true all of the toggle buttons can be turned off, if false one will always be turned on.

class.doc: GUI element representing a toggleable button.

property.name: IsToggled
property.doc: Checks or unchecks the toggle.

event.doc: Triggered whenever the button is toggled on or off.

class.doc: Helper class that performs various operations related to GUI.

method.doc: Calculates optimal content size for the provided text using the provided font and size. Size is calculated without word wrap.

param.name: text
param.doc: Text to calculate the size for.

param.name: font
param.doc: Font to use for rendering the text.

param.name: fontSize
param.doc: Size of individual characters in the font, in points.

returns.doc: Width/height required to display the text, in pixels.

class.doc: Used for string localization. Stores strings and their translations in various languages.

method.doc: Checks does the string table contain the provided identifier.

param.name: identifier
param.doc: Identifier to look for.

returns.doc: True if the identifier exists in the table, false otherwise.

method.doc: Adds or modifies string translation for the specified language.
Returns a string translation for the specified language. Returns the identifier itself if one doesn't exist.
Removes the string described by identifier, from all languages.

ctor.doc: Creates a new empty string table resource.

property.name: NumStrings
property.doc: Returns a total number of strings in the table.

property.name: Identifiers
property.doc: Returns all identifiers that the string table contains localized strings for.
