struct.doc: Descriptor that describes a simple sprite sheet animation. The parent area is split into a grid of x , each representing one frame of the animation. Every frame is of equal size. Frames are sequentially evaluated starting from the top-most row, iterating over all columns in a row and then moving to next row, up to frames. frames are evaluated every second, allowing you to control animation speed.

field.name: RowCount
field.doc: Number of rows to divide the parent area in. Determines height of the individual frame (depends on parent area size).

field.name: ColumnCount
field.doc: Number of columns to divide the parent area in. Determines column of the individual frame (depends on parent area size).

field.name: FrameCount
field.doc: Number of frames in the animation. Must be less or equal than * .

field.name: FramesPerSecond
field.doc: How many frames to evaluate each second. Determines the animation speed.

struct.doc: Information about a SpriteImage.

field.name: AnimationPlayback
field.doc: Determines if animation is enabled and how should it play.

field.name: Animation
field.doc: Describes the sprite sheet grid used for animation, if animation is used.

struct.doc: Descriptor structure used for initialization of a SpriteImage.

field.name: AnimationPlayback
field.doc: Determines if animation is enabled and how should it play.

field.name: Animation
field.doc: Describes the sprite sheet grid used for animation, if animation is used.

struct.doc: Descriptor structure used for initialization of a SpriteGlyph.

field.name: Font
field.doc: Font from which to render the glyph from.

field.name: Glyph
field.doc: Unicode code for the glyph to render.

field.name: DefaultSize
field.doc: Size of the unscaled glyph in points. Actual rendered size might be different depending on DPI scale or other scale factors.

field.name: AnimationPlayback
field.doc: Determines if animation is enabled and how should it play.

field.name: Animation
field.doc: Describes the sprite sheet grid used for animation, if animation is used.

class.doc: Implementation of SpriteImage that renders a single glyph from a Font.

ctor.doc: Creates a new sprite glyph.
Creates a new sprite glyph.

enum.doc: Determines how is a font rendered into the bitmap texture.

struct.doc: Descriptor structure used for initialization of a SpriteTexture.

field.name: AtlasTexture
field.doc: Texture used as the atlas.

field.name: UVRange
field.doc: Range in the atlas texture that the image is to be read from, in [0, 1] range.

field.name: AnimationPlayback
field.doc: Determines if animation is enabled and how should it play.

field.name: Animation
field.doc: Describes the sprite sheet grid used for animation, if animation is used.

class.doc: Implementation of SpriteImage that uses a user-provided texture as the atlas texture.

ctor.doc: Creates a new sprite texture that references the entire area of the provided texture.
Creates a new sprite texture that references a sub-area of the provided texture.

property.name: Texture
property.doc: Retrieves the atlas texture where the image is stored.

property.name: UVRange
property.doc: Determines the UV range that the image is referencing.

enum.doc: Determines how to scale path canvas when rasterizing for a particular size.

enumentry.doc: Canvas will stretch non-uniformly in both dimensions in order to cover the raster area fully.
Canvas will scale uniformly until one dimension is aligned with the raster area. Remaining dimension might have empty space, and canvas will be placed in the center of the raster dimension.
Canvas will scale uniformly until both dimensions are larger or aligned with the raster area. Remaining dimension might be cropped.
Do not perform any scaling. Canvas size is ignored and path coordinates are mapped 1:1 to raster coordinates.

class.doc: Represents a vector path containing curves and geometric shapes that can be rasterized to any dimension.

struct.doc: Descriptor structure used for initialization of a SpriteVectorPath.

field.name: VectorPath
field.doc: Vector path to render on the sprite.

field.name: DefaultSize
field.doc: Size of the unscaled rasterized path, in pixels. Actual rendered size might be different depending on DPI scale or other scale factors.

field.name: ScalingMode
field.doc: How to scale the path canvas onto the rasterized destination.

field.name: AnimationPlayback
field.doc: Determines if animation is enabled and how should it play.

field.name: Animation
field.doc: Describes the sprite sheet grid used for animation, if animation is used.

struct.doc: Contains a resource that was imported from a file that contains multiple resources (for example an animation from an FBX file).

field.name: Name
field.doc: Unique name of the sub-resource.

field.name: Value
field.doc: Contents of the sub-resource.

class.doc: Module responsible for importing various asset types and converting them to types usable by the engine.

method.doc: Imports a resource at the specified location, and returns the loaded data. If file contains more than one resource only the primary resource is imported (for example an FBX a mesh would be imported, but animations ignored).

param.name: inputFilePath
param.doc: Pathname of the input file.

param.name: importOptions
param.doc: (optional) Options for controlling the import. Caller must ensure import options actually match the type of the importer used for the file type.

param.name: UUID
param.doc: Specific UUID to assign to the resource. If not specified a randomly generated UUID will be assigned.

returns.doc: Imported resource.

method.doc: Same as import(), except it imports a resource without blocking the main thread. The resulting resource will be placed in the returned AsyncOp object when the import ends.

Imports a resource at the specified location, and returns the loaded data. This method returns all imported resources, which is relevant for files that can contain multiple resources (for example an FBX which may contain both a mesh and animations).

param.name: inputFilePath
param.doc: Pathname of the input file.

param.name: importOptions
param.doc: (optional) Options for controlling the import. Caller must ensure import options actually match the type of the importer used for the file type.

returns.doc: A list of all imported resources. The primary resource is always the first returned resource.

method.doc: Same as importAll(), except it imports a resource without blocking the main thread. The returned AsyncOp will contain a list of the imported resources, after the import ends.
Checks if we can import a file with the specified extension.

param.name: extension
param.doc: The extension without the leading dot.

method.doc: Imports a resource at the specified location, and returns the loaded data. If file contains more than one resource only the primary resource is imported (for example an FBX a mesh would be imported, but animations ignored).

param.name: inputFilePath
param.doc: Pathname of the input file.

param.name: importOptions
param.doc: (optional) Options for controlling the import. Caller must ensure import options actually match the type of the importer used for the file type.

returns.doc: Imported resource.

method.doc: Imports a resource at the specified location, and returns the loaded data. If file contains more than one resource only the primary resource is imported (for example an FBX a mesh would be imported, but animations ignored).

param.name: inputFilePath
param.doc: Pathname of the input file.

returns.doc: Imported resource.

method.doc: Same as import(), except it imports a resource without blocking the main thread. The resulting resource will be placed in the returned AsyncOp object when the import ends.
Same as import(), except it imports a resource without blocking the main thread. The resulting resource will be placed in the returned AsyncOp object when the import ends.

class.doc: Information about how to split an AnimationClip into multiple separate clips.
Changes the size of the particles over the particle lifetime.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle size evolver.
Creates a new particle size evolver.

property.name: Settings
property.doc: Options describing the evolver.

enum.doc: Flags that can be set per-resource package meta-data.

enumentry.doc: Resource entry represents a folder and has no associated resource data.

class.doc: Contains import options you may use to control how is a mesh imported from some external format into engine format.

ctor.doc: Creates a new import options object that allows you to customize how are meshes imported.

property.name: CpuCached
property.doc: Determines whether the texture data is also stored in CPU memory.

property.name: ImportNormals
property.doc: Determines should mesh normals be imported if available.

property.name: ImportTangents
property.doc: Determines should mesh tangents and bitangents be imported if available.

property.name: ImportBlendShapes
property.doc: Determines should mesh blend shapes be imported if available.

property.name: ImportSkin
property.doc: Determines should mesh skin data like bone weights, indices and bind poses be imported if available.

property.name: ImportAnimation
property.doc: Determines should animation clips be imported if available.

property.name: ReduceKeyFrames
property.doc: Enables or disables keyframe reduction. Keyframe reduction will reduce the number of key-frames in an animation clip by removing identical keyframes, and therefore reducing the size of the clip.

property.name: ImportRootMotion
property.doc: Enables or disables import of root motion curves. When enabled, any animation curves in imported animations affecting the root bone will be available through a set of separate curves in AnimationClip, and they won't be evaluated through normal animation process. Instead it is expected that the user evaluates the curves manually and applies them as required.

property.name: ImportScale
property.doc: Uniformly scales the imported mesh by the specified value.

property.name: CollisionMeshType
property.doc: Determines what type (if any) of collision mesh should be imported. If enabled the collision mesh will be available as a sub-resource returned by the importer (along with the normal mesh).

property.name: AnimationSplits
property.doc: Animation split infos that determine how will the source animation clip be split. If no splits are present the data will be imported as one clip, but if splits are present the data will be split according to the split infos. Split infos only affect the primary animation clip, other clips will not be split.

property.name: AnimationEvents
property.doc: Set of events that will be added to the animation clip, if animation import is enabled.

class.doc: Contains import options you may use to control how is a texture imported.

ctor.doc: Creates a new import options object that allows you to customize how are textures imported.

property.name: Format
property.doc: Pixel format to import as.

property.name: GenerateMips
property.doc: Enables or disables mipmap generation for the texture.

property.name: MaxMip
property.doc: Maximum mip level to generate when generating mipmaps. If 0 then maximum amount of mip levels will be generated.

property.name: CpuCached
property.doc: Determines whether the texture data is also stored in main memory, available for fast CPU access.

property.name: SRgb
property.doc: Determines whether the texture data should be treated as if its in sRGB (gamma) space. Such texture will be converted by hardware to linear space before use on the GPU.

property.name: Cubemap
property.doc: Determines should the texture be imported as a cubemap. See CubemapSourceType to choose how will the source texture be converted to a cubemap.

property.name: CubemapSourceType
property.doc: Determines how should the source texture be interpreted when generating a cubemap. Only relevant when is set to true.

enum.doc: A set of all languages that localized strings can be translated to. Loosely based on ISO 639-1 two letter language codes.

class.doc: Manages string tables used for localizing text. Allows you to add and remove different tables and change the active language.

method.doc: Returns the string table with the specified id. If the table doesn't exist new one is created.

param.name: id
param.doc: Identifier of the string table.

returns.doc: String table with the specified identifier.

method.doc: Removes the string table with the specified id.

param.name: id
param.doc: Identifier of the string table.

method.doc: Registers a new string table or replaces an old one at the specified id.

param.name: id
param.doc: Identifier of the string table.

param.name: table
param.doc: New string table to assign to the specified identifier.

property.name: ActiveLanguage
property.doc: Determines the currently active language. Any newly created strings will use this value.

enum.doc: Types of emission modes.

enumentry.doc: Position will be picked randomly on a shape.
Similar to Loop, except the order will be reversed when one loop iteration finishes.
Positions will loop around the shape in a predictable fashion.
All particles spawned on the shape at some instant (usually a frame) will be spread around the shape equally.

class.doc: Represents a physics mesh that can be used with a MeshCollider. Physics mesh can be a generic triangle mesh or a convex mesh. Convex meshes are limited to 255 faces.

property.name: Type
property.doc: Returns the type of the physics mesh.

class.doc: Base class from all emitter shapes. Emitter shapes determine the position and direction of newly created particles.

enum.doc: Determines the emission type for the cone particle emitter shape.

enumentry.doc: Emit particles only from the cone base.
Emit particles from the entire cone volume.

class.doc: Particle emitter shape that emits particles from a cone. Particles can be created on cone base or volume, while controlling the radial arc of the emitted portion of the volume, as well as thickness of the cone emission volume. All particles will have random normals within the distribution of the cone.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle emitter cone shape.
Creates a new particle emitter cone shape.

property.name: Settings
property.doc: Options describing the shape.

class.doc: Particle emitter shape that emits particles from a sphere. Particles can be emitted from sphere surface, the entire volume or a proportion of the volume depending on the thickness parameter. All particles will have normals pointing outwards in a spherical direction.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle emitter sphere shape.
Creates a new particle emitter sphere shape.

property.name: Settings
property.doc: Options describing the shape.

class.doc: Particle emitter shape that emits particles from an axis aligned box. Particles can be emitted from box volume, surface or edges. All particles have their normals set to positive Z direction.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle emitter box shape.
Creates a new particle emitter box shape.

property.name: Settings
property.doc: Options describing the shape.

struct.doc: Information describing a ParticleEmitterLineShape.

field.name: Length
field.doc: Length of the line.

field.name: Mode
field.doc: Determines how will particle positions on the shape be generated.

class.doc: Particle emitter shape that emits particles from a line segment.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle emitter edge shape.
Creates a new particle emitter edge shape.

property.name: Settings
property.doc: Options describing the shape.

struct.doc: Information describing a ParticleEmitterStaticMeshShape.

field.name: Type
field.doc: Determines from which portion of the mesh are the particles emitted from.

field.name: Sequential
field.doc: When enabled the particles will be emitted sequentially from mesh vertices in the order they are defined. Only relevant for the Vertex emit mode.

field.name: Mesh
field.doc: Mesh to spawn particles on. Must at least contain per-vertex position data encoded as 3D float vectors. Can optionally contain per-vertex normals encoded as 3D float vectors or as 4-byte unsigned-normalized format.

struct.doc: Information describing a ParticleEmitterRectShape.

field.name: Extents
field.doc: Extents of the rectangle.

class.doc: Particle emitter shape that emits particles from the surface of a rectangle.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle emitter rectangle shape.
Creates a new particle emitter rectangle shape.

property.name: Settings
property.doc: Options describing the shape.

enum.doc: Determines the emission type for the mesh particle emitter shape.

enumentry.doc: Particles will be emitted from mesh vertices.
Particles will be emitted from mesh edges.
Particles will be emitted from mesh triangles.

enum.doc: Flags that are used to further desribe a type of a managed serializable object.

struct.doc: Descriptor structure used for initialization of a Font.

field.name: Name
field.doc: Optional name of the font. Used primarily for easier debugging.

field.name: DPI
field.doc: Determines dots per inch scale that will be used when rendering the characters.

field.name: RenderMode
field.doc: Determines the render mode used for rendering the characters into a bitmap.

struct.doc: Specifies a burst of particles that occurs at a certain time point.

field.name: Time
field.doc: Time at which to trigger the burst, in seconds.

field.name: Count
field.doc: Number of particles to emit when the burst triggers.

field.name: Cycles
field.doc: Determines how many times to trigger the burst. If 0 the burst will trigger infinitely. Use to to control the time between each cycle.

field.name: Interval
field.doc: Controls how much time needs to pass before triggering another burst cycle, in seconds.

class.doc: Handles spawning of new particles using the specified parameters and shape.

ctor.doc: Creates a new emitter.

property.name: Shape
property.doc: Shape over which to emit the particles.

property.name: EmissionRate
property.doc: Determines the number of particles that are emitted every second.

property.name: EmissionBursts
property.doc: Determines discrete intervals to emit particles.

property.name: InitialLifetime
property.doc: Determines the lifetime of particles when they are initially spawned, in seconds.

property.name: InitialSpeed
property.doc: Sets the initial speed of the particles, in meters/second. The speed is applied along the particle's velocity direction, which is determined by the emission shape and potentially other properties.

property.name: InitialSize
property.doc: Determines the size of the particles when initially spawned. The size is applied uniformly in all dimensions. Only used if 3D size is disabled.

property.name: InitialSize3D
property.doc: Determines the size of the particles when initially spawned. Size can be specified for each dimension separately. Only used if 3D size is enabled.

property.name: Use3DSize
property.doc: Determines should the initial particle size be applied uniformly (if disabled), or evaluated separately for each dimension (if enabled).

property.name: InitialRotation
property.doc: Determines the rotation of the particles when initially spawned, in degrees. The rotation is applied around the particle's local Z axis. Only used if 3D rotation is disabled.

property.name: InitialRotation3D
property.doc: Determines the rotation of the particles when initially spawned, in Euler angles. Only used if 3D rotation is enabled.

property.name: Use3DRotation
property.doc: Determines should the initial particle rotation be a single angle applied around a Z axis (if disabled), or a set of Euler angles that allow you to rotate around every axis (if enabled).

property.name: InitialColor
property.doc: Determines the initial color (in RGB channels) and transparency (in A channel) of particles.

property.name: RandomOffset
property.doc: Determines a range of values determining a random offset to apply to particle position after it has been emitted. Offset will be randomly selected in all three axes in range [-value, value].

property.name: FlipU
property.doc: Determines should particle U texture coordinate be randomly flipped, mirroring the image. The value represents a percent of particles that should be flipped, in range [0, 1].

property.name: FlipV
property.doc: Determines should particle V texture coordinate be randomly flipped, mirroring the image. The value represents a percent of particles that should be flipped, in range [0, 1].

class.doc: Provides functionality for particle texture animation. Uses the sprite texture assigned to the particle's material to determine animation properties.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle texture animation evolver.
Creates a new particle texture animation evolver.

property.name: Settings
property.doc: Options describing the evolver.

struct.doc: Structure used for initializing a ParticleOrbit object.

field.name: Center
field.doc: Position of the center around which to orbit. Evaluated over particle system lifetime.

field.name: Velocity
field.doc: Determines the speed of rotation around each axis. The speed is specified in "turns" where 0 = no rotation, 0.5 = 180 degree rotation and 1 = 360 degree rotation. Evaluated over particle lifetime.

field.name: Radial
field.doc: Speed at which to push or pull the particles towards/away from the center. Evaluated over particle lifetime.

field.name: WorldSpace
field.doc: True if the properties provided are in world space, false if in local space.

class.doc: Moves particles so that their sprites orbit their center according to the provided offset and rotation values.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle orbit evolver.
Creates a new particle orbit evolver.

property.name: Settings
property.doc: Options describing the evolver.

struct.doc: Structure used for initializing a ParticleVelocity object.

field.name: Velocity
field.doc: Determines the velocity of the particles evaluated over particle lifetime.

field.name: WorldSpace
field.doc: True if the velocity is provided in world space, false if in local space.

class.doc: Applies linear velocity to the particles.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle velocity evolver.
Creates a new particle velocity evolver.

property.name: Settings
property.doc: Options describing the evolver.

struct.doc: Structure used for initializing a ParticleForce object.

field.name: Force
field.doc: Determines the force of the particles evaluated over particle lifetime.

field.name: WorldSpace
field.doc: True if the force is provided in world space, false if in local space.

struct.doc: Structure used for initializing a ParticleGravity object.

field.name: Scale
field.doc: Scale which to apply to the gravity value retrieved from the physics sub-system.

struct.doc: Structure used for initializing a ParticleColor object.

field.name: Color
field.doc: Determines the color of the particles evaluated over particle lifetime.

struct.doc: Structure used for initializing a ParticleSize object.

field.name: Size
field.doc: Determines the uniform size of the particles evaluated over particle lifetime. Only used if 3D size is disabled.

field.name: Size3D
field.doc: Determines the non-uniform size of the particles evaluated over particle lifetime. Only used if 3D size is enabled.

field.name: Use3DSize
field.doc: Determines should the size be evaluated uniformly for all dimensions, or evaluate each dimension with its own distribution.

class.doc: Rotates the particles over the particle lifetime.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle rotation evolver.
Creates a new particle rotation evolver.

property.name: Settings
property.doc: Options describing the evolver.

enum.doc: Types of collision modes that ParticleCollisions evolver can operate in.

enumentry.doc: Particles will collide with a user-provided set of planes.
Particles will collide with physics colliders in the scene.

class.doc: Particle evolver that allows particles to collide with the world.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle collision evolver.
Creates a new particle collision evolver.

property.name: Planes
property.doc: Determines a set of planes to use when using the Plane collision mode. Planes are expected to be in world space.

property.name: PlaneObjects
property.doc: Determines a set of objects whose transforms to derive the collision planes from. Objects can move in the world and collision planes will be updated automatically. Object's negative Z axis is considered to be plane normal.

property.name: Settings
property.doc: Options describing the evolver.

class.doc: Provides global physics settings, factory methods for physics objects and scene queries.

method.doc: Enables or disables collision between two layers. Each physics object can be assigned a specific layer, and here you can determine which layers can interact with each other.
Checks if two collision layers are allowed to interact.

class.doc: Physical representation of a scene, allowing creation of new physical objects in the scene and queries against those objects. Objects created in different scenes cannot physically interact with eachother.

method.doc: Casts a ray into the scene and returns the closest found hit, if any.

param.name: ray
param.doc: Ray to cast into the scene.

param.name: hit
param.doc: Information recorded about a hit. Only valid if method returns true.

param.name: layer
param.doc: Layers to consider for the query. This allows you to ignore certain groups of objects.

param.name: max
param.doc: Maximum distance at which to perform the query. Hits past this distance will not be detected.

returns.doc: True if something was hit, false otherwise.

method.doc: Adds a new physics region. Certain physics options require you to set up regions in which physics objects are allowed to be in, and objects outside of these regions will not be handled by physics. You do not need to set up these regions by default.
Removes a physics region.
Removes all physics regions.

property.name: Gravity
property.doc: Determines the global gravity value for all objects in the scene.

class.doc: Material that controls how two physical objects interact with each other. Materials of both objects are used during their interaction and their combined values are used.

ctor.doc: Creates a new physics material.

param.name: staticFriction
param.doc: Controls friction when two in-contact objects are not moving lateral to each other (for example how difficult is to get an object moving from a static state while it is in contact other object(s)).

param.name: dynamicFriction
param.doc: Sets dynamic friction of the material. Controls friction when two in-contact objects are moving lateral to each other (for example how quickly does an object slow down when sliding along another object).

param.name: restitution
param.doc: Controls "bounciness" of an object during a collision. Value of 1 means the collision is elastic, and value of 0 means the value is inelastic. Must be in [0, 1] range.

property.name: StaticFriction
property.doc: Controls friction when two in-contact objects are not moving lateral to each other (for example how difficult it is to get an object moving from a static state while it is in contact with other object(s)).

property.name: DynamicFriction
property.doc: Controls friction when two in-contact objects are moving lateral to each other (for example how quickly does an object slow down when sliding along another object).

property.name: Restitution
property.doc: Controls "bounciness" of an object during a collision. Value of 1 means the collision is elastic, and value of 0 means the value is inelastic. Must be in [0, 1] range.

class.doc: Allows you to manipulate the platform cursor in various ways.

method.doc: Moves the cursor to the specified screen position.
Retrieves the cursor position in screen coordinates.
Hides the cursor.
Shows the cursor.
Limit cursor movement to specific area on the screen.
Disables cursor clipping that was set using any of the clipTo* methods.
Sets a cursor icon. Uses one of the built-in cursor types.
Sets a cursor icon. Uses one of the manually registered icons.

param.name: name
param.doc: The name to identify the cursor, one set previously by calling setCursorIcon().

method.doc: Registers a new custom cursor icon you can then set by calling "setCursor".

param.name: name
param.doc: The name to identify the cursor.

param.name: pixelData
param.doc: Cursor image data.

param.name: hotSpot
param.doc: Offset on the cursor image to where the actual input happens (for example tip of the Arrow cursor).

method.doc: Registers a new custom cursor icon you can then set by calling setCursor().

param.name: type
param.doc: One of the built-in cursor types.

param.name: pixelData
param.doc: Cursor image data.

param.name: hotSpot
param.doc: Offset on the cursor image to where the actual input happens (for example tip of the Arrow cursor).

method.doc: Removes a custom cursor icon and releases any data associated with it.
Removes a custom cursor icon and releases any data associated with it. Restores original icon associated with this cursor type.

class.doc: Settings that control automatic exposure (eye adaptation) post-process.

property.name: HistogramLog2Min
property.doc: Determines minimum luminance value in the eye adaptation histogram. The histogram is used for calculating the average brightness of the scene. Any luminance value below this value will not be included in the histogram and ignored in scene brightness calculations. In log2 units (-8 = 1/256). In the range [-16, 0].

property.name: HistogramLog2Max
property.doc: Determines maximum luminance value in the eye adaptation histogram. The histogram is used for calculating the average brightness of the scene. Any luminance value above this value will not be included in the histogram and ignored in scene brightness calculations. In log2 units (4 = 16). In the range [0, 16].

property.name: HistogramPctLow
property.doc: Percentage below which to ignore values in the eye adaptation histogram. The histogram is used for calculating the average brightness of the scene. Total luminance in the histogram will be summed up and multiplied by this value to calculate minimal luminance. Luminance values below the minimal luminance will be ignored and not used in scene brightness calculations. This allows you to remove outliers on the lower end of the histogram (for example a few very dark pixels in an otherwise bright image). In range [0.0f, 1.0f].

property.name: HistogramPctHigh
property.doc: Percentage above which to ignore values in the eye adaptation histogram. The histogram is used for calculating the average brightness of the scene. Total luminance in the histogram will be summed up and multiplied by this value to calculate maximum luminance. Luminance values above the maximum luminance will be ignored and not used in scene brightness calculations. This allows you to remove outliers on the high end of the histogram (for example a few very bright pixels). In range [0.0f, 1.0f].

property.name: MinEyeAdaptation
property.doc: Clamps the minimum eye adaptation scale to this value. This allows you to limit eye adaptation so that exposure is never too high (for example when in a very dark room you probably do not want the exposure to be so high that everything is still visible). In range [0.0f, 10.0f].

property.name: MaxEyeAdaptation
property.doc: Clamps the maximum eye adaptation scale to this value. This allows you to limit eye adaptation so that exposure is never too low (for example when looking at a very bright light source you probably don't want the exposure to be so low that the rest of the scene is all white (overexposed). In range [0.0f, 10.0f].

property.name: EyeAdaptationSpeedUp
property.doc: Determines how quickly does the eye adaptation adjust to larger values. This affects how quickly does the automatic exposure changes when the scene brightness increases. In range [0.01f, 20.0f].

property.name: EyeAdaptationSpeedDown
property.doc: Determines how quickly does the eye adaptation adjust to smaller values. This affects how quickly does the automatic exposure changes when the scene brightness decreases. In range [0.01f, 20.0f].

class.doc: Settings that control tonemap post-process.

property.name: FilmicCurveShoulderStrength
property.doc: Controls the shoulder (upper non-linear) section of the filmic curve used for tonemapping. Mostly affects bright areas of the image and allows you to reduce over-exposure.

property.name: FilmicCurveLinearStrength
property.doc: Controls the linear (middle) section of the filmic curve used for tonemapping. Mostly affects mid-range areas of the image.

property.name: FilmicCurveLinearAngle
property.doc: Controls the linear (middle) section of the filmic curve used for tonemapping. Mostly affects mid-range areas of the image and allows you to control how quickly does the curve climb.

property.name: FilmicCurveToeStrength
property.doc: Controls the toe (lower non-linear) section of the filmic curve used for tonemapping. Mostly affects dark areas of the image and allows you to reduce under-exposure.

property.name: FilmicCurveToeNumerator
property.doc: Controls the toe (lower non-linear) section of the filmic curve. used for tonemapping. Affects low-range.

property.name: FilmicCurveToeDenominator
property.doc: Controls the toe (lower non-linear) section of the filmic curve used for tonemapping. Affects low-range.

property.name: FilmicCurveLinearWhitePoint
property.doc: Controls the white point of the filmic curve used for tonemapping. Affects the entire curve.

class.doc: Settings that control white balance post-process.

property.name: Temperature
property.doc: Temperature used for white balancing, in Kelvins. Moves along the Planckian locus. In range [1500.0f, 15000.0f].

property.name: Tint
property.doc: Additional tint to be applied during white balancing. Can be used to further tweak the white balancing effect by modifying the tint of the light. The tint is chosen on the Planckian locus isothermal, depending on the light temperature specified by #temperature. In range [-1.0f, 1.0f].

class.doc: Settings that control screen space ambient occlusion.

property.name: Enabled
property.doc: Enables or disables the screen space ambient occlusion effect.

property.name: Radius
property.doc: Radius (in world space, in meters) over which occluders are searched for. Smaller radius ensures better sampling precision but can miss occluders. Larger radius ensures far away occluders are considered but can yield lower quality or noise because of low sampling precision. Usually best to keep at around a meter, valid range is roughly [0.05, 5.0].

property.name: Bias
property.doc: Bias used to reduce false occlusion artifacts. Higher values reduce the amount of artifacts but will cause details to be lost in areas where occlusion isn't high. Value is in millimeters. Usually best to keep at a few dozen millimeters, valid range is roughly [0, 200].

property.name: FadeDistance
property.doc: Distance (in view space, in meters) after which AO starts fading out. The fade process will happen over the range as specified by .

property.name: FadeRange
property.doc: Range (in view space, in meters) in which AO fades out from 100% to 0%. AO starts fading out after the distance specified in .

property.name: Intensity
property.doc: Linearly scales the intensity of the AO effect. Values less than 1 make the AO effect less pronounced, and vice versa. Valid range is roughly [0.2, 2].

property.name: Power
property.doc: Controls how quickly does the AO darkening effect increase with higher occlusion percent. This is a non-linear control and will cause the darkening to ramp up exponentially. Valid range is roughly [1, 4], where 1 means no extra darkening will occur.

property.name: Quality
property.doc: Quality level of generated ambient occlusion. In range [0, 4]. Higher levels yield higher quality AO at the cost of performance.

enum.doc: Types of available depth of field effects.

enumentry.doc: Fastest depth of field variant, uses gaussian blur to approximate depth of field on near and far objects, but doesn't use any physically based methods for determining blur amount.

Very expensive depth of field variant that allows you to use a bokeh texture, controlling the shape of the blur (usually intended to mimic real world camera aperature shapes). Blur is varied according to actual object distance and the effect is more physically based than gaussian blur (but not completely). Very expensive in terms of performance.

class.doc: Contains import options you may use to control how is a file containing script source code importer.

ctor.doc: Creates a new import options object that allows you to customize how is script code imported.

property.name: EditorScript
property.doc: Determines whether the script is editor-only or a normal game script.

class.doc: Settings that control the depth-of-field effect.

property.name: BokehShape
property.doc: Texture to use for the bokeh shape. Only relevant when using Bokeh depth of field.

property.name: Enabled
property.doc: Enables or disables the depth of field effect.

property.name: Type
property.doc: Type of depth of field effect to use.

property.name: FocalDistance
property.doc: Distance from the camera at which the focal plane is located in. Objects at this distance will be fully in focus. In world units (meters).

property.name: FocalRange
property.doc: Range within which the objects remain fully in focus. This range is applied relative to the focal distance. This parameter should usually be non-zero when using the Gaussian depth of field effect. When using other types of depth-of-field you can set this to zero for a more physically-based effect, or keep it non-zero for more artistic control. In world units (meters).

property.name: NearTransitionRange
property.doc: Determines the size of the range within which objects transition from focused to fully unfocused, at the near plane. Only relevant for Gaussian and Bokeh depth of field. In world units (meters).

property.name: FarTransitionRange
property.doc: Determines the size of the range within which objects transition from focused to fully unfocused, at the far plane. Only relevant for Gaussian and Bokeh depth of field. In world units (meters).

property.name: NearBlurAmount
property.doc: Determines the amount of blur to apply to fully unfocused objects that are closer to camera than the in-focus zone. Set to zero to disable near-field blur. Only relevant for Gaussian depth of field.

property.name: FarBlurAmount
property.doc: Determines the amount of blur to apply to fully unfocused objects that are farther away from camera than the in-focus zone. Set to zero to disable far-field blur. Only relevant for Gaussian depth of field.

property.name: MaxBokehSize
property.doc: Determines the maximum size of the blur kernel, in percent of view size. Larger values cost more performance. Only relevant when using Bokeh depth of field.

property.name: AdaptiveColorThreshold
property.doc: Determines the maximum color difference between surrounding pixels allowed (as a sum of all channels) before higher fidelity sampling is triggered. Increasing this value can improve performance as less higher fidelity samples will be required, but may decrease quality of the effect. Only relevant when using Bokeh depth of field.

property.name: AdaptiveRadiusThreshold
property.doc: Determines the minimum circle of confusion size before higher fidelity sampling is triggered. Small values trigger high fidelity sampling because they can otherwise produce aliasing, and they are small enough so they don't cost much. Increasing this value can improve performance as less higher fidelity samples will be required, but may decrease quality of the effect. Only relevant when using Bokeh depth of field.

property.name: ApertureSize
property.doc: Camera aperture size in mm. Only relevant when using Bokeh depth of field.

property.name: FocalLength
property.doc: Focal length of the camera's lens (e.g. 75mm). Only relevant when using Bokeh depth of field.

property.name: SensorSize
property.doc: Camera sensor width and height, in mm. Used for controlling the size of the circle of confusion. Only relevant when using Bokeh depth of field.

property.name: BokehOcclusion
property.doc: Set to true if Bokeh flare should use soft depth testing to ensure they don't render on top of foreground geometry. This can help reduce background bleeding into the foreground, which can be especially noticeable if the background is much brighter than the foreground. Use to tweak the effect.

property.name: OcclusionDepthRange
property.doc: Range in world units over which range to fade out Bokeh flare influence. Influence of the flare will be faded out as the depth difference between the flare's origin pixel and the destination pixel grows larger. Only relevant if is turned on.

enum.doc: Determines which parts of the scene will trigger motion blur.

enumentry.doc: Camera movement and rotation will result in full-screen motion blur.
Both the camera movement and object movement will result in motion blur.

Object movement and rotation will result in blurring of the moving object. Can be significantly more expensive than just using camera blur due to the requirement to use a velocity buffer (unless some other effect also requires it, in which case it will be re-used).

enum.doc: Determines the number of samples to take during motion blur filtering.

enumentry.doc: 16 samples per pixel.
12 samples per pixel.
4 samples per pixel.
6 samples per pixel.
8 samples per pixel.

class.doc: Settings that control the motion blur effect.

property.name: Enabled
property.doc: Enables or disables the motion blur effect.

property.name: Domain
property.doc: Determines which parts of the scene will trigger motion blur.

property.name: Filter
property.doc: Type of filter to use when filtering samples contributing to a blurred pixel.

property.name: Quality
property.doc: Determines the number of samples to take during motion blur filtering. Increasing this value will yield higher quality blur at the cost of the performance.

property.name: MaximumRadius
property.doc: Determines the maximum radius over which the blur samples are allowed to be taken, in percent of the screen width (e.g. with 1% radius, on 1920x1028 resolution the maximum radius in pixels will be 1920 * 0.01 = 20px). This clamps the maximum velocity that can affect the blur, as higher velocities require higher radius. Very high values can adversely affect performance as cache accesses become more random.

class.doc: Settings that control the screen space reflections effect. Screen space reflections provide high quality mirror-like reflections at low performance cost. They should be used together with reflection probes as the effects complement each other. As the name implies, the reflections are only limited to geometry drawn on the screen and the system will fall back to refl. probes when screen space data is unavailable. Similarly the system will fall back to refl. probes for rougher (more glossy rather than mirror-like) surfaces. Those surfaces require a higher number of samples to achieve the glossy look, so we instead fall back to refl. probes which are pre-filtered and can be quickly sampled.

property.name: Enabled
property.doc: Enables or disables the SSR effect.

property.name: Quality
property.doc: Quality of the SSR effect. Higher values cast more sample rays, and march those rays are lower increments for better precision. This results in higher quality, as well as a higher performance requirement. Valid range is [0, 4], default is 2.

property.name: Intensity
property.doc: Intensity of the screen space reflections. Valid range is [0, 1]. Default is 1 (100%).

property.name: MaxRoughness
property.doc: Roughness at which screen space reflections start fading out and become replaced with refl. probes. Valid range is [0, 1]. Default is 0.8.

class.doc: Settings that control the chromatic aberration effect.

property.name: FringeTexture
property.doc: Optional texture to apply to generate the channel shift fringe, allowing you to modulate the shifted colors. This texture should be 3x1 size, where the first pixel modules red, second green and third blue channel. If using the complex aberration effect you can use a larger texture, Nx1 size.

property.name: Enabled
property.doc: Enables or disables the effect.

property.name: Type
property.doc: Type of algorithm to use for rendering the effect.

property.name: ShiftAmount
property.doc: Determines the brightness of the lens flare effect. Value of 1 means the lens flare will be displayed at the same intensity as the scene it was derived from. In range [0, 1], default being 0.05.

class.doc: Settings that control the film grain effect. Film grains adds a time-varying noise effect over the entire image.

property.name: Enabled
property.doc: Enables or disables the effect.

property.name: Intensity
property.doc: Controls how intense are the displayed film grains.

property.name: Speed
property.doc: Controls at what speed do the film grains change.

class.doc: Various options that control shadow rendering for a specific view.

property.name: DirectionalShadowDistance
property.doc: Maximum distance that directional light shadows are allowed to render at. Decreasing the distance can yield higher quality shadows nearer to the viewer, as the shadow map resolution isn't being used up on far away portions of the scene. In world units (meters).

property.name: NumCascades
property.doc: Number of cascades to use for directional shadows. Higher number of cascades increases shadow quality as each individual cascade has less area to cover, but can significantly increase performance cost, as well as a minor increase in memory cost. Valid range is roughly [1, 6].

property.name: CascadeDistributionExponent
property.doc: Allows you to control how are directional shadow cascades distributed. Value of 1 means the cascades will be linearly split, each cascade taking up the same amount of space. Value of 2 means each subsequent split will be twice the size of the previous one (meaning cascades closer to the viewer cover a smaller area, and therefore yield higher resolution shadows). Higher values increase the size disparity between near and far cascades at an exponential rate. Valid range is roughly [1, 4].

property.name: ShadowFilteringQuality
property.doc: Determines the number of samples used for percentage closer shadow map filtering. Higher values yield higher quality shadows, at the cost of performance. Valid range is [1, 4].

class.doc: Contains meta-data for a resource stored in a Package.

property.name: ResourceName
property.doc: Returns the name of the resource.

property.name: Path
property.doc: Path to the resource within the package.

property.name: Id
property.doc: Unique ID of the resource.

property.name: TypeId
property.doc: RTTI type ID of the resource contained.

property.name: Dependencies
property.doc: IDs of other resource that this resource depends on.

property.name: CompressionType
property.doc: Type of compression used on the serialized resource data.

property.name: Flags
property.doc: Flags to provide additional information about the resource.

property.name: AdditionalMetaData
property.doc: Optional additional meta-data set explicitly by the user. This can be anything, but should be kept small.

property.name: ResourceMetaData
property.doc: Meta-data that is inherited from the Resource object.

property.name: ResourceType
property.doc: Returns managed type of the resource described by the meta-data.

class.doc: Contains meta-data for the entirety of a Package (not individual resources).

struct.doc: Options that may be used to customize resource load operation.

class.doc: Manager for dealing with all engine resources. It allows you to save new resources and load existing ones.

method.doc: Checks if the resource at the provided path exists.

param.name: resourcePath
param.doc: Path to the resource. This may be a virtual or physical path. e.g.: Virtual path: '/game/textures/path/to/resource' Physical path: 'D:/path/to/package.b3d/path/to/resource'

returns.doc: True if the resource can be located, false otherwise.

method.doc: Checks if the resource with the provided ID exists.

param.name: resourceId
param.doc: ID of the resource.

returns.doc: True if the resource can be located, false otherwise.

method.doc: Releases an internal reference to the resource held by the resources system. This allows the resource to be unloaded when it goes out of scope, if the resource was loaded with option. Alternatively you can also skip manually calling ReleaseInternalReference() and call UnloadAllUnused() which will unload all resources that do not have any external references, but you lose the fine grained control of what will be unloaded.
Finds all resources that aren't being referenced outside of the resources system and unloads them.
Forces unload of all resources, whether they are being used or not.
Checks is the resource with the specified UUID loaded.

param.name: uuid
param.doc: UUID of the resource to check.

param.name: checkInProgress
param.doc: Should this method also check resources that are in progress of being asynchronously loaded.

returns.doc: True if loaded or loading in progress, false otherwise.

method.doc: Returns the loading progress of a resource that's being loaded

param.name: resource
param.doc: Resource whose load progress to check.

returns.doc: Load progress in range [0, 1].

event.doc: Called when the resource has been successfully loaded.
Called when the resource has been destroyed. Provides UUID of the destroyed resource.
Called when the internal resource the handle is pointing to has changed.

class.doc: Contains information about a type of a managed game object or resource.

Prefab is a saveable hierarchy of scene objects. It can be instanced, and instances will maintain a link to the original prefab they were created from, allowing you to update them to latest version if the prefab changes. Prefabs can also be nested within each-other, as long as there are no circular dependencies. Instanced prefabs can also contain per instance modifications that will be preserved even if the prefab they were created from changes.

method.doc: Instantiates a prefab by creating an instance of the prefab's scene object hierarchy. The returned hierarchy will be parented to the provided scene instance root.

param.name: sceneInstance
param.doc: Scene instance into which to instantiate the prefab instance in.

returns.doc: Instantiated clone of the prefab's scene object hierarchy.

ctor.doc: Creates a new prefab from the provided scene object. If the scene object has an existing prefab link it will be broken. After the prefab is created the scene object will be automatically linked to it.

class.doc: Saveable hierarchy of scene objects that can be instantiated into a SceneInstance.

method.doc: Instantiates a scene by creating an instance of the scene object hierarchy.

ctor.doc: Creates a new scene from the provided scene object. The provided scene object and all saveable children will be cloned as part of the scene.

class.doc: Keeps track of all active SceneObject%s and their components. Keeps track of component state and triggers their events. Updates the transforms of objects as SceneObject%s move.

property.name: MainScene
property.doc: In a standalone game this represents the scene that is playing. In editor this represents the primary scene being edited. When creating scene objects and no scene is provided, the object will be created in the main scene. If null main scene is set, new empty main scene will be created internally, as the main scene must always exist.

enum.doc: Flags that mark which portion of a scene-object is modified.

struct.doc: Describes a single character in a font of a specific size.

field.name: CharId
field.doc: Character ID, corresponding to a Unicode key.

field.name: Page
field.doc: Index of the texture the character is located on.

field.name: UvX
field.doc: Texture coordinates of the character in the page texture.

field.name: UvY
field.doc: Texture coordinates of the character in the page texture.

field.name: UvWidth
field.doc: Width/height of the character in texture coordinates.

field.name: UvHeight
field.doc: Width/height of the character in texture coordinates.

field.name: Width
field.doc: Width/height of the character in pixels.

field.name: Height
field.doc: Width/height of the character in pixels.

field.name: XOffset
field.doc: Offset for the visible portion of the character in pixels.

field.name: YOffset
field.doc: Offset for the visible portion of the character in pixels.

field.name: XAdvance
field.doc: Determines how much to advance the pen after writing this character, in pixels.

field.name: YAdvance
field.doc: Determines how much to advance the pen after writing this character, in pixels.

field.name: PointSize
field.doc: Size in points that the character was generated from. May be 0 if glyph was generated using pixel width/height.

field.name: KerningPairs
field.doc: Pairs that determine if certain character pairs should be closer or father together. for example "AV" combination.

enum.doc: Available types of FontBitmapPage.

enumentry.doc: Glyphs in this page can be dynamically allocated at runtime and won't be saved.
Glyphs in this page can be dynamically allocated at runtime and will be saved. Next time they are loaded they will use the Loaded type.
Glyphs in this page can be read, but no new glyphs can be added to the page.

class.doc: Font resource containing data about textual characters and how to render text. Contains one or multiple font bitmaps, each for a specific size.

method.doc: Returns font bitmap information for a specific font size.

param.name: size
param.doc: Size of the font in points.

returns.doc: Bitmap object if it exists, false otherwise.

method.doc: Finds a rendered bitmap closest to the provided size.

param.name: size
param.doc: Size of the bitmap in points.

returns.doc: Nearest available bitmap size.

struct.doc: Represents a range of character code.

class.doc: Import options that allow you to control how is a font imported.

ctor.doc: Creates a new import options object that allows you to customize how are fonts imported.

property.name: FontSizes
property.doc: Determines font sizes that are to be imported. Sizes are in points.

property.name: CharIndexRanges
property.doc: Determines character index ranges to import. Ranges are defined as unicode numbers.

property.name: Dpi
property.doc: Determines dots per inch scale that will be used when rendering the characters.

property.name: RenderMode
property.doc: Determines the render mode used for rendering the characters into a bitmap.

property.name: Bold
property.doc: Determines whether the bold font style should be used when rendering.

property.name: Italic
property.doc: Determines whether the italic font style should be used when rendering.

enum.doc: Contains a list of all icons that can be retrieved through the StockIcons module.
Flags used to further describe a shader parameter.

enumentry.doc: Parameter is for internal use by the renderer and isn't expected to be set by the user.
Parameter should not be displayed in the editor inspector.
Allows the color parameter to be edited using the HDR color picker.

class.doc: Provides easy access to variety of icons for use in the engine.

method.doc: Retrieves a particular stock icon.

param.name: icon
param.doc: Icon to retrieve.

param.name: size
param.doc: Size of the icon in points.

method.doc: Returns the unicode character corresponding to an icon.
Returns the font in which the provided icon is stored in.
Parses an icon name and returns the corresponding enum entry if found.

enum.doc: Types of resources accessible from script code.
Valid reference script types.

class.doc: Contains information about a type of a managed object.

method.doc: Checks if the current type matches the provided type.
Checks does the managed type this object represents still exists.

class.doc: Contains information about a type of a managed primitive (for example int, float, etc.).
Contains information about a type of a managed enum.
Contains information about a type of a reference to a resource.
Contains information about a type of a generic managed object (for example struct or class).
Contains information about a type of a managed Array.
Contains information about a type of a managed List.

struct.doc: Contains information about a style of a serializable field.

field.name: RangeMin
field.doc: Returns the lower bound of the range. Only relevant if

field.name: RangeMax
field.doc: Returns the upper bound of the range. Only relevant if

field.name: StepIncrement
field.doc: Minimum increment the field value can be increment/decremented by. Only relevant if

field.name: DisplayAsSlider
field.doc: If true, number fields will be displayed as sliders instead of regular input boxes.

field.name: CategoryName
field.doc: Name of the category to display in inspector, if the member is part of one.

field.name: Order
field.doc: Determines ordering in inspector relative to other members.

class.doc: Contains data about a single member (field or property) in a managed object (class or struct).

method.doc: Parses style attributes for this members and returns a structure holding all the relevant style information.
Returns a boxed value contained in the member in the specified object instance.

param.name: instance
param.doc: Object instance to access the member on.

returns.doc: A boxed value of the member.

method.doc: Sets a value of the member in the specified object instance.

param.name: instance
param.doc: Object instance to access the member on.

param.name: value
param.doc: Boxed value to set on the member.

property.name: IsSerializable
property.doc: Determines should the member be serialized when serializing the parent object.
